19/11/2025, 18:46 - olympicson: NEW STORY ALERT ‼️ 

❣️LOVE IN THE ELITES’ WORLD❣️
       🌏🌏Her new world 🌎 🌏
.
 Story by: SUC WRITES 
*
 GENRE 
 - Highschool romance 

 TAGS
 -Billoniares, - Love triangle  -Weak female lead - Rivals - Friendship -Repentance - Bully  - Elites -Suspense -Illegitimate -Secrets -Revenge  
*
 Synopsis 
.
Paulina Andrew’s, 18-years old teenager tried to adjust in her new world, after her life takes a toll that she never dreamed of. 
 She lived humbly with her single mother in a small village and was satisfied; they made a living by fishing, and managed to make ends meet. She never imagined what a luxurious life would look like, she only dreamed of living with her mother forever. But her life takes a drastic turn after the sudden disappearance of her mother. 
 A strange knock on the door opened an entire new world for her. She was introduced to a family she never imagined, a father she never knew existed, a conglomerate family she never thought of being a part in, an entire new world. 

  It was difficult for her to adjust in her new home with a father who only listens to his wife,  a stepmother who could do anything to save her political career, and the worse was her half-sister sister, Ava, who is determined to make her life miserable at home  and at school. 

 To spice up her already miserable life, she was enrolled in a new school - a school where students are classified based on hierarchy. There she  entangled with Micheal Russ, the leader of the infamous group known as MASS - an alliance made up of Michael, Austin, Stanley, and Steven: sons of multibillionaires.  Michael was engaged to Ava bond by a merger between the two families; although neither of them wanted the engagement, but their families’ decision was final. When Paulina became close to Michael, Ava became determined to fight for him - a determination that made led to unexpected twists and turns in Paulina’s life. 

*
*
 Short prologue brought out of the story.***
*

 Michael kissed Paulina, and she opened her mouth to kiss back before she pushed him away after realizing what she was doing, and ran off.
 Michael bites his lips in frustration before going after her. 

   “Lina, where are you running off to when we will still meet at school tomorrow?” He shouted, and she stopped on her track to look at him. 
    
   “This isn’t right, Mike.” She muttered, and he walked closer to her. 
     
    “What isn’t right? The kiss, or you running off after a passionate kiss?”
    
    “Everything! You are engaged to Ava!”

    “So?” He muttered, and she looked at him. 

     “Mike…”. She shuddered. 

   “I make the rules, Lina. I go after what I want, and I get it either the easy or hard way. From now henceforth, you are mine. We’re a couple now, so never run off like that again.” He said, and dragged her close to him smashing his lips on hers, and they kissed passionately like their lives depend on it. 
*
  How will Paulina win against her half-sister?
 Will Mike be able to fight for feelings when his future was at stake?
 Can love conquer it all?
  What’s behind Paulina’s mother sudden disappearance?
.
  Let’s find out all and more in this roller coaster 🤭
19/11/2025, 18:46 - olympicson: ❣️LOVE IN THE ELITES’ WORLD❣️
       🌏🌏Her new world 🌎 🌏
     📖📖CHAPTER ONE.1️⃣.

          ❣️❣️ Her new family ❣️❣️
.
 Story by: SUC WRITES 
*
  ❌DO NOT COPY OR REPOST ❌
.
  ‼️‼️Ghosts readers stay away ‼️‼️
*
*
 The weather was dark and cloudy; Paulina checked the  wall clock to confirm the time for the fifth time and realized that it’s still 6 in the evening, and the cloud was already covered in darkness. Anyone could think it’s past midnight, and she panicked gazing outside the window with hopes that her mother doesn’t get drained in the rain. 

  Thunder strikes, followed by heavy rain fall, and she ran to the living room to place a pail under the licking zinc.  She let out a shallow sigh wondering when her mother would be back from the market where she sells fishes. She couldn’t accompany her because she had a long day at school, and an assignment to finish working on so her mother insisted that she return home straight after school to take care of her own business. But now she started regretting her decision to agree with her. 
    
   “I should have gone to her, I would’ve been able to persuade her to leave before the rain pours.” She thought, and started blaming herself. 

  She dialed her mother’s number but it was out of reach, as expected the village never have accurate service. She wandered around the  living room for hours already scared, and started thinking of raising an alarm when she heard a knock on the door. Obviously, it wasn’t her mother so she hurried to open the door wondering if whoever was there have a message to deliver to her from her mother. 

 She opened the door and was stunned to see two dazzling strangers that are obviously not from their village or the neighboring village. 
*
 She offered a glass of water each to them, and the woman looked at the dropping water followed by a smirk. 
Paulina instantly knew that her presence wasn’t a pleasant one. But what could such a sophisticated woman, in company of a man who might be likely her bodyguard want in their humble home if she didn’t knock at the wrong door. 

   “Good evening, madam. I don’t know you, and I doubt if you knew me either. I welcomed you in because I felt it’s impolite to question you in the rain.” She started, and the woman chuckled - which likely means she wasn’t there by mistake, but she had an unfriendly business to discuss. 

    “You are so kind… just like your mother.” The woman said, and Paulina looked at her immediately, wondering how she knew her mother. 

   “How did you know my mother?” She asked, and the woman chuckled softly. 

   “Paulina Andrew’s, right?” The woman asked, and the man gave her a file that contains her biography. 

    “You… you… you run a background check on me?” She shuddered already scared. “Who are you?” She shouted, and stood up ready to alert the neighbors. 

   “Relax, darling. You should be more concerned about your mother. It’s past her regular time to return home, and the rain is pouring heavily. Any accident could occur.” She said, scaring Paulina more. 

   “What are you talking about? My mother is probably still at the market waiting for the rain to subside before leaving.” 

  The woman chuckled, and shakes her head. “I see… you don’t even have a tv, how can you be updated?” She teased, and the man gave Paulina a tablet.  

 Before she could look at the screen, she heard a hard knock on the door, and the door opened even before she could respond to it - to deliver her nightmare. 

   “Pauli, is your mother home?” Ms. Rena their neighbor asked. 
.
  Ms. Rena and Paulina’s mother sells fishes in the market together, and they always leave home and return back together everyday. 
 .
   “Ms. Rena, I’m supposed to ask you. You and mum are supposed to be together.” 

   “Oh, God. I left her at the market when my son came down with fever, I had to take him to the hospital and your mother promised to help me pack.”

    “Oh… she isn’t home yet. She is probably stranded at the market because of the rain.”
     
    “Nooooo… people saw her leave the market, and the next bus had an accident, your mo…-“

    “What?” Paulina cut in. “My mother was involved in an accident ? Don’t joke around Ms. Rena, you left my mother at the market and she couldn’t leave because of the rain. Nothing happened to my mother.” 

    “She got on the bus, Paulina. And there was an accident.” Ms. Rena cried. 

    “Noooooooooooo….” Paulina wailed, and Ms. Rena failed to console her. 
*
  They went to the accident scene, and they couldn’t confirm her mother’s body. The accident was very fatal that most of the victims body were burnt down. 

  They couldn’t find her mother’s body but they were able to recognize her because a bracelet she always had on. Paulina didn’t know the history behind the bracelet, all her mother told her was that it was the last gift she received from her mother, and promised to give it to her after highschool. 

    “Mummmm…”. She cried, and took the bracelet. 

    “This is your mother’s bracelet.” Those around said. 

   “Nooooooo…. Mummy….”  She cried. 
*
  She was escorted back home by the strangers, and met the living room flooded - the pail under the licking zinc fell but she couldn’t even do anything. Her head was blank, wishing that everything was a nightmare that she would wake up from. 

    “Darling, your mother is gone, but you aren’t alone. You have a family that have been searching for you all these years.” The woman said, and Paulina stared at her blankly. 

   “Take a look at this.” She said, and the man dropped pictures on the table. 

  Different pictures of her mother while she was younger, with a man. They both look happy in the pictures, and her mother looked different - she was all dressed up. 

   “Your father is waiting for you.” The woman said, and Paulina looked at her. 
     
   “My father…”. She whispered, talking for the first time since they left the accident scene. 

     “Yes, darling. Your father.” The woman said, and the man with her gave Paulina a DNA test result which showed 99.9999999999% matched of the two sample tested. 

   “Your mother left when she was pregnant with you, and your father has searched endless for you. Who knows that you existed somewhere in this small village.” The woman said, and Paulina looked at her. 

   “You. Who are you?” She asked. 

   “Your new mother.” The woman replied all smiles. 
*
STANFORDS’ MANSION***
*
 Paulina alighted from the fancy car, and looked around the mansion that the car pulled over. Her mother was gone, she had no other family so accepting the new family that came to find her was a blessing to her, but to top it  all, her supposed new family are Billoniares. She glanced around blankly wondering the kind of life waiting for her in the new world she found herself within a blink of an eye. 

    “Come inside, darling.” The woman said, and gestured a maid to collect her luggage- a small bag that contains most of her things, and the valuables her mother left behind. 
*
  She trailed behind the woman, as they emerged into the paradise in disguise of a house. 

  The woman asked her to wait behind, and she took her time to look around before the woman returned with a man - the man in the picture with her mother. 

    “Who… who’s she?” The man asked, and her face fell - her supposed father didn’t even know of her existence. 

    “Happy birthday, honey. This was the birthday present I was preparing for you, so it came late. Meet Paulina, your daughter with Charlotte.” The woman said, and his eyes widen. 

    “My… daughter?” The man shuddered

 Obviously he never knew she existed, so something was wrong somewhere. It only means that her stepmother was the one who knew about her existence and brought her here, but for what reason? Was she just being kind?

  A lot of questions run on her mind, but she decide to ignore it all, because she could never find an answer by thinking about it. 

   “That bracelet… you’re truly Charlotte’s daughter.” The man said, and she touched her bracelet before looking at him. 

   “Don’t be dramatic, there are a lot of time for that. Paulina, you should freshen up first.” The woman said, and gestured a maid to escort her away. 
*
  Paulina was escorted to the most beautiful and elegant room she could never dream of. She took her time to look around and opened the wardrobe that was already filled with clothes and other necessities. 

   “You can call me if you need anything, ma’am.” The maid said. 

   “Wait! Someone collected my luggage when I arrived, do you know where she kept it?” She asked, and the maid shook her head. 

    “No ma’am, but I’ll help you ask around.”

    “Ok, thank you.” 
*
  She took her time to look around, and started wishing her mother was there. After wailing for few minutes, she went to freshen up when a maid came to fetch her, and she was escorted to a different living room; there her supposed father and stepmother were. 

    “You now look like a Stanford now, come take a seat darling.” The woman said, and she nodded before she obliged. 

    “Well… I didn’t introduce myself properly earlier, I’m Grace Packard Stanford, and as I said earlier, I’m your new mother, and he is chairman Paul Stanford, your father. But that’s only going to be between us.” Ms. Grace said, and Paulina looked at her. “You are an illegitimate daughter of my husband past affair that we just knew of, and you recently lost your mother which means we are your only family now. So, in order to save the face of this honorable family, and also shield yourself from scandals, you are going to remain Paulina Andrew, a pitiful teenager we adopted after the tragic passing of her mother.” She added, and passed a document to Paulina. “Sign that, my husband signature is already there as a sign of his approval.” 

   Paulina looked at Ms. Grace, and her supposed father who avoided eye contact with her since she got there. 

    “Ma’am, may I ask why you came looking for me? I doubt if my father knew of my existence as you claimed.” She said, and her father looked at her - his expression was blank and unreadable. 

    “Because you could be a barrier in my election. I’m running for mayor, my husband having an illegitimate child would banter my election, so it’s best I’ve my weakness under control. Is that enough to answer your question?” Ms. Grace replied, and Paulina stared at her speechlessly. “I’m impatient, darling. Let’s make this quick.”

    “What if I don’t sign this? What if I want to return back home?”
    
     “This is your new home now, home is where your family are. We’re your new family now. Even if you leave now, you can’t cause me much problems, but you could also save yourself and grab this new home. Many people would love to be in your shoes. I won’t bother you, neither would I protect you. You aren’t existing to me, but I want you under my watch. It’s a win-win for the both of us, darling. Even your father isn’t against it. Don’t act all righteous, it’s not like you have any other choice.” Ms. Grace said. 

  Paulina sniffed in, and looked at them before picking the pen. Ms. Grace was right, it’s not like she had any other choice. 

    “Good girl. Now we can live as one big happy family. Welcome to the family, darling.” Ms. Grace said, and stretched her hand for a shake. 

  Paulina hesitated for a while before accepting it. Ms. Grace was right, they are families now. 
*
 Paulina tossed and turned on her bed thinking of how her life would be in the house where she doesn’t seem to belong - a nonchalant father who allow his wife have her way, and a stepmother that is willing to do anything for her political career. While thinking about it all, she was thankful that there was no stepbrother or stepsister, otherwise her new world would be fit for a fairytale. She was still deep in thoughts when she heard a knock on the door - a maid was outside to escort her to the dinning hall. 

 She arrived at the large dinning hall, and met her new family there, words couldn’t describe her shock when she saw a girl about her age seated in the dinning as well - now she is living in a fairy tale. 

   “Mum, who is she?” The girl blurted. . 

    “Ava, be nice. She is the one I told you about, your sister.”

    “What sister? This must be a joke, mum.” Ava shouted, and Paulina bite her lips nervously. 

    “Paulina, darling. Take the seat, next to Ava.” Ms. Grace said, and Ava glared at her. 

     “Mum, you can’t be serious. First, you made me return from England, and now you are introducing me to a sister?” 

    “Ava, maintain the dinning rule.” Chairman Stanford muttered. 

    “Dad, what were you doing when she brought in this?”

    “Ava, she is same age as you, so help her adjust properly.” Ms. Grace said. 

    “Is this why you ask me to return? To help dad’s illegitimate daughter fit in?”

    “I ask you to return so I can keep a close watch on you, I don’t want you to act careless in England and ruin things for me. My competitors are not joking at all. That’s why you will also help Paulina adjust, just until the election is over. Then… you can return freely to England if you want to.” 

    “You take care of your election, I’m not obligated to anything.” Ava shouted and hit Paulina, sending her to the ground before leaving in anger. 

    “Help yourself up, darling.” Ms. Grace said, all smiles. 

   Paulina managed to get up, and started wondering how she would survive in that house. 

   “I already work your transfer to a new school. Holland High. Only few people are privileged to attend the school because it’s meant for conglomerates only. Welcome to your new world, darling.” Ms. Grace said, and Paulina looked at her. 
**
**
  Paulina arrived at school, and all eyes fell on her. Thanks to Ava who pushed her out of the car to stain her uniform, she stand out. 

  She blinked her lashes nervously and bite her lips wondering how to attend classes on her first day in her stained uniforms. She was lost in thoughts looking at all the disgusting glare sent at her. 

   👤”Who’s she?”
    👤”When do peasants get to attend our school?”
  👤”I thought only one scholarship student is accepted per session.”
   👤”Gosh, I gotta transfer or she leaves.”

  The students murmured, and some started taking pictures and videos of her. 

    “Gosh, you stink. Change to that.” Ava said, and threw some clothes at her. 

   👤”Oh, my. It’s Ava.”
   👤”Ava Stanford is back.”
   👤”Ava is back.” 

  The students shouted, and started forming a circle around them. 
*
  Paulina looked around before  bending down to pick the clothes, and Ava stepped on her. 

    “There… that’s where you belong, you belong under my feet. So know your place.” Ava blurted, and the students gasped and started taking videos. 

   The hallway suddenly became silent, and everyone started to pave way. The only person  that could make that possible was Michael Russ, the leader of MASS and also the owner of the school. 

  Fours boys emerged the hallway walking majestically until they got to the scene, and Ava glared at them. 

   “Ouwww… Ava is back.” Austin, one of the MASS said, and Michael stared at her emotionless. 

    “Pave way.” He muttered, his voice soft but commanding. 

    👤”What’s going to happen now?“
    👤”Do you think Ava would pave way for Mike?”
    👤”Of course he is Michael, the only son of chairman Russ, and the owner of the school. Why won’t she?”
    👤”They used to be close in first year.”
   👤 “She was his ex.” 

   “Why is it getting noisy here?” Michael lashed, and everyone kept mute. 

     “Ava, why don’t you wrap this up and grab some snacks with us before attending class.  We could skip the first class.” Austin said, and Ava glared at him taking him aback. “Oops, I take that back.” He muttered, and winked at her. 

   Micheal walked closer to her with just few inches gap between them, while Paulina sobbed silently on the floor wishing she could teleport away from there. 

   “What’s going on here?” A teacher asked approaching them, and all the students dismissed, excluding the MASS, Ava, and Paulina who is still weeping with Ava foot on her. 

    “Oh…. Mr. Russ, Miss Stanford, the both of you can continue your conversation later, class already started. And Miss Stanford you still have to complete your transfer paperwork.” The teacher said. 

  They both ignored rye teacher glaring at each other while Paulina weeped silently, wondering if she was invisible to them. 
.
 TO BE CONTINUED…

*
 Paulina had the most hectic dinner then returned to her room and a maid trailed behind her with her uniform. A peek at the uniform already tell that Ms. Grace wasn’t exaggerating about the school, and she started doubting if she actually belongs there. 
*
  She woke up the following day after dreaming  about her regular day back at home with her mother, the dream felt so real that she broke down in tears when she woke up. 

  She put herself together before going to freshen up and prepared for school. 

  A maid came to escort her to have breakfast, leading the way to a different dinning room. Paulina wondered why the house was that big and complicated with too many duplicate. If no one was escorting her, she could likely get lost. She got there and met her stepmother who welcomed her with a smile - she seems to be the only one who cared about her existence, although she has foul intentions. 

   “Good morning, ma’am.” She greeted. 
     
    “Morning darling, hope you slept well and enjoyed your first night in a princess world?” She asked, and Paulina chuckled shallowly - she would rather not wake up. 
     
   “I did, ma’am.”

    “Good. I stayed back to inform you that you would ride with Ava to school. Have a nice time at school.” With that she left. 

  Ava got downstairs, and Paulina contemplated before joining her. 

    “Good morning, Ava. Your mother said I would be riding with you to school.”

    “You are so audacious.” Ava blurted, and a message beeped into her phone from her mother. 

  💬*Behave, baby. I’ve an eye on you.* it reads and she scoffed. 

   “Which of you is spying on me?” She shouted, and the few maids around flinched. “I wish I could just tear you into pieces, but I gotta give you a ride anyways.” She blurted, and left while Paulina trailed behind her. 
*
  The car was already close to Holland High when Ava ordered the driver to stop. 

   “Something stinks.” She said. 

   “I’m going to spare the freshener again, madam.” The driver said. 

     “I don’t think it’s from the air.” She blurted, and alighted from the car. 

   She opened the door to the passenger’s seat that Paulina was seated, and asked her to come down. 

  Paulina looked at her wondering if they were going to walk to school before she obliged. 

    “I believe the smell is coming from there.” Ava said, and pushed her to the ground. “The school is few blocks from here, see ya.” With that she returned to the car and the driver drove off.  

  Paulina helped herself up, and started dusting her uniform, but the stain didn’t come off because the ground was still wet from rain. 
    
   “How am I supposed to go to school with this stain? And I can’t even walk back home.” She cried. 
*
 HOLLAND HIGH SCHOOL ***
*
  After the hectic moment, the MASS walked past Ava, and Ava kicked Paulina before leaving. 

   “You should avoid getting into trouble.” Was all the teacher said before leaving, and Paulina started wondering what kind of school that was. 
*
  She found her way to the bathroom and tried to wash off the stains from her uniform but it didn’t come off. The clothes Ava threw at her were already torn and she started sobbing thinking of how to carry on that day. 

    “Hey, you can change into this and avoid Ava and the MASS for the rest of the school year.” A girl said, and gave Paulina a uniform. 

    “What is mass?” Paulina managed to ask. 

   “What? You don’t know MASS? They are a group of friends, Micheal Russ, Austin Wilson, Stanley Hewlett, and Steven Hearst. How come you don’t know them?” The girl gasped. 

    “I’m a new student.”

    “Even still.… everyone in California knows them. They are the sons of elites billionaires, the heir to trillions of wealth. They rule this school.”

    “Why must I avoid them?” She muttered, and the girl gasped trying to hid in laughters. 

    “I’ll tell you all you have to know about them  later, not just them but Holland high. It’s not just a school to study, we are in a heist to survive. We can’t meet in the open because I don’t know why Ava bullied you earlier, so let’s meet at the cafeteria rooftop during recess. Don’t say hi if we meet outside, for my sake at least. Because I’ve to be saved to be able to save you.” The girl rushed her words and hurried out, but stopped when she got to the exit. “I’m Sophia by the way, Sophie. But that doesn’t make us friends.” With that she hurried out. 

 Paulina let out a sigh wondering how she is going to survive a day at school. 
*
  She changed into the uniform that Sophia gave her; although it didn’t fit properly, but it’s better than a stained uniform. 

  She found her way to the principal’s office, and was given all the necessities, before he introduced her to a teacher that escorted her to her class - supreme class. 

 The teacher lead her to the elevator and they stopped in a floor that looks different from the others, there was a lock in a slide door, and the teacher had to use her ID card to scan it open, and also taught her how to scan with her ID as well. 

The teacher went in to announce her presence before summoning her in, and she entered the modest classroom, the classroom can’t be referred to as a classroom in her world - there were comfortable seats. She couldn’t take her time to look around when she realized that she was supposed to introduce herself. 

    “What? She is in our class?” A girl shouted. 

    “I thought she was a peasant.” A boy blurted. 

    “Class, please, let’s allow her introduce herself.” The teacher said, and gestured her to go ahead. 

     “Good morning, class. I’m Paulina Andrew.”

     “Andrew?” A boy repeated. 

    “Search that name up.” A girl said, and all the students started pressing their computers. 

    “Hun hun, Ms. Andrew, please go take your seat.” The teacher said. 

    “Hey, who the f¥ck are those Andrew’s’.? I can’t find anything on them.” A boy shouted, and others started supporting him. 

   “Who are you and how did you get privileged to this class?” A boy asked, and blocked her path. 

    “Everyone, she is a new student so help her feel welcome.” With that the teacher left. 

    “Guys, why don’t we get to know her first. No peasant can access this floor, not to mention our class.” A girl said, and everyone nodded. 

     “I don’t know who you are, but I hope you aren’t just lucky and actually belong here.” The boy blocking her path said, and returned to his seat. 
 
   Paulina wobbled on her feet to the vacant seat, and everyone started chattering, but the chatters subside when the MASS entered the class. 

    “Oh, she is in our class?” Austin said, and they all looked at her expect Micheal who continued on his way to his seat. 

    “What gibberish school is this?” Paulina thought and placed her head on her desk. 
*
  The classes went on, and Paulina wondered how they receive lectures in the school - the teachers just come to class and reference them to a text book or a link online. Each teacher did the same till its recess and she stood up to leave in a hurry, but she bumped into the one everyone respects - Micheal Russ. 

    “Are you blind?” Austin shotted. 

    “I’m… I’m sorry.” She shuddered. 

    “Do you think your apology worths anything?” Stanley shouted. 

    Micheal glared at her and hit his shoulder against hers to continue on his way. 
 
    “That girl got guts.” Austin said. 

    “She was the one in the scandal with Ava this morning.” Stanley said. 

      “We know.” Austin blurted. 

     “Michael, why are you awfully quiet today?” Stanley asked. 

    “Are you blind? Can’t you see that Ava is back? Considering the history between them, how do you expect him to be?” Austin lashed. 
*
  Paulina went to the rooftop, and met Sophia there waiting for her. 

     “Sophie.” She called, and Sophia fell on her knees immediately. “Sophie, are you ok?” She asked worriedly and hurried to her. 

    “I’ve sinned against you, please I plead for mercy. Forgive me.” Sophia cried. 
     
    “What did you mean? It’s me, Paulina. You gave me your uniform.”

    “Who am I to give you my tattered uniform? I should have removed this neat one for you instead, no, I should have ordered a new one for you. Oh, my. I’m sorry, now can I make up for this?” She cried, confusing Paulina. 
*
  Paulina managed to make her stop crying, and they both sat down before she explained to Paulina the reasons behind the sudden outburst, it was because she realized that she belonged to the supreme class. 
   
    “Are there different kind of classes again?” She asked already exhausted, and Sophia gasped. 

    “How come you don’t know and you are in the class? Who are your family? What’s your name? Are you new in this planet?”

   “Too many questions. Which should I answer first? But I rather answer none. You wanted to tell me something about some boys, and request we meet here.”
    
     “Yes, your royal majesty. I was going to tell you about the MASS. Am I permitted to speak freely?” Sophia asked bowing her head. 

    “Quit this, Sophie. You are making me uncomfortable.”
     
    “Oh no, oh no. I can’t dare such.” Sophia said, and Paulin let out a sigh. 

    “Sophie.” She called, and Sophia composed herself. 

    “The MASS… I already introduced them to you earlier, I mean I told you their names. Micheal Russ is the youngest CEO in California, he became the managing director of Yikes Enterprise at age 12, and he is currently the director of this school. Unlike other teenagers, Michael have been running his father business since childhood, rumors have it that he attended his first board meeting at age 5, and served as an intern in their company in New York when he was 10. Well… what do we expect, he is the only son of billionaire, Leonard Russ. I heard that he will be inaugurated as the chairman after high school.” She said, and Paulina’s eyes widened in bewilderment. 

    “Wow.” Was the only word that escaped her mouth. 

    “And Austin Wilson is the only son of Grayson Wilson, he has three elder sisters and they are all married to elites. His eldest sister is married to a prince from England, his immediate sister is married to a prime minister in Thailand, and his last sister is married to the only son of Dowan’s Groups - they are a prestigious family of lawyers, the chairman is a retired judge, his wife is a retired prosecutor, and their only son is a famous lawyer and also the director of Dowan’slaw firm. They also own a law school and the chairman was also the former mister of Justice. Austin is also a rare gem in his own realm, he is the master behind the school basketball, he has won different trophies for the school, and he has also represented the country in basketball tournaments, he is the only heir to Will’s Group. A large group comprises of many flourishing companies.” She said, and Paulina looked at her astonished. 

    “Wow.” She muttered again. 

    “As for Stanley Hewlett, he is a born genius. The brain of mathematics, and cybersecurity. He never failed any maths competition, and he can hack into any connected device. He is a genius, and also the third son of King’s Group. His family own the best hotel in California, King’s lounge, and also an airport and many shopping malls.” She said, and Paulina nodded. 

    “What about the last?” She asked. 
    
    “Oh, Steven Hearst? I know nothing much about him. He is the most discreet among the MASS and nobody dares to pry. He barely talk, and I doubt if he is good at anything. Rumors have it that he is from an infamous mafia family. Trust me, you don’t know want to get on his bad side.” She said, and Paulina nodded. 

    “How come you knew all these?” Paulina asked. 

    “That’s not a question. Oh, mind my speech, pardon me but everyone knows. If you are a student of this school, you must know all that. It’s mandatory if you want to survive. Who am I to talk, you are also one of them. But why was Ava bullying you earlier?”

    “Ava?” Paulina repeated confused on what to say. 
     
    “Yes. Don’t you know Ava  Stanford, the only daughter of billionaire Paul Stanford. The chairman of First Group. Her mother is currently campaigning for mayor, her grandfather was a former prime minister. Ava is very scary, back then in middle school we usual add her group to the MASS to form MASSALAR.”

     “MASSALAR?”

    “ALAR stands for Ava, Lia, Alicia, and Rae. They were the queen bees during first year. They are very close to the MASS so we joined them together. Ava was dating Michael then, but rumors have it that she broke up with Micheal and flee abroad. Now that she is back, it’s gonna get more hectic. Everyone has to be on their toes.” 

     “Oh… so she is that scary.”

     “More than scary. No scholarship student ever survived a semester when we were in middle school. They either transfer or drop out. Many even tried to commit suicide. I heard she was also infamous at her school I. England as well, I wondered why she returned.”
    
    “And you? Why are you so scared of them?”

    “Gosh, everyone is. Even those in the supreme class are, not to talk of me in the regular class.”

     “I wonder why there are different class?” Paulina grumbled. 
    
    “That’s because everyone has different class outside, so the school also try to maintain same status. We have the regular class, middle class, and supreme class.”

    “Middle class?”
    
    “Middle class are for those that can’t make it to the supreme class, there are mostly children of investors, and high share holders in the school. They are be billionaires that surpassed the regular class, but they aren’t fit for the supreme class, so there is a different class for them. And the regular class are for those that could afford the tuition, but the school cared less about our existence. Our status is the necessary to earn us  admission.There are differences in our ID card, the middle class and supreme class has an OR code in their ID card that can give them access to all floors, but the middle class can’t access the supreme class floor. Let me see your ID card.” She said, and collected Paulina’s ID card. “Oh, I’ve always wanted to touch this.”

   “Let me see yours.” Paulina said, and she gave her the card. The only difference was the OR code she mentioned, then she realized why the teacher was teaching her how to use it. 
   
   “This is sooooo beautiful. I wlways wish to touch one before I graduate. This is a fulfillment.” She said kissing it, and Paulina let out a sigh. 

   “So there are three different class in this school.” Paulina muttered, and Sophia looked at her. 

   “Yes, babe. Welcome to Holland High where every student maintains their class, and those who try to cross the line learn the hard way. Oh, I forgot to mention the bottom class.” 

    “Bottom class?”

    “Only one student belong to that class in a session. That  means, we only have a bottom student among us so there is no separate class for them, but they know their place. They are scholarship students. Since the school couldn’t design a separate class for them, their uniform is different, they have a different type of collar and even their custom, for us to recognize them.” 

    “Oh… so you belong to the regular class.”

   “Yes. My family own a shopping mall, and few department stores, so how could I compete for a class? I always thought my family were welloff while growing up, but when I get  admitted into holland, I realized that I still have a long way to go. And you, who are you? You are able to make it to the supreme class, and got bullied on your first day.” Sophia asked, and Paulina looked at her at loss. 

   “Who am I?” She thought. 
**
**
  Chairman Stanford got to his office, and his head secretary Mr. John went to him. 

    “Sir, I’ve news for you. Ms. Charlotte is d£af, she was involved in an accident.” 

    “I know.” The chairman muttered, and his secretary looked at him. “Grace told me, and she brought my daughter home as well. What are you doing John? How come Grace found them before you?” He shouted. 

    “I’m sorry, sir.”

    “Sorry can’t take responsibility for this, I’ve to protect my daughter from Grace, but now it’s too late. I’ll have to dance to her tone.” He grumbled with clenched fist. 

  He excused his secretary later, and took a picture from his cabinet. 

    “Even till the end, Charolette, I failed to protect you, and our daughter.” He muttered, sniffing silently. 
.

 <<>>YEARS AGO>>>>
*
   Three children were climbing up the mountain, their families went hiking together and the children were no expectations. 12-year old Paul Stanford, 11-year old Grace Packard, and 12-year  Louis Crocker.  The Stanfords’, Packards’ and Crockers’ are all friends united by the interest they got from each other. The Stanford’s are the owner of First Group, one of the top 3 groups in California, and top 10 group in America. The Packards’ are a politician’s family, they have high positions in politics and a lot of connections around the world, while the Crockers’ are medical professionals; the family own a lot of hospitals and their children are all trained to be medical experts. The three families bond have grown for decades, which unite their children as friends. They hang out together often to have fun, and chatter the stress away. 
*
  The children were struggling to climb up, and Paul was fast ahead of them. 

    “Hey, Paul, why are you so fast?” Louis shouted, already exhausted. 

   “You should try to be fast yourself rather than complaining.” Grace teased. 

    “Hey guys, I’m here.” They heard a familiar voice shouting, and they turned to see their friend - 10_year old Charlotte Flood. 

  The Floods’ are high influenced family and the owner of FD Group. Charlottes grandfather is infamous as a notorious businessman, and his son also follows same steps. Her parents are barely home, and she attends same school as the other three children which made them close, and they hang out together often. 
*
   “Hey, Charlotte. Wait there, I’ll get you.” Paul said, and started climbing down. 

    “What? You are climbing back? She could just follow behind.” Grace complained. 

    “Paul, I’m closer, I’ll get her instead.” Louis said, and started climbing down too. 

    “What? Even you?” Grace shouted. 

     “Who is going to get Charolette first?” Paul shouted, and they started rushing. 

     “Be careful guys, I could actually climb up myself.” Charlotte said. 

     “Heyyyyyy… I just heard a strange sound.” Grace screamed scaring the others. 

  Paul missed his step, and started rolling down from the mountain. 

    “Nooooo…. Paul.” They all shouted. 
*
*
 <<<<<BACK TO PRESENT>>>>>
*
 HOLLAND HIGH SCHOOL ***
*
  Ava went to the cafeteria, and three girls went to join her. 

   “What?” She muttered looking up at them - they are her friends - Lia, Alicia, and Rae. 

    “Don’t you owe us an explanation?” Lia said, and the others nodded. 

    “I doubt.” She muttered, and continued her meal, but Rae dragged it away from her. “What?” She shouted, and stood up angrily and the students started to gather. 

   “You suddenly appeared without informing us, you sneaked in on us and we had to find out about your presence from the scandal, do you think that’s fair? Is that how little our friendship worth to you?” Lia fired back. 

   “She even ignored us in class.” Rae blurted. 

   “What are you girls saying? I know you’d accept me no matter how bad my entrance was.” Ava said, and they all giggled before embracing her. 

   “We’ve really missed you, Ava.” Alicia whined. 

   “You girls are the only people I missed here.” She muttered, and they all joined her table. 

    “Babe, who was that girl from the scandal, I heard she belongs to the supreme class.” Lia said. 

    “My worst enemy.” Ava muttered with clenched fist. 

     “Is she your family’s rivalry daughter?” Rae asked, and she scoffed. 

    “That would have been better, but she is more of my rival. She could take everything from me if I aren’t careful.”

    “What do you mean?” Alicia asked. 

    “Should we get rid of her?” Lia asked.

    “We’ve to.” Ava muttered, and they all smiled. 

     “I’ve missed you, babe. I’m incomplete without you.” Lia said, and embraced Ava while the others giggled. 

   “You are screwed, Paulina.” Ava thought smiling wickedly. 
**
**
  Paulina was able to escape Sophia question thanks to the bell that giggled to signal the end of recess. 

  They both left the rooftop and Sophia headed to her own class while Paulina took a turn leading to her own class wondering who she really was in that school. 

  She was lost in thoughts when three girls approached her to bring her out of her thoughts. 

    “Hi, I’m Lia, you must be the new student in the supreme class?” Lia said, and she nodded. 

    “Yeah, I’m Paulina.”

    “What a name.” Alicia said, and they all giggled confusing Paulina. 

    “Such a nice ID card you got.” Rae said. 

   “Can I take a look?” Lia asked, and pulled it forcefully. 

    “Arggh..”. Paulina groaned. 

    “Sorry, Paulina Andrew. But I’ve to check this out, and I’m late for class.” With that Rae left. 

     “Wait. My ID.” She shouted, and intend to go after her but the other two blocked her path. 

     “Poor you, she already left with it. But don’t worry, I’ll ensure she returns it.” Alicia mocked. 

     “Gosh, how can you attend classes now? And it’s your first day.” Rae whined. 

     “Maybe there is a class you can attend without the ID access. That’s where you actually belong.” Alicia blurted, and with that they left. 

    “Oh my, what’s this? What do I do now?” She thought. 

  Their system atthe  school was so new to her, that she started doubting if it’s actually a school. 

    “How do they even know me?” She thought and instantly recalled the name, and all the names that Sophia mentioned. “Lia? Then she must be Ava’s friend. What do I do now?” She grumbled. 
*
  She went to the principal to complain about her predicament and the principal let out a heavy sigh before advising her to skip the day class till they retrieve her ID card for her. 

  She left there in disappointment wondering how a principal can’t control students in a school. 

    “This is indeed more than a school.” She thought. 

  She was so lost in her thoughts that she almost bumped into someone, and she looked up to see he is one of the MASS. She didn’t know who he was specifically, but he isn’t the leader, rather one of the disciples. 

    “I’m sorry.” She grumbled, but he ignored her and continued on his way. 

  She let out a sigh and intend to keep walking when she saw a pendant on the floor. 

   “Oh, this must have fell from him.” She muttered and picked it up; turning around to look for him, but he was already gone. “This looks expensive, I will return it to him later.” She thought, and kept it in her bag. 
**
**
  The LAR joined Ava in class, and she looked up at them wondering why they are grinning widely. 

    “What’s new?” She asked. 

     “Guess what, babe?” Lia asked. 

    “Guessing is boring.” She blurted. 

   ““We already put that girl in her place.” Alicia said, and the others giggled. 
      
     “You mean Paulina? What did you do to her?” She asked, her curiosity piqued. 
   
    “We told you we are going to put her where she belongs, and that’s actually what we did.” Rae said.

      “I don’t still get.”

      “TADA!” They all chorused, and Lia showed her the ID card. 

     “What’s this?” She asked. 

      “Paulina’s ID card.” Alicia replied. 

     “Obviously. Why is it with you?” She asked, already irritated. 

     “This will put her in her place.” Lia said. 

      “How is stealing her ID card supposed to put her in her place?” She blurted, and Lia showed her the OR code. 

    “She won’t have access to this class again.” Rae said, and Ava smirked in disgust. 

     “You will be summoned to return that back, so how does that make sense? She could even get another one. Aren’t you thinking?” She blurted, and the girls jaw dropped in awareness. 

     “Miss Lane, you are summoned to the principal’s office.” A teacher announced, and everyone looked at Lia. 
     
     “You see?” Ava lashed, and the girls sighed in annoyance before leaving. 

  Ava let out a sigh in annoyance thinking of the right way to get Paulina out of her life when someone approached her table, she looked up to see its Austin. 

     “Hi, Ava.” 

     “What?” She blurted. 

     “I see you returned in a really bad mood. I thought we were friends, we actually go way back from kindergarten.” 

     “Stop blabbering. What do you want?”

     “I invited you for a meal earlier, but you turned it down. So I’m back to invite you again properly. I guess it was the wrong timing then.”

      “I’ll pass.”

      “You should make up with Mike since you are back. Let’s start a new from a meal. You were our friend too.”

     “Well… too bad, I never considered you guys as friends. So will you leave? I need some air.”

     “That’s harsh. But incase you change your mind, I haven’t changed my contact.” He said and winked at her before leaving. 

     “So annoying.” She grumbled. “Why is everyone annoying today?” She shouted, and the few students around looked at her. “What?” She lashed, and they looked away immediately. 
**
**
 Paulina wandered around in the school, admiring its beauty. She walked around thinking about how difficult her life would become in that school and ended up finding her way to the cafeteria rooftop before she gets lost. 

  She got to the rooftop and saw a student standing very close to the tip like he is about to jump off. She remembered what Sophia told her about attempted suicide, and her eyes wide in shock. 

    “Nooooooo…. Don’t….”  She screamed at the top of her voice and went to drag him forcefully- together they both fell back together. She looked up at whom she saved, and was startled to realize he is one of the MASS - the one she bumped into earlier. “What have I done?” She thought in fear and got up immediately. 

  He glared at her speechless when he stood up and dust his body before leaving. That was when she realized that he must be Steven, the quiet one. EarPods were plugged to his ears that she doubted if he heard her scream earlier. 

  When he was out of sight, she remembered the pendant he dropped earlier and ran after him. 

    “Hey, wait. Mass, Steven.” She called but he didn’t turn around- perhaps he didn’t hear her. 

  She ran faster until she caught up with him, and dragged him, but she fell in the process and he didn’t even make any attempt to hold her. 

    He glared at her like asking her what she wants, and she put herself together before standing up. 

    “I’m sorry, but you dropped something earlier.” She said, and touched her shirt pocket where she kept the pendant but found nothing. “Oh… it was just here. What you dropped. It must have fell off in the rooftop.” She grumbled, and he continued on his way. “That means he could hear me but pretended not to.” She thought. “Hey, I’ll go get it, I must have left it at the rooftop, just wait.” She shouted, but he ignored her. “I should find it first and rerun it some time.” She thought. 

  She hurried back to the rooftop and started searching for the pendant till she found it. 

   “Finally.” She thought. “Who knows, maybe it’s not as important as I thought.” She grumbled before keeping it in her pocket and another thought crossed her mind. “It might not even be for him, it’s a feminine style.” She exclaimed 

  She was about to leave the rooftop when she heard a broadcast requesting her presence in the principal’s office so she went there immediately hoping it’s to get back her ID card. 

  On her way to the principal’s office, Ava blocked her path accompanied by the cheesy girls from earlier, and she flinched. 

   “Here is your ID, be more careful in the future.” Ava said, and gave her the ID card. 

    “Thank you.” She grumbled and collected it in fear. 

     “Girls, got something to say?” Ava blurted. 

    “We’re sorry.” They chorused, and Paulina doubt the sincerity behind their apology. 

     “Their apology might not sound sincere, but they mean it. Seizing your ID card is a very lousy game. I really wish I could get rid of you, but since it might jeopardize my mum election, we should get along till it’s over.” Ava said, and Paulina looked at her doubting her words. 
    
    “Yeah. Since you are cool with Ava, we should get along as well.” Lia said. “What do you think girls?” She asked. 

    “Yeah, yeah.” They chorused sarcastically. 

    “Thank you, I’ll be on my way now.” She said, and intend to leave but Lia dragged her back. 

   “‘Not too fast, honey.” Lia said, and Paulina flinched in fear. 

    “What…” She shuddered. 

   “There is gonna be a party at my place tomorrow night to celebrate Ava’s return. You should be there, no, you must be there to prove that we are cool.” Lia said, and Paulina looked at her. 

    “I don’t like parties, but we are cool. So I’ll pass.” 

    “You don’t have to like parties, you just have to be there.” Ava said a little intimidating, and she started wondering what they plan to do to her at that party. 
*
  When school dismissed, Paulina was walking down the hallway, and a hand dragged her - it was Sophia, and they both went to the cafeteria rooftop together. 

    “What? Lia’s party?” Sophia shouted. 

    “I really have a bad feeling about it.”

    “What nonsense? It’s a privilege to get to attend her party. What am I saying? Everyone in the supreme class are privileged to attend.”

    “There is class in parties as well?” 

    “Of course. I always wanted to attend, but here you are with the free access. You are in the supreme class, and you are personally invited by Lia. You should go, you should be there.” 
 
    “But I really have a bad feeling about that party.” She grumbled. 

   “You have to get your hair done before then. Oh… it’s tomorrow night, you don’t have enough time to prepare.” Sophia said, and Paulina looked at her. She expected nothing much from her to begin with. 

    “What will happens if I don’t attend the party?” 

   “Nothing, precisely. But since you were personally invited by Lia, you could get on her bad side and trust me that will do you no good.” Sophia said, and Paulina became silent, wondering how she would escape the party. 
.

Micheal tossed and turned on his bed when his third alarm sounded. He had already broke the first two when it sounded and he started contemplating about getting up or breaking the third one as well. He drifted back to dream land after breaking it down until his usual nightmare woke him up. He opened his eyes immediately, and left the bed. That’s just how he is, he might look outstanding among his peers, but seven alarms always work together but none ever wake him up, only his nightmare could do the magic. His staffs replace his alarms everyday. 

  Waking up to a new day has always been a struggle to him, it was like a mission to fight or accept defeat so he just had to keep moving. 

  He went to freshen up and did all his morning routine before going out, and met his staffs assembled by the doorway - as usual. 

    “Good morning, Mr. Russ.” They chorused, and he walked past them. 

     “Mr. Russ, the chairman request your present at the main dinning hall.” Mr. Jones; his personal assistant said. 

Mr. Jones has been working for him since he was a child, so of all the staff he had, Mr. Jones is the only one that dares to communicate with him. 

    “Dad is back!” He thought, as Mr. Jones escorted him to the dinning hall. 

His family was not different from many other conglomerates family; everyone are busy attending to their business that they barely seat together to have a meal. His father is the chairman of Russ Group, and his mother was the vice, neither was he an expectation. He learnt about the business in a tender age, and he has been doing well so far. His hard work earned him the position of the director of Holland high, and he intend to ace more wins once he graduates from high school. 
.
   “Good morning, dad, good morning, mum.” He greeted and took his respectable seat. 

    “I heard that you proposed that the school stop admitting scholarship applicants.” Chairman Russ said, and Micheal looked at him - that’s their usual way to exchanging greeting. 

    “Yes, dad. I felt it’s wrong for elites to associate with the likes of them. It’s understandable enough that we’ve to put up with the students in the regular class, but those scholarships students are way beneath our leagues.” He said, and the chairman nodded while his mother chuckled. 

    “Interesting.” Mrs. Russ said. 

   “Micheal, the community are against our system  of separating classes based on hierarchy, admitting one from them is our way of pleasing  them, so I would advise you rethink that before proposing it.”

     “Why should their opinions matter?”

     “Because no matter how big you are, you need the society approval   With that being said, I would advise you introduce a compulsory volunteer program for all the students in the supreme class, that’s going to be a huge advantage to you.”

      “What? No, never. No one is gonna turn up to that, I personally won’t. The purpose of canceling scholarships is to avoid the likes of them.” 

    “But you will always need them. They are the crowds that will attack each other to hail your praise.” The chairman said, and Micheal nodded, although he wasn’t pleased. 
*
 He arrived at school, and headed to the MASS suite - an elegant hangout designed for him and his friends. 

    “Mike, wassup.” Austin greeted, and they exchanged a friendly gesture. 

     “Guys, I strongly recommend that school days should be reduced to thrice a week, five days is kllling.” Stanley whined, wobbling on his feet to join them. 

     “If I got three quarter of your brain, I’d quit school.” Austin blurted. 

    “I’m against that motion. To me, school is simply a place to relive stress with pride. The weekend is always packed, buddies.” Micheal said.  
    
     “Arrggh…”. Austin and Stanley screamed, and Steven entered the room. 

    “Steve, wassup.” Austin greeted, and they both exchanged friendly gestures. He exchanged greetings with the others and went to seat. 

 They might have been friends for years, but he is always quiet. He barely utter anything in a conversation. 

     “Guyyysssss… I finally found a new babe.” Stanley said in jubilant, and they scoffed. 
 
  He fancy cars a lot, and considered them his babe. After a long list of unserious relationships, he surrendered that filling his heart with what he loves is worth more than devoting it to someone. 

    “Guys, we gotta get this one. Matching with this new ride to Chris party next weekend would make an amazing entrance.” He said, and Micheal nodded. 

     “Mike, don’t tell me you are listening to him. This obsessed man can do anything to make us get that car. I’m out, I’ve more than enough cars already.” Austin blurted. 

    “Enough isn’t the end, bro. There is what we called exceed. We might likely be the first teenager to get this in California.” Stanley said. 

   “I’m not falling for that again. I rather spend my money on a nightstand. Guys, cancel all your plans this month end it’s a three days tournament in an exclusive club. Italian girls are the hosts, and to spice it up, they grant special requests.” Austin said licking his lips. 

    “What are you going to do with your sugarpie if you are away for three days. You gotta explain that.” Micheal teased. 

    “We’ll break up before then. So, in?” He asked, and  Micheal scoffed. 

    “I gotta check my schedule.” Micheal said. 

     “Adjust it, man and don’t play director shit with us.” 

     “Three days tournament with exclusive offer is a good idea, but imagine matching in with this beautiful baby.” Stanley said, and Austin hissed  while Michael laughed. 

    “Then that means we are in. I’m gonna make reservations now.” Austin said. 
    
    “It’s still weeks away.” Michael said. 
    
    “Do you know how many people are into this offer? A tournament is gold, trust me you won’t regret it.” Austin said. 
     
     “Your wish! And I also agree with Stan. We should get that car before any other teenager gets it. We can’t lose to Roy like the last time.” Micheal said, and they both looked at him. 

   “That’s the spirt.” Stanley cheered. 

     “That was a f¥cking helicopter, buddy. I don’t think Roy is into cars.” Austin blurted. 

    “We could never tell, that guy has been getting on my nerve lately. He will also be at Chris party, we should put him in his place.” Michael said. 

     “We’ve gotten a lot of things all for that reason, but none of them ever knows their place. I’m strongly against it!” Austin blurted. 

    “Two against one!” Stanley teased, and they all looked at Steven. 
   
    “What’s your take, man?” Micheal asked. 

    “In or out?” Stanley asked, raising his hand to indicate in, then folded it as out and Steven didn’t hesitate to raise his hand. 

     “Gross… what was I even thinking? He will always follow majority.” Austin blurted angrily. 

    “And you are always against that majority.” Stanley teased.  

    “We gotta run, we are late for class.” Austin said, and they let out a heavy sigh. 

     “Gosh, man. I thought we are skipping today.” Stanley whined. 

     “I wish I could. But I need the attendance to save my head.” Austin said. 

    “I’ll take care of that.” Micheal assured. 

    “That old man is wiser now. He now request for footage from the principal. I barely managed to escape last week.” 

     “Gosh, what’s your punishment anyways to warrant these suffering?” Stanley whined. 

     “Special internship as his secretary.” He said, and Stanley gasped. 

  “That’s no punishment to me.” Micheal said, and Stanley cleared his throat. 

    “We are late for class.” Stanley said, and Austin laughed. 

   “I can’t believe this.” Austin grumbled. 

    “This is absolutely why school days should be reduced.” Stanley said, and Austin nodded in agreement. 

    “I strongly agree with you now.” Austin said. 

   “ Thanks to you, we will set a good example for the juniors behind.” Stanley teased . 

    “Gosh… do we really have to do this every week days? I thought we are here to relive stress!” Micheal exclaimed. 

    “I’ll set you up with a nice girl before Chris party.” Austin said, and Michael’s eyes brightened. 

   “What about me?” Stanley whined. 
    
    “You already have a baby.” Austin teased, and Micheal laughed. 
*
  They got to class, and the teacher paused to greet them before they went to their seats. 

   “We are really doing this.” Stanley grumbled. 

    “Shit! I can’t believe we are late.” Austin said, and they all glared at him. 
*
   After the class, everyone started talking about Lia’s party, and Austin got interested in it. 

    “Lia’s house party is always the best. She knows how to throw a party.” Austin said, and Stanley glared at him.

    “You were looking for me? Why?” She asked, with hopes that he came to comfort her. . 

    “You still have my card.” He replied, and her face fell in disappointment. 

   “Oh, yeah. I should’ve returned it earlier.” She said, and wanted to get the card from her bag but he held her hand. 

    “Are you ok?” He asked, and she shook her head before breaking down in tears. 

   “It was all lies.” She cried, and he embraced her. 

   “I know. I believe you.” He muttered, patting her gently. 

    “But no one would believe me.” She cried harder, and he disengaged from the embrace. 

  “Who cares?” He asked, and she looked at him. “What matters is that you aren’t guilty, either others believe you or not shouldn’t stop you from standing up for yourself.” He said, and helped her wipe her tears with his handkerchief. 

    “Thank you, Sage.” She muttered, and he held her hand. 

    “You should go out there and defend yourself, I got your back.” He said, and she looked at him. 

   “Lina, tell me it’s not true.” Sophia shouted joining them. “Oh, Sage is also here.” She added, and started  adjusting her hair. 

   “It’s not true, Sophie.” Paulina muttered, and Sophia went to embrace her. 

     “I knew it, I know you could never sloop so low.” Sophia said. “The nerve of that, Jayden. He even dare to call you a special scholar. Wait… are you real..-“. She was saying, but Sage cut in. 

    “…-that’s enough, Sophia. It won’t help her feel better.” Sage said, and Sophia nodded. 

   “I got your back, Lina, I trust you.” Sophia said, and Paulina managed to smile. 

    “Thank you guys, I’m so glad to be friends with  you guys.” She said, and they both embraced her at a time. 

    “We got this, Lina. Let’s go.” Sage said, and they both held her, and together they left. 
*
  They all get to the hallway and students gathered around them. A student threw cake at Paulina, but Sage blocked it and got stained instead. 

    “Sage.” Paulina called. 

    “It’s fine.” He muttered. 

   “Look who we have here.” They heard Ava voice, and all the students paved way for her as she matched forward with her friends. 

    “Sage.” Alicia called, and wanted to go to Sage but Lia held her back. 

    “The low life parasite who thought she got lucky is here with her fellow musketeers. You Pretentious peasant!” Ava shouted, and wanted to kick her but Sage held her leg while everyone gasped. “Let me go!” She shouted, and he dropped her leg carelessly making her fall to the floor. 

    “Ava.” The LAR shouted, and ran to her. 

    “I can get up on my own.” She shouted, and stood up immediately. “You… you are going to pay for this. The both of you.” She threatened. 

    “What has she done so wrong to you? Does having a special scholarship made her a loser?” Sage shouted. 

    “Then what does that make her?” Ava fired back. 

     “It makes you the loser, because you think you have  everything under control, and that is only agreeable among losers.” He blurted, and she wanted to slap him but he held her hand. 

    “Let me go.” She shouted. 

    “What if I don’t?” He teased, and she forcefully freed herself 

  She looked at him for a while and chuckled. “I’m willing to let this all go, Sage.”

   “You even know my name.” He muttered, and scoffed. 

    “I’m willing to let her go and act like she doesn’t exist in as much as she doesn’t get on my nerves, and I’ll also clear off any sort of misunderstanding.”

    “On what condition?” Sage asked. 

    “You knee to me.” She said, and everyone gasped. 

    “And if I don’t?” He blurted. 

    “I’ll ensure her scholarship is over, and her life will be over along with Jayden’s.” She blurted. 

    “Don’t, Sage, don’t.” Alicia whispered, and Lia gave her a warning look. 

     “You don’t have to get involved, Sage.” Paulina said. 

    “I thought school dismissed already.” They heard Michael’s voice from behind. 

  Everyone kept mute and started paving way for the MASS. 

    “What’s going on here?” Michael asked, and Ava glared at him. 

    “Stay out of this, Mike.” Ava blurted, and Micheal chuckled. 

    “I thought I made myself clear to everyone, Lina is special to me so nobody dares to hurt her.” He muttered, and everyone gasped. 

   👤”This is about to get wild.” The students mumbled awaiting the drama. 
.
