 CHAIRMAN STANFORD’S MANSION ^^^
*
Paulina tossed and turned on her bed unable to sleep, she couldn’t believe everything that happened at school. She left her room later and went to the garden to receive fresh air. She stood in the garden staring at the moon. It was an old habit that she learnt from her mother, her mother always tell her that staring at the moon when you are restless would clear your worries and help you find some relive. 

    “The moon is so round today.” She heard a strange voice say from behind and she flinched in fear before turning to see her father - he was more of a stranger to her even though they live under same roof, she haven’t seen him after the first day she came there. 

    “Good evening…. Sir.” 

    “Did I scare you?”

    “No, good night.” She said and intend to leave. 

    “Is anything bothering you?” He asked, and she looked at him wondering why he was trying to start a conversation with her. 

    “I’m fine.” 

    “Your mother always comes out to stare at the moon whenever she was restless.” He muttered, and slipped from his glass of wine.

    “You know my mother?” She found herself asking before she realized how gullible that sounds. 

  He emptied his glass of wine and chuckled. “It wasn’t a fling, Paulina, I’ve known your mother for a very long time before you came into the pictures. Charlotte and I were childhood friends.” He muttered, and she looked at him. 

 She looked at him confused, wondering how her mother could be his childhood friend. He looked like he was born with a sliver spoon, and her mother has always struggled all her life to make hands meet. She didn’t bother to ask any question before excusing herself. 

  She went to her room and laid flat on her bed thinking about her mother. 

   “I miss you so much, mum.” She cried. 
**
**
  CHAIRMAN GREENWOOD MANSION^^^
*
  Sage joined his parents in the dinning, and they engaged in random talks and his mother noticed that he was absentminded. 

    “Sage, I just asked after your first day at your new school.” His mother said bringing him out of his thoughts. 

    “Oh… that?” He grunmbled. 

   “That wasn’t what she said.” His father teased and they all laughed. 

   “Sage, you don’t look good.” His mother said. 

    “I had a long day at school.”

    “Are your peers bullying you for not making it to that superior class or whatever it’s called?” His father asked. 

    “Not that.”

    “What sort of school is that? Father has been against that school since it was established, I’m sure he is gonna throw a tantrum if he find out  that you transfer there.” His mother  said. 

    “Sage, you haven’t tell me the real reason behind your transfer.” His father sided . 

    “Holland high is a good school, dad. Many of my friends are there.”

    “No!” His father blurted. 

    “You should have make that donation, he is obviously having a hard time at school.” His mother said. 

    “It’s hard enough that we have to keep his transfer to that school a secret from father, but he would be aware if I donates billions of dollars to the school funding program or whatsoever it’s called. But if it’s giving you a hard time son, I’ll get it done. We shouldn’t let others walk over you.”
    
    “I’m not the one having a hard time, dad.” 

   “Then it’s her.” His mother said, and Sage gave her a look. 

    “Who is her? Are you two hiding something from me?” His father asked, and Sage chuckled. 

   “I don’t know what you are talking about, mum.”

    “I know you suddenly transfer for a reason son, I know you too well, and it can only be a girl. Is it Alicia?” 

    “Alicia? The daughter of  Richard Alexander?” His father asked. 

    “Yes, her. They have been friends since kindergarten. You should invite her over some time, I would be on leave soon, you can invite her over then, we could go fishing together.” She said, and winked. 

   “That’s false, mum. Alicia and I aren’t close anymore. We stopped being close since grade school. And I didn’t transfer because of her. Mind you, not everyone loves fishing. I’m getting seasick already.” 

    “Come on, fishing is fun.” 

    “Son, you are just whining because you always loose all your catch.” His father teased. 

    “Your father is right. But if you didn’t transfer because of Alicia, then it’s another girl.”

   “Mum, she is just a friend.”

    “Yaphie! You already admit to it.” His mother giggled and he laughed softly. 

    “What’s going on? What girl?” His father asked. 

    “Just eat, honey.” His mother said, and feed her husband some food. 

    “You are the best.” He muttered with mouth full of food. 

     “She is being bullied at school and I don’t how to help her.” Sage said, playing with his food. 

    “She could simply report to the school disciplinary board.” His mother said. 

    “The system at holland high is different, honey. In my opinion, Sage shouldn’t get involved. Sage, you shouldn’t be mixed up with a complicated person like that, you can’t fight against those people at holland high. We can’t afford having enemies.” His father said. 

    “What nonsense! There is rules and law to abide to everywhere. Get her to report the bullies and get a lawyer.” His mother said. “You shouldn’t abandon your friends at their weakest, doing that will make you a fair weather friend.” His mother said, and he nodded. 

    “Who is this girl in question anyways? Don’t she have parents, and where did you meet such complicated person?” His father asked. 

   “She is on a special scholarship.” He muttered 

   “Oh.”His mother exclaimed. 

    “I can’t believe you are getting this on your first day. You shouldn’t cause us any worries, Sage. Otherwise, I’ll send you abroad.” His father said. 

    “He knows better, he is aware that he needs a good record to attend the best law school, in order to become a diligent prosecutor.” His mother said. 

    “Sage, have you really made up your mind to become  a prosecutor?” His father asked. 

    “Honey, that’s what father wants, and Sage is also pleased with it.”

   “He is the heir to the company, being a prosecutor… well, it’s his choice.”

   “Clara can just take over the company when she is of age.” His mother said. 

   “I rather make her the prosecutor, and let Sage take over.”

    “Nah… with such bad grades? Father doesn’t have any expectations on her.”

  “So how do you expect her to run the company that way?” 

   Sage let out a sigh, and dropped his cutleries. “Thank you for the meal, good night.” With that he stood up. 

    “What? But you didn’t even eat halfway.” His mother complained. 
    
    “I’m satisfied.”

    “You are always this way whenever we talk about your future. It’s fine if you don’t want to become a prosecutor, I’m sure father won’t bring too much from it. But he has always wanted three generations in the legal field.”

    “Don’t worry, Clara will improve her grades and become the prosecutor, and Sage can just focus on taking over the company.” 

   “Good night.” Sage muttered and left before they say anything else. 

   “I’ll bring your food to your room.” His mother shouted after him. 

   “He is mad again.” His father muttered. 

   “What do we do now?” His mother grumbled. 

    “You shouldn’t have brought up that prosecutor profession or even mention law school. We both know he despise hearing it.”  

   “And you shouldn’t have talked about  taking over the company either.”

   “But you made it worst when you talked about that third generation nonsense. Just take the food to his room, and make up with him. I can’t bear his wrath.” With that he stood up. 

    “What? You want me to go to him alone?” 

    “You are alone on this one.” He teased and making a of mockery look before leaving. 
**
**
 HOLLAND HIGH ^^^
*
 Paulina got to school, and everyone started pointing fingers at her again. She let out a sigh and started wondering when they would forget about the scandal. 

   “She is really unbelievable.” A girl said, and snubbed her before walking past her. 

   “I can’t believe she want to have it all. A supreme class, middle class, and bottom class.” Another girl said. 

  She tried to ignore them all and continue on her way when Sophia blocked her path glaring at her. 

   “What?” She muttered taken aback. 

   “Why did you lied to me?” Sophia asked, and she became confused. 

     “About what?” 

    “This!” Sophia blurted, and showed her the video posted on the bulletin board - the entire footage from the guest room, starting from when Jayden was there till Austin chased him out, and when she entered the room. 

   “How does this make me a liar?” Paulina asked confused. 

     “You lied about everything. Jayden was waiting for you in that room as he claimed, but Austin chased him out, you didn’t know Jayden already left before you went in, and you were surprised to see Mike there instead.” Sophia said, and Paulina let out a sigh. 

   “That’s a perfect makeup story. But if you  believe that, can you also explain who locked the door later?” Sage asked joining them. 

   “Sage.” Paulina called. 

   “Hi, Lina.” 

    “Nobody said the door was locked.” Sophia muttered, looking at the video again. 

   “Lina said so, are you going to believe them or believe her?” Sage asked, and Sophia jaw dropped. 

    “I’m so sorry, Lina, I’m sorry for doubting you.” Sophia said, and embraced Paulina. 

    “That’s enough, let’s go Lina, you’ve to clear your name.” Sage said, and they both looked at him. 

   “How will I do that?” She asked. 

    “We will report this to the school disciplinary committee, and call Jayden to the panel, he would have to present an evidence to support all his claims. Don’t worry Lina, I’ll get you a lawyer, you won’t be alone.” Sage said, and Sophia opened her mouth in amazement. 

    “Sage, do we have to take it this far?” Paulina asked. 

   “Of course! Your reputation is on the line here. That boy is going around spreading false rumors, and everyone is buying it. You are the victim here, Lina. They might not stop here, they might be plotting something again, so we’ve to take action now before it gets worse.”

    “Sage is right, Lina, you’ve to take action now and put an end to this once and for all.” Sophia said, and she nodded. 

   “I don’t know how to go about that.” Paulina grumbled.  
   
   “Leave it all to me, let’s go.” Sage said and grabbed her hand leading her away. 
*
  They got to the doorway to the principal’s office and saw Jayden coming out of the office. They intend to walk past him because they don’t know recognize him, but he stopped them. 

    
    “Sorry, can I have a word with you?” Jayden asked. 

    “Me?” Sage asked, pointing at himself. 

    “No, her.” He replied. 

    “And who are you?” Sage asked. 

    “I’m… I’m… Jayden.” 

    “It’s you!” Paulina and Sage chorused. 

    “How dare you announce that false accusations?” Sage asked, and grabbed him by his collar. 

    “Sage, please stop. Let’s hear him out.” Paulina said, and Sage released him. 

   “What does he have to say?” Sage blurted. 

   “What do you want to tell me?” Paulina asked. 

    “Can we talk elsewhere?” Jayden asked. 

    “No, you say whatever you have to say right here.” Save blurted. 

    “Yes, say whatever you have to say here.” Paulina replied. 

   Jayden nodded, and he bowed his head before going on his knees. 

   “What? What are you doing?” Paulina muttered. 

   “I’m really sorry, Paulina. I didn’t intend to do that to you, I’m really sorry.” He cried. 

   “That won’t change anything, if you are sorry then you go back there and set things right.” Sage said. 

    “Why did you do it? I know that Ava put you up to it, but why did you give in?” Paulina asked. 

    “I’ve no choice, Lia and her friends attacked me and dragged me to their car, there I met Ava, and together we all went to Lia’s party. Ava threatened to cut off my grandmother’s treatment if I refuse to do as she say so I was left with no choice. But when Austin chased me out, I left with intention to transfer school but I was unable to transfer.” He cried, and Paulina wanted to mold him but Sage held her back. 

   “So why did you make that announcement?” Sage asked. 

   “I was threaten.” He cried, and narrate everything to them. 

    “How could they do that?” Paulina in a sober voice, and Sage let out a sigh. 

   “But that didn’t change anything, man. You would have to take back everything you said, Lina also have a reputation to uphold. Saying all that was too much, so make it right.” Sage said. 

   “I’m sorry, but I can’t. If I take it back, my grandmother will be in danger.” He cried. 

  “Even still…” Sage muttered. 

    “Sage, that’s enough. His grandmother’s live is in danger.” Paulina said. 

    “So, what then? Are you going to let this go on like this? And ruin your reputation?” Sage shouted. 

    “I believe everyone will forget about eventually.” Paulina said. 

    “Lina, I don’t know the system you were used to in the past, but it’s different here. The reputation you have from high school will go a long way in your life. Even if they stop talking about it gradually, it doesn’t mean they will forget it.” 

   “But…”. She shuddered. 

    “No, buts Lina. I’m very sorry, man, but you have to worry about yourself.” Sage said. 

    “Sage..”. 

    “I think this will do.” Sage said, and they both looked at him.  

    “Look who we have here.” They heard Micheal said, and they saw the MASS approaching them. 

    “The famous anchor is here.” Austin said, and kicked Jayden sending him straight to the floor. 

  Paulina face flushed in fear, and Sage held her hand when he noticed. 

   “Mike, I really don’t know if I should trust you, but I believe you can’t act sneaky. You should know better.” Sage said, and everyone looked at him. 

    “Who do you think you are?” Austin blurted aiming at him but Stanley held him back. 

   “What’s your deal?” Micheal asked. 

   “Let that guy go first.” Sage said referring to Jayden. 

    “Why should he? We will deal with the peasant in our own way.” Austin blurted. 

   “I hope you won’t end up wasting my time.” Micheal said. 

    “Mike, don’t tell me you are giving in to this low class guy.” Austin said. 

   “Let’s hear his deal Austin, Jayden is all ours anyways.” Stanley said, and Austin smirked before kicking Jayden again. 

   “You heard that, run while I’m being nice.” Austin laughed kicking him continuously until he ran off.

 Paulina hide behind Sage in fear wondering how the MASS could be that heartless and merciless. 
*
*
 <<<<YEARS AGO>>>>
*
  Charlotte returned to her normal life and gradually forgot about her ex-boyfriend. He was way below her class anyways, and her father would never approve of their relationship. She believed love would conquer all that’s why she agreed to date him, but at the end it was all worthless. 

  She grew closer to Paul and Grace, most especially Paul who transferred to her department few days ago due to reasons that he explained but she wasn’t really interested in. Coincidentally, they were grouped together to work on an assignment, and he insisted that they meet up in her house after school to discuss to start working on it together.
.
  She stood in her house garden staring at the full moon when Paul joined her. 

    “What’s bothering you?” He asked, and she turned to look at him. 

    “I thought you left.” She muttered. 

    “Yeah, but I forgot something so I came back. You haven’t stopped your old habit of staring at the moon when you are restless.” 

   “It’s indeed a habit, but it actually helps take all my worries away.” She said, and he looked up as well. 

    “You could just stare at it from the window, the air is chilly.” He said, and placed his jacket on her shoulder. 

    “That makes it effective, the cold air, and the full moon works together. When I return to my room, I’ll be grateful for the warmth and forget my worries completely.” She said, and he chuckled. 

    “I wish it could work for me as well.” He muttered. 

    “Is something bothering you?” He asked. 

   “Many things are.” He muttered. 

    “If the moon won’t help you, you can share it with me. A problem shared is half solved I heard.”

   “Does it works when you share it with whoever the problem is coming from?” He asked, and she looked at him. 
     
    What do you mean? Am I the cause of your problem? Wait, is it because of the group assignment? It’s also giving me headache. I’m the end, I’ll surrender to paying someone to finish it. I throw away my pride, if that’s your problem.” She said, unable to control her laughter. 

    “That’s not the problem… this is.” He said, and pecked her lips leaving her startled. 

    “Paul.” She called in surprise. 

   “I like you, Charlotte. I’ve always liked you.” He said, leaving her dumbfounded by his confession. 
.
TO BE CONTINUED…

 HOLLAND HIGH ^^^

  Sage played a recording of Jayden’s confession to everyone leaving them astounded. 

    “When did you record that?” Paulina asked in surprise. 

    “Just now, when he said he wanted a word.” Sage replied. 

    “Indeed you are the grandson of the world best lawyer.” Austin hailed, and Michael scoffed. 

    “What do you want me to do with that?” Michael asked. 

    “At first, I wanted to report this to the school disciplinary board, but after hearing his confession, I thought against it. The school can’t penalize all the culprits merely because we asked them to, but if the owner of the school himself takes action, no one can stop him.” Sage said, and Michael smiled softly. 

    “So now you are admiring that he is superior.” Austin said, and Sage scoffed. 

   “I never said he wasn’t.” Sage blurted. 

   “Fine, I’ll have them penalized accordingly, but I’m not doing this because you asked, you aren’t deaf when I said Lina was special to me.” Micheal said, and Paulina looked at him. 

    “She is special to me as well.” Sage said, and they all looked at him. 

    “Are you trying to prove a point?” Austin asked, and scoffed. 

   “I’ll send you a copy of the recording, but I’ll hold on to the original copy incase you play foul.”Sage said, and Micheal glared at him. 

    “I’m a man of my words.” Micheal blurted. 

  “And this is your chance to prove that.” Sage said, and grabbed Paulina hand, leading her away. 
    
     “Why is he always hovering around her?” Micheal shouted making a fist. 
*
  The MASS returned to their suite later totally ignoring whatever they were planning to do before. 

    “This is our chance Mike, we can get Ava out of the picture completely. This is the best way to get  back at her.” Austin said. 

    “Mike, can you really do it? You will have to summon Ava to the panel, and she might be suspended or when worse, expelled.” 

    “serves her right, she should just return to England.” Austin said. 

   “This is not only about Ava, it also includes Lia, Alicia, and Rae. All four of them.” Stanley said. 

    “Come to think of it, Mike. I think I now understand Stan. They might deny it, or say it’s voice pitching. They could even make Jayden testify against his words.” 

    “I’ll tell everyone that I recorded this myself, that way no one could say it’s pitched. And Stanley, I want you to get all the footage since Lina resumed, find anything that could prove Ava involvement with Jayden. Steven, you 
 find the hospital that Jayden’s grandmother is  admitted. Austin you…-“. He was saying but Austin cut in. 

  “…- there is also a task for me?”

   “Find Jayden, and bring him to me.” With that he stood up. 

    “Where are you going to?” Austin asked. 

   “To hand the recording over to the disciplinary board.” With that he left. 

     “Mike is really determined to make Ava regret dumping him.” Austin said. 

   “I hope that’s the case.” Stanley grumbled, and Austin looked at him. 
    
   “What do you mean?” Austin asked. 

   “I got things to do.” With that he carried his laptop. 

    “Hey, explain what you meant by that.” Austin shouted, but Stanley ignored him. 
**
**
  Micheal went to Ava’s class, and everyone composed themselves when he went in. 

   “Hi, Mike. It’s a surprise to see you here.” Lia said, and he smirked. 

   “I thought I was the only one who had the copy.” He muttered, and she smiled. 

   “I don’t understand what you are talking about.” 

   “Don’t play dumb with me.”

  “Why? Are you suddenly accepting defeat after seeing the true colors of your little miss special?” She teased.  

     “I don’t hit women, so don’t let us drag this longer.” He said, and she flinched. 

  She composed herself later and counted few steps closer to him. 

   “There is something called backup file, handsome… turn out you aren’t as smart.” She whispered and he chuckled. 

   “Go and report yourself to the principal if you want to be spared from this.” With that he left. 

   “What do you mean by that? Spared from what?” Lia shouted after him. 

  “Babe, what did he want?” Alicia asked. 

    “He came to threaten me.”

   “With what?”

   “I don’t know, he just asked me to go and confess  to the principal if I want to be spared. I don’t even understand him.”

   “What if he actually has something?” Alicia asked. 

   “What could he possibly have? I’m sure he is just buffing.” Lia blurted. 

    “And if that’s not the case?” Alicia said, and Lia looked at her in fear. 

   “What do we do?” Lia grumbled, and they both went to Ava. 

   “We are in class, later.” Ava blurted. 

    “Ava, this is really serious.” Lia whined. 

   “The teacher is here, later.” Ava said, and they both looked at the teacher that entered the class.

    “Miss Stanford, Miss Lane, and Miss Alexander, the principal request your presence in his office.” The teacher said. 

    “We are done for.” Lia lament. 
**
**
  During recess, Paulina went to the cafeteria with Sage, and they both sat together after getting their food. 

    “This is my first time at the school cafeteria.” She said, and he chuckled. 

    “You don’t get hungry?” He teased. 

   “I actually don’t know where I belong. Sophia told me there are different classes of cafeteria.” 

     “Oh… is that so? Then this is the one for my class. This is where my classmates eats.” 

     “Oh.”
    
    “You should have gone to the last floor, that could be for the superiors or whatever you guys are called.” He said, and they both laughed. 

    “Thank you for everything, Sage.”

     “What are friends for?”
    
     “Attending that party worth it after all.” She said, and he chuckled. 

     “But you are in this mess because of that party.”

     “It will be over soon, and I’m glad that we might be able to save Jayden as well, he looks so helpless.” She said. 

     “I hope so.” He muttered, and she looked at him. 

    “Are you also doubting, Mike?” She asked, and he looked at her. 

     “Do you also have doubts?” He asked back, and she shook her head. 

     “I don’t know. I don’t really trust Mike, but he has been acting strange, I don’t understand his true motives, and that baffles me a lot.” She said, and he held her hands. 

   “Same, but we can always give him a shot, I also have my doubts, that’s why I have a copy with me as well.” He said, and she nodded.

  ALEXANDER’S MANSION ^^^
*
  Alicia returned home after school and her nanny went to welcome her. Unlike all the other staffs in the house, she was closer to her nanny and they often gist together. 

    “Madam, your father is home and he received a mail from your school. What trouble have you gotten yourself involved in?”

    “Where is he currently?”

    “In the living room, your mother is trying to calm him.” She said, and Alicia let out a sigh. 

     “It’s fine, I got it.” 

   “What happened at school?” 

   “Later, I’ll tell you later.” She muttered and went in. 

  She entered the living room and her mother hurried to her. 

    “What have you gotten involved with, Alicia?” Her mother shouted. 

    Is daddy really angry?” She muttered. 

   “Alicia, you are back.” Her father said, joining them and her heart skips a beat. 

    “Good evening, daddy.”

    “Is there anything good today? What did you get involved with at school? Why did your school have to send me a message?” He shouted, taking her aback. 

     “Daddy, it’s not me. It’s Lia and Ava, I just got mixed up in it somehow.” She fired back. 

     “Alicia.” Her mother cautioned. 

    “Ava? That Stanford’s daughter, is she back?” 

    “Yes, she returned this week.” 

   “And you are still associating with her? The daughter of your father’s opponent.” Her mother said  

    “The daughter of that rude brat who thoughts she knows a lot about politics because of her father’s influence. Why are you still associating  with her? Aren’t you aware that I’m running against her mother?” 

    “Daddy, your political opponent has nothing to do with my friendship. Ava and I have been friends since middle school, and I value our friendship.”

   “I knew it. This is all planned by her mother. She wants to use you to tarnish my image; she must’ve put her daughter up to it.”

    “Daddy…”

    “Do you know how sly that family could be? I’ve been in this political world all my life, I started from the scratch Alicia, and I made my way this far. Do you know how many greedy politicians I associate with?  And those Packards’ can do anything to win, they must’ve sent their daughter back to have something against me. They want to use you, Alicia.”

    “Daddy!”

    “Honey, I doubt if that’s the case.”

    “What else could it have been? Alicia has always have her way at school, but now she is being called out for what? Isn’t it obvious that there are people behind this? Someone in power obviously, and it could only be those Stanford’s, they are the only ones with a motive.”

   “Daddy, it’s not just me. Even Lia and Rae are involved.” She shouted. 

    “Lia? That daughter of KSB news? You should stop associating with her as well, her father is working with the opponent party, they might all be in it together “

    “Daddy, do you want me to cut ties with all my friends because of your campaign?”

   “After the election, my dear. Let’s see if they will still want to be your friend. But for now, it’s either you stay away from them or I send you abroad.” 

   “Daddy, you are so unbelievable.” With that she stormed out. 

    “Hey, Alicia, where are you going?” Her father shouted. 

    “Honey, just leave her be. What are we going to do about the situation?”
      
    “I’m going to call chairman Russ now, what could our daughter ever done wrong to be summoned  by the disciplinary board? I’m not making huge donations, and paying a fortune for her tuition to get such treatment. This could only be from the opponent, they are trying to get our daughter expelled so they could tarnish my image.” 

    “That’s never going to happen, honey. You should call chairman Russ now.” 
**
**
 Ava arrived home from school, and her mother sent a driver to pick her up. Her grandfather recently returned from his vacation, and the family planned to meet up for their usual family dates. Being in England spared her from attending such events, and now she couldn’t even contain her anger at the thought of resuming to it again. 

 She arrived at the resort they usually meet up and met her mother there. 

    “Mum.” 

    “What took you so long? I sent the driver few hours ago.”

     “It was importune. I had to take my time to prepare. I haven’t seen grandfather in a long time.”

    “Oh… I see. And your school prepared the utmost surprise for us. What’s that about, Ava? I asked you to behave.What did you get involved with to be summoned by the school disciplinary board?” 

    “It’s just a trifle with a bottom class student, I didn’t expect the school to take it that far.” 

    “I warned you to behave yourself, Ava. Just until the election is over, but you couldn’t even last a week!”

   “It’s just a trivial matter, the school boards are exaggerating it.”

    “I’ll make sure this doesn’t become an issue, but father mustn’t hear about this, and you must not mention Paulina either. He went to receive a call, he will return soon. Do I make myself clear?” 

   “Yes mum.” Ava muttered, and looked away. 

   “Your grandfather is coming, compose yourself.” Ms. Grace muttered when they saw Mr. Packard coming their way.

  He approached them with a smile, and Ava exchanged pleasantries with him. 

    “Ava, I see you have been on a low. Just until the election is over, I want you to maintain that. Your mother will become the mayor, and climb the ladder as governor next. You should grow up fast as well, and your grandfather will make you the president.” He said, and they both forced a smile. 

   “Father, isn’t that too much?” Ms. Grace asked. 

   “Nothing is too much, my granddaughter can become the first youngest president in California. I might have stopped as the prime minister, but that wasn’t my limit, I was just too old to keep climbing. But I’ll make my granddaughter the president.” He said, and patted Ava’s shoulder. “What do you think, my dear?” He asked, and Ava smiled. 

    “I appreciate it grandpa, it’s huge but that’s where I belong.” She replied, and they all laughed. 

    “Do you see that? That’s the spirt. My granddaughter is so much like me. If you weren’t chasing Paul around while you were younger, you would have learnt a lot as well, and could have climbed the ladder higher by now.” 

   “Father, it’s not late until it’s over.” 

    “Well… you got that. Noting is late, as long as you are my daughter.” He said, and they all smiled. “Ava, how does it feel to be back?”
    
    “I wish I could return.” She wanted to say but changed her mind. “No special feelings, but it’s nice since I could see you on our regular dates again.” 

    “That’s my girl. Grace, why is your husband not here yet?” 

    “Dad, he can’t make it. He has important meetings.”

    “Important meeting? So this isn’t important enough for him to clear his schedule beforehand. That good for nothing husband of yours is always finding an excuse to escape meeting with me.”

    “Dad, it’s nothing like that.”

    “Shut that mouth! He is really different from his parents, I should have known better before letting you marry him.” ‘

    “Dad…” 

    “Come with me, Ava. You should only watch your grandfather’s step.” With that he led Ava away. 
*
  They all went for an archery section together, and he took his time to monitor Ava who didn’t cease to impress him. 

    “You’ve  become so good over the year, Ava. You are just that girl.” He said, and they all smiled. 
*
  They engaged in horse racing, and he asked Ava to race with the fastest horse rider among his men. 

   “Father, you want Ava to compete with Caleb?” Ms. Grace asked. 

   “No one has ever won against Caleb, but Ava has to prove that she has what it takes to be win if she really wants to be the future president.” He said, and his daughter let out a sigh. 

   “I’ll give it a shot, grandpa.” Ava blurted, and her mother looked at her. 

    “Ava…” Ms. Grace called. 

   “That’s the spirit, Ava. You’ve to take little challenges like this to overcome bigger one in the future.”

    “I understand, grandpa.”

    “Caleb, no foul play. This is simply a game to test my granddaughter’s capabilities.” He said, and Caleb nodded. 

    “I understand, sir.”  

  They engaged in the race and Ava tried her best to win her competitor while her grandfather watch in amusement cheering her on until the race ended and Ava failed  to beat her competitor. 

   “You’d well, Ava. The horse was just too slow.” Her grandfather said. 

   “Angie has been a good horse, I just couldn’t beat him.” Ava said, and her grandfather shook his head. 

   “No, my dear. It is all the horse fault. We should teach it some lesson.” 

    “What? Grandpa.”

    “You should never blame your incapacity on yourself, my dear. Let others carry the blame. That’s how you win. Now, shot it.” He said, and gave theater arrow. 

    “Grandpa, but Angie did nothing wrong. It has been a good girl since you gift her to me.” She said in a sober. 

    “It is a gift to you, so you can have your way with it. Will you shot it?”

    “Ava, get it done.” Her mother whispered loud enough for her to hear, and she started to shiver as she remembered when he gifted her the horse. 
*
*
  FLASHBACK to when Ava was 10-year-old.***
*
  During the launching of the resort, the family members arrived early, and her grandfather brought the horse to her. 

    “Wow, it’s so beautiful.” 

    “Don’t dwell on beauty, you should focus on its purpose.” Her grandfather said. 

    “Ava, do you’ve any name in mind for grandpa’s present to you?” Her father asked patting her hair. 

    “Angie, she is Angie.” She said touching the horse, and her father smiled. 
*
*
FLASHBACK ENDS***
*
  She grew fond of the horse over the years, and trained it well, so she couldn’t bear to shot it. 

   The arrow fell off her hand, and she shook her head. 

   “I can’t. “ she muttered, and her grandfather pat her back. 

   “Yeah, you shouldn’t get your hands dirty.” He said, and collected a gun from one of his guards shot the horse leg, and also shot Caleb’s shoulder. 

    “No, Angie.” Ava cried. 

    Caleb held his shoulder, and Mr. Packard chuckled. 

   “You should always know when to win, that’s how you will know your place. Ava, that’s how you win.” With that he left. 

    “Why are you such a weakling?” Her mother blurted, and followed. 

  Ava made a fist staring at their retreating back, the horse was taken away for treatment, and she  went to pick her arrow trialing after them to the stable to look at the horse. 

   “I should have won.” She muttered, and shot it with two arrows before leaving.

 <<<YEARS AGO>>>
*
  Paul and Charlotte continued their friendship like nothing unusual happened. After his confession the other day, she was so shocked that she couldn’t respond so he promised to give her time to think about it before he left. 

  They continued their friendship, and neither of them talked about it again. Charlotte has always considered Paul as a friend, so the thought of having anything other than platonic relationships was kinda weird. 
*
 It was Grace’s birthday, Paul invited Charlotte to surprise her with a visit. After the successful surprise, they all sat down  to have dessert together. 

Charlotte went to stand by the balcony later slipping from her glass of wine, enjoying the fresh air when Paul joined her, surprising her by embracing her from behind. 

    “Paul.” She muttered. 

   “I’m glad you guessed right this time. When we were younger, you always mistook Louis for every little surprise.”

    “Don’t ignore my feelings, Charlotte, I’m getting impatient.” He muttered, and she became speechless.

    “Oh… I see. Charlotte, can you assist me to organize something in my room?” 

   “What’s it?” Paul asked. 

    “I called Charlotte not you, and stay out of it, it’s ladies stuff.” She said, and she left with Charlotte.

    “He could have tell you, or give you a hint. She might even be you?”

     “Me?” Charlotte shouted. 

     “I’m just kidding.” Grace laughed, and Charlotte let out a sigh of relief. “But we could never tell, you are also a girl. But I doubt if it’s you, because he would’ve tell you by now and you have no reason to hide it from me. After all we are friends, aren’t we?” Grace said, chuckling sarcastically, and Charlotte nodded her head 

    “Yes, we are.”

    “Can you help me watch him to find out who he likes?”

    “What will you do when you find out?”

   “I just want to see her to know if she is better than me, which I doubt.”

     “And afterwards?”

     “I’ll make sure I get her out of the way, Paul is mine and I’ll do whatever I can to have him. I endured watching him with other girls for too long.” She said, and Charlotte let out a sigh. “You will help me, right?” She asked, and Charlotte looked at her speechlessly. “Thanks babe, we are friends after all.” She said, and embraced Charlotte who remained speechless.

   CHAIRMAN RUSS’S MANSION ^^^
*
  Micheal was engrossed with work in his study when one of his staffs knocked on his door - whoever it was knocked severally before leaving. He hates interruptions whenever he is working, and his staffs know better than knocking twice. His phone rang later, and he let out a sigh before picking it after he  checked the caller ID and saw it was Mr. Jones. 

 Mr. Jones called to request his presence in his  father’s living room. He let out a sigh wondering what his father wanted to discuss before he wrapped  up his work. 

 He went to freshen up and headed to his father’s living room, where his father was currently on a call. 

   📞*Not at all, chairman, it will all be sorted. I’m sure it was just a mere misunderstanding.*
   📞*Alright, we will get to it.* With that he dropped the call. 

  His glared at Micheal , and his phone rang again. Micheal instantly knew that the ruckus was caused by the issue at school. 

   📞*That’s not an issue at all.*
   📞*Goodness, we should have at least exchange greeting first, we’ve come a long way together.*
   📞*It will all be sorted, and I’ll make sure to clear any forms of misunderstanding.* With that he dropped the call and threw a fist in the air. 

    “Honey, oh, Mike is here.” Mrs. Russ said joining them. 

    “Mike, what have you done?” His father shouted, and Michael began to shiver in fear. 

    “Honey, Mike must’ve a reasonable excuse for this carelessness.” Mrs. Russ said. 

   The chairman phone rang for rang, and he checked the caller ID before glaring back at  Michael again. 

    “Is that the seventh time?” The chairman shouted. 

    “Mike, start to explain.” Mrs. Russ said. 

    “It was just a matter at school, the school disciplinary committee are going to handle it.” Mike muttered, his voice barely above a whisper, but they managed to hear and the chairman slapped him. 

   “The nerve of you to say that! You called the panel against the two running mayor candidate daughters, aside from that, one of them is a Stanford’s.  A complain against the chairman of KSB news daughter, and chairman Brown only daughter, are you kidding me?” He shouted, and slapped Micheal again. 

    “And the worst part was that the root of all these is a scholarship student. What good will a lowlife like that do you? When did you start getting involved with such people? Where was the Micheal who disgusts lowlife like them? Have you forgotten the promise you made years ago?” Mrs. Russ said, and Michael started to shiver. 

    “I… I…”. He shuddered in fear. 

   “I just got informed that Mr. Narson’s only grandson recently transferred to our school.” The chairman said, and his wife eyes widened. 

    “Mr. Narson, the president’s legal adviser?” She asked. 

    “Yes, and the worst part is the school requested for donations, so he couldn’t make it to the supreme class.”

    “Why would his grandson attend our school? He was highly against our school since it was established, he filed several petition against us for equality among students. Why would his grandson transfer to our school?” She shouted. 

    “I don’t know. His grandson recently transferred, and all these is happening. Doesn’t it seem like a trap ?” 

    “Honey, what are we going to do about this?”
 
    “I’ll take care of it, and that scholarship students will be taken care of as well. He should know his place when he is given an opportunity.” 

    “Micheal, you should have known better as the director. Your father and I trusted you, and entrust the school to you, how could you be this careless? If you can’t even lead a school, how do you expect to handle bigger tasks in the future?” Mrs. Russ said, and Micheal bowed his head bitting his bottom lip.  “That again? How many times do I warn you against showing your nervousness? You are a Russ, and others must never know when you are weak. How long will I keep repeating that?” His mother shouted, and Micheal instantly composed himself. 

   “I’m going to address this matter, and you Mike. You stay out of it, and turn in your resignation letter tomorrow.” The chairman said, and they both looked at him. 

   “Dad…”. He shuddered. 

   “Your father is right, you still have a lot to learn. A break to focus on your studies will help you a lot.”

   “Have you both always been perfect?” He shouted, and they both looked at him. 

    “How dare you raise your voice?” His mother fired back. 

    “I worked so hard, dad. I worked as hard as I could to impress you, I worked hard to get everything I’ve now. I was just trying to honestly address an issue at school, and you both threw a tantrum because of that. And now, you want me to resign?” 

    “You are a business man, Mike, there are statics in businesses, you can’t afford to have enemies when you could keep them as friends!” His father shouted.

    “How does that warrants my resignation? It was  my mistake, so I’m going to set it right.” 

    “No, you stay out of it. But this is your last chance.” The chairman said, and his wife looked at him 

    “You have never given me any chances before, those that mean I’m  not allowed to make a mistake?” 

    “Yes, because you can’t afford it. The most expensive thing a businessman can’t afford is mistake. You are excused.” 
*
   Micheal returned to his room deep in thoughts. He knew all this could happen, but he still took the risk. 

   “I must’ve lost it.” He grumbled, and laid flat on his bed. 

   “How do I look at Lina now? And that busybody who is always hovering around her.” He whined and started feeling uneasy.

    <<<YEARS AGO>>>

 Paul started courting her openly, bringing her flowers and going on dates together. She has always regarded him as a friend, but as they grew closer intimately, her feelings for him started confusing her. She couldn’t understand if she only see him as a friend or wanted more than friendship but she eventually accepted his proposal totally forgetting about Grace confession.

  Their school held a banquet to celebrate Valentine’s Day, everyone started talking about the banquet and making preparations. Grace invited Charlotte to go shopping with her, and Charlotte made up her mind to take advantage of that to tell Grace about her relationship with Paul. 

  They finished shopping and went to have lunch together. 

   “Their food here is really nice Charlotte, you should fry it.” Grace said, and Charlotte nodded. She and Paul already had lunch at the l restaurant before. 

   “Paul brought me here three days ago.” Grace said, and Charlotte chuckled because she and Paul went there there weeks ago, before they started dating. 

    “Grace, I’ve something to tell you.” Charlotte said, and Grace looked at her. 

   “I know.” Grace said, and Charlotte looked at her wondering how she found out. 

    “Ho..-“. She was saying, but Grace cut in. 

   “…- I know that Paul is dating someone. He now has a girlfriend, he goes out regularly and always busy my calls because he is on call with her, but I’m not giving up. I don’t care who she is, I’ll propose to Paul tonight.” 

    “Tonight?” Charlotte shouted, and Grace nodded. 

    “Yes! I’ll ask them to pack the food for us, I suddenly lost my appetite. But you could wait back if you want.” Grace said, and Charlotte shook her head. 

    “I’m good, we should pack it.” She muttered. 

  Her phone rang, and she ended it immediately when she saw it was from Paul, that was the fourth time he called. 

   “It seems urgent.” Grace said. 

  “Not really, it’s just my boyfriend.” She muttered. 
     
     “Wow. Congratulations, you met a new guy. Good for you, Charlotte, you know how to move on. But I’ll never let Paul go if I get my hands on him.” 

   “Grace, Paul and I are…”. She was saying, but Grace stood up. 

   “I’ll go get the waiter.” With that Grace left. 

   “Why does it feel like she is trying to avoid my conversation.” She thought, and let out a sigh.

    “What? So how are we supposed to get home? You will call them, right?” 

   He bit his lower lip nervously, and touched his pocket. “I wish I could.” He muttered, and she glared at him. 

    “Don’t tell me you aren’t with your phone either.” She lashed, and he nodded. “It’s fine, I’ve my phone, you can just call. But there doesn’t seem to be any server around here.” 

   “Let’s give it a try.” He said, and she gave him her phone. 

  He dialed a number, and called it, but it didn’t connect. 
   
    “How is this possible? How do people at this village survive?” He muttered. 

   “They should be able to survive somehow, maybe the problem is coming from here. We can just go around to look for a place with a better network.” She suggested, and he nodded. 

  They walked around together, searching around for network but it was to no avail; already exhausted, they sat under a tree to relax. 

   “Why were you left behind? There is no way they would’ve leave without you.” Paulina said, and Micheal looked at her. 

   “And you? It’s the staff’s responsibility to ensure that all students get in the bus, there should be no  exemptions.” He replied, and she looked away. 

   “We know it doesn’t work that way at that school of yours.” She muttered. 

    “School of mine? You go there too.” 

   “No one cares about my existence at that school, expect my friends tho…” She muttered, and Michael rolled his eyes before scoffing. 

   “More people care about your existence than you think.” He said, and she looked at him. 

   “You don’t have to try to make me feel better, I know my place.” 

    “No, I care about your existence.” He found himself saying, and she looked at him. 

    “You?” She asked, and he nodded as he realized what he said. 

    “Yeah, I do. We got locked uo in a room together, and ended up together here somehow. That’s enough for me to know you exist.” 

   “Is that what you meant?”

    “Of course! I’m not trying to make you feel better. What’s running in that head of yours?”  He said, and poked her head before getting up. 

    “Ouch! Was that necessary?” She shouted. 

    “If it will help you think properly.”

  “Actually, Micheal, do you know you are weird?”

   “I’m getting that for the first time. But, wait! Micheal? You call me Mikey whenever you please, and now you are calling me Micheal. Can’t you just call me Mike like most people do?” He blurted. 

  “Is that your problem now?” She lashed. 

   “Hey, why are you talking back at me?”

   “Is there a rule which states that  I can’t? Or don’t they talk back at the mighty owner of the school?” 

    “Again?”

   “I wish I could do more than lashing at you, but I enjoy attending school without worries. You literally backstabbed Sage and I, when we trusted you.” She said, and his face fell. 

   “Why mention him?” He muttered, and she looked at him in disbelief. 

    “Are you offended because I mentioned, Sage? Is that the point here?” 

    “It was above me, I really wish I could put those girls in their place, but I don’t have the final say.” He muttered, and she could feel the sincerity in his voice but she refused to be swayed, she has been deceived enough. 

   “Why did you say I was special to you? We were coincidentally locked up together, and nothing special happened between us, emphasizing that I was special to you made you weird. How am I special to you?” 

   “That was my way of getting back at them.” He muttered, and she looked at him in confusion. 

    “I don’t quite follow.”

   “You must know by now that Ava and I don’t have the best relationship, she was my ex. It’s not a secret to anyone at school.” 

    “So?”

    “Oh… I see that you knew.” He exclaimed, nodding his head. 

    “You said it wasn’t a secret.”

   “Yes, it isn’t, but only those  who were curious about me knew it, and you didn’t seem like the type. Seems like I got the wrong impression on you.”

    “Gosh, what are you blabbering on?” She muttered, rolling her eyes.

    “Well… what do I expect? I stand out a lot.”

    “Of course you do, you go around with your puppies like you own the school., what am I saying? Apparently, you do.” 

     “What puppies?”

     “What should I call those disciples of yours?”

    “What? Did you seriously called my friends that to my face?” He shouted. 

     “I’m sorry if it annoys you, but they always follow you around like puppies.” 

    “They don’t follow me around, we walk together.”

    “Following your lead.”

   “That’s because I walk faster.” 

    “I’m sorry, I must’ve offended you.”

   “I don’t care about whatever anyone says behind my back, but to my face, you should mind your choice of words.” He said, scaring her a little. 

   “You haven’t answered my question.” She said, hoping that changes the topic.

    “To your question, I don’t know why Ava loathe you, but I wish to protect you from her.” He said, and she looked at him. 

    “Protect me?” She repeated. 

    “Oh, no, no, don’t take it wrongly, that’s my way of getting back at her, because I despise her.”

    “You wanted to use me? No, you used me?”

    “I’m sorry if that was the case, I was a c0ward as that puppy of yours claimed, even though I refused to admit it.”

    “What puppy?”

    “Just as you called my friend’s puppies, you also have one yourself. He is always hovering around you, but too bad, he couldn’t follow you to the supreme class. He got a limit.” 

    “I also wish I could get back at her.” She muttered, and Michael looked at her. 

   “You waht to get back at Ava?” 

    “She locked me up in a strange room the other day, and now, she locked me up in a strange town. What worse could she do in the future?” She said, and Micheal cleared his throat. 

   “What made you certain that she was behind this?”

   “It could only be her. She put Alicia up to everything, and I was gullible to fall in their trap again.”

    “Don’t say that, Lina. You aren’t gullible for trusting people. They are the bad people who broke your trust.” 

    “I don’t know how you plan to use me to get back at Ava, but I’m giving you the permission to use me. Use me as much as you can, because that’s the only way I can get back at her as well.” She said, and he shook his head. 

   “You don’t have to say what you don’t mean out of anger, you don’t even know me.” 

   “I know that you are a j£rk.” She blurted, and he glared at her. 

    “What?”

    “I know that you can’t be trusted, I know that you are also like them, like Ava and the rest, but since we have mutual enemies, we’re in same boat. Let’s walk together, Mike, just until we get back at Ava together. I don’t care about your reasons for hating her, and you don’t have to know mine either. I won’t ask you questions, and you won’t pry in my private life as well. We will tolerate each other to get back at Ava. As long as I’ll be safe.” She said, and stretched her hand to him. 

    “Wait! Is this like some contract? Why is there so many conditions?” 

   “I see no conditions here.” She muttered. 

   “If that’s so, cool. But I’ll add my condition as well.”

    “You aren’t going to ask me to stay away from Sage, are you?” She blurted, and his eyes widened slightly. 

    “How could you tell? Can you read my mind?” 

   “Because I don’t understand why you disgust Sage so much.”

    “He is a sneaky guy, he seems to have interior motive. You shouldn’t trust guys like him.”

    “I rather not trust you.”
    
   “But too bad… you do.” 
  
    “I never said I do. I’m just getting my all, since I’ve nothing to loose.”

    “Fine, I won’t ask you to stay away from him, but let this agreement be a secret between us till it’s over, and till forever.” He said, and stretched his hand towards her. 

   “You aren’t gonna tell your friends either?” 

    “They already know.”

    “But I can’t tell mine?” 

    “My friends knew before now, and it should end there, that’s how I work, I don’t trust anyone.”

    “Sage isn’t like that.”

    “I don’t trust him, just as you don’t trust me.”

    “If that’s what you want, I’ll keep it a secret from him, but that doesn’t mean I trust you.”

    “You don’t have to trust me, but I trust you.” He said, and she chuckled. 

    “Are you trying to pull a string on me?”

    “Just accept my hand, it’s hanging.” 

    “Then it should falloff, we already have an agreement even if we don’t shake hands.”

     “What? So how are we supposed to seal it?”

    “Why? Did you betrayed us last time because we didn’t shake hands?” She blurted, and he dropped his hand. 

    “We already have an agreement.” She blurted. 

    “We should find a way out of here. If we can’t call for help, we should at least find a way back ourselves.” 

    “How do you plan to do that?”

   “Do you want to do nothing and remain stranded here?” She shouted, and he shook his head. 

    “I don’t have any money on me, do you have any?”

    “Yes, I still have Sage’s card with me.”
     
    “What? He gave you his card?”

    “Yes, a black card at that.” She flaunted. 

    “Wait! Are you two in a relationship? No, are you getting married? Why would he give you his card?”

    “Are you questioning my friendship? Is that a part of our agreement as well?”

    “I’m just curious.”

    “Curiously killed the cat.”
    
    “Are you literally comparing me to a cat?”

   “Then don’t act like one.” She said, and ran off. 

    “Where are you running off to?” He shouted, running after her. 
*
  She was lucky to find the house of the granny who saved her earlier, there they discovered that there was never a good server at the place they lay their tent, but the server at the granny’s place disappeared as well although she ensured them that it’s gonna be back before midnight. They tried to buy transportation tickets, but the last bus already left, and the train won’t leave till midnight. 

  They both sat in front of the granny’s house lost on what to do, when they heard a loud noise from the air, they looked up to see a helicopter. 

    “I knew it, my friends must’ve been worried, they sent the helicopter to get me.” Micheal boasted, and Paulina looked up at the sky. 

  The helicopter looked for a safe place to land, and Michael held  Paulina who was disturbed by the breeze. 

  Immediately the helicopter landed, to their surprise, Sage came down from it. 

Paulina’s eyes widened in bewilderment, she freed herself from Michael and ran into Sage’s embrace. 
.
TO BE CONTINUED…

I tried your number, but it didn’t connect, so I confirmed from the principal if any students alighted in another stop, but confirmed it was false. What really happened?” He asked, and she embraced him.

  They arrived in the city and Sage called his driver to come pick them up, before glaring at Michael.

   “What’s your business? So far I’m closer to her than you, I’ve nothing to explain to you.” Sage blurted, and Micheal scoffed before rolling his eyes. 

    “Of course, everyone knows you are closer to her because you are always hovering around her like a lost puppy.” Micheal mocked, and Sage’s face twist in anger. 

    “What do you say? Lost what?” He shouted aiming at Micheal. 

   “Lost puppy.” Micheal repeated ready to fight him, but Paulina was quick to stand between them. 

   “That’s enough.” She said. 

   “Don’t you hear what that guy juts called me?” Sage shouted. 

    “That’s enough, Sage. Mike, this isn’t nice, you told me you don’t like it when I called your friends puppies, so why are you using it with my friend?” Paulina blurted. 

    “Because he won’t stick to his place, a friend shouldn’t be this clingy.” Micheal blurted. 

   “What really happened, Lina? What did you guys discussed?” Sage asked. 

   “We’ll talk about this later, Sage. Mike, take, call whoever to come pick you up.” Paulina said, and gave Micheal her phone. 

  “Why are you giving him your phone? Doesn’t he have his?” Sage blurted.   

   “Sage, please…” 

  Micheal called his secretary who promised to come pick him up immediately before returning Paulina’s phone to her.

    “What agreement?” Sage asked, and Paulina shook her head. 

   “It’s nothing serious, we should leave now your driver is here.” She said, and grabbed his hand dragging him away while Micheal glared at them in disgust till they entered the car.

 On their way, Paulina told Sage everything that happened, and he blamed himself for trusting Alicia. 

   “It’s not your fault, Sage.” 

    “No, it’s my fault. You trusted her because of me.” 

   “We both trusted her, and she is the bad one for breaking your trust.” 

     “And Mike? Why were you two together?”

    “He was the one who rescued me when I was locked up.”

   “I don’t know, he didn’t tell me.”

    “Sage, that’s not what I mean.”

    “Sage, I don’t like to see you worry about me.”

    “Why? Aren’t you my friend? If I’m distressed can’t you worry about me as well?”

    “But it makes you feel uncomfortable.”

    “Sage…”

   “You will find a cab from here. Stop the car.” He said, and the driver pulled over. 

    “Sage…” 

    “Take care.” He muttered, and looked away. 

    “Thanks for the ride, I’ll give you a call.” She muttered, and alighted from the car.

 She arrived home, and she was welcomed by a resounding slap immediately she opened the door. 

   She held her already burning cheek, and looked up at Ms. Grace. 

   “You lowly peasant!” Ms. Grace shouted, and slapped her other cheek. 

    “What did I do?” Paulina cried, counting few steps away from her. 

   “How dare you ask me that?” Ms. Grace shouted, and wanted to hit her again but chairman Stanford held her hand. 

    “Honey, at least let’s hear her out.”

   “What do you want to hear? What did she have to explain?” Ms. Grace shouted, trying to free herself from her husband’s grip. 

   “Paulina, go inside.” Chairman Stanford said. 

    “She is going no where.” Ms. Grace shouted, and the chairman let out a sigh. 

    “Grace, for God’s sake!” Chairman Stanford shouted. 

    “It’s either she leaves now, or I call father and tell him all about her.” She threatened, and went inside.

   “With me? How?”

   “Why did you hangout with local street people when the bus was about to leave?” 

    “Something happened…”

   “Whatever the case, someone took a picture of you with a man and sent it to Grace. The blackmailer thought you were adopted and they are trying to use it against us. You know how serious the case is now that the election is behind the corners.” He said, and Paulina looked at him confused. 

   “Just go, take the card and leave.” With that he told her the passcode and closed the door. 

   “Sir, sir, at least hear me out.” She shouted barging the door. “Where do he want me to go?” She cried. 

  She left the house later and boarded a taxi to the nearest bus station, she took her phone and Sage’s number was the only one saved there, but she didn’t want to call him. She think for a while, and created a TikTok account to search for Sophia. She found her account, and sent a message to Sophia. She waited for almost an hour but got no response so she intend to call Sage when Sophia message popped in. 

  She happily explained her situation to Sophia who asked for her location and promised to come pick her up.

   “Lina, what happened to you?” Sophia shouted, running into her embrace. 

    “Sophie, I don’t know who to call, I don’t even know anywhere around here.” She cried. 

    “It’s ok, Lina. Get in the car.” She said, with that they went to the car, and the driver drove off.

    “Good evening, ma’am.”  Paulina greeted.

    “Lina, you guys arrived.” Sophia’s father said, joining them. 

    “Good evening, sir.”

   “Good evening, dear. Oh, you look stressed. Sophia told us what happened, you’ve nothing to worry about anymore.” He said, and Paulina nodded in gratitude, wondering what Sophia told them.

    “Oh, gently, do you want to hurt her?” Sophia’s mother said. 

    “Be careful, Sophie.” Her father said. 

    “You two shouod go freshen up, I will get the table ready for dinner.” Her mother said. 

    “Thank you, mummy.” Sophia said, and grabbed Paulina’s hand, dragging her away. 

   “Be careful with the way you are dragging her.” Her mother shouted loud enough for them to hear.

The room is beautifully decorated all pink and a big BTS wallpaper on the wall. Sophia is their huge fan, and she tell Paulina about them a lot, so Paulina wasn’t surprised.

   “Oh, them…”

   “Well… about that… they were aware of the supreme class volunteer programe, so I simply told them what happened to you there.”

    “What happened?” Paulina asked. 

   “I told them what you tell me.” She muttered, scratching her hair. 
    
    “Everything?” Paulina shouted with widened eyes. 
   
    “Of course not, I told them that you missed your flight  because you had to use the toilet, and you don’t know how to explain the situation at home because you will be blamed for it, so you were  stranded outside. The point is, you missed your flight back.” Sophia said, rushing her words, and Paulina chuckled. 

   “Thanks, Sophie.”

   “Don’t mention.” Sophia said, and they heard a knock on the door. “Tell mummy I’ll be down in a jiffy.” Sophia shouted before whoever it was said anything. 

   “Yes, madam. Take your time, and don’t stress Lina out, let her at least eat something before you bleed her ears with your mouth.” They heard her mother’s voice said, and they both laughed. 

   “We will be down in a jiffy, mum.” Sophia shouted.

  They joined Sophia’s parents in the dinning hall after they both freshened up, there a special dinner was served. 

   “Wow, mummy, why is dinner so big, are we expecting anyone?” Sophia asked taking her seat. 

   “What are you saying? I don’t know what Lina might like, so I prepared different dishes, but it’s nothing much, It’s just the way we normally have dinner. Right, sweetheart?” Her mother said, and her father nodded. 
 
   They gestured Paulina to take the seat next to Sophia, and they started eating in silence until Sophia’s mother gestured her father to talk. 

    “Errmm… Lina, Sophie said you are in the supreme class, and you went to the volunteer.” Sophia’s  father asked, and Sophia looked at him, she couldn’t already tell that he wanted to interrogate Paulina to know her family worth. 

    “Yes, sir.”
     
    “I hope it wasn’t stressful?” Sophia’s mother asked. 
    
    “Not really, ma’am.” 
    
    “Your parents must be very influential for you to be in the supreme class.” Sophia’s father said, and his wife nodded along. 

    “Not re-…”. Paulina was saying, but Sophia cut in. 

    “…- daddy, Paulina’s family are close acquaintances with the Stanford’s.” Sophia said, and they all looked at her. 
    
    “The Stanford’s ?” The couple shouted, and Sophia’s mother cutlery fell off her hand. 
    
   “The Stanford’s of first group, in-laws with the Packards’, the former prime minister’s family? Goodness, Sophia you never tell us your friend is from such an influential family. Welcome to our humble home, dear.” Sophia’s mother said, and Paulina managed to smile before looking at Sophia who gave her a pleasing look. 

    “So, what do your parents do? We know the Stanford’s, who are your parents?” Sophia’s father asked, and Paulina looked at Sophia. 

   “Daddy, you can’t know her parents.” Sophia said, and they all looked at her. 

    “Why?” Her mother asked. 

   “Her parents are foreign business owners, she lost her father years ago, and her mother was involved in a tragic accident few months ago which brought her to California to stay with the Stanford’s, so the Stanford’s are her legal guardian now.” Sophia said, and everyone eyes widened in bewilderment, including Paulina - she wondered how long Sophia made up that biography for her. 

  “Oh, dear.” Sophia’s mother said in a sober tone. 

  “Mummy, can we eat now? Lina doesn’t feel comfortable with this conversation.” Sophia said, and Paulina looked at her again. 

  “I hope you enjoy dinner, sweetie.” Sophia’s mother said passing some food to Paulina’s plate.

  They talked about other things till they finished eating, and returned to their various room. 
*
  Paulina pulled Sophia by the collar when they entered the bedroom, and Sophia locked the door immediately. 

   “I’m so sorry, Lina, but that’s the only way my parents could accept you.” Sophia said. 
    
    “What do you mean by that?” 

    “My parents paid a fortune for me to attend holland high so I can associate with influential people for them to build connections. If I tell them I’m close friends with a special scholarship student, they would kick us out of the house together. I can accept you for whoever you are, but my parents won’t understand.” Sophia said rushing her words, and Paulina let out a sigh. 

   “I’m sorry for putting you in a tight spot.” 

  “Don’t be sorry, Lina. What matters is that everything is settled. Now tell me what happened?” Sophia said, and dragged her to seat on the bed. 
    
   “I already explained everything.” Paulina muttered.. 
    
   “Not in details. You only said you missed the flight because Alicia locked you up in the toilet, and the Stanford’s kicked you out. How did you get back? Why did they kicked you out? That Alicia is also a dem0n, I wish I could kick her a$$. I’ll make sure she hear a handful from Sage.” Sophia said, and Paulina laid flat on the bed. 
   “Tell me everything in details…” Sophia shouted, bouncing on her.

 Paulina and Sophia were fast asleep late in the morning, they spent all night chattering away so they slept late but a hard knock on the door jabbed them off from bed. 

  Sophia wobbled her feet to go open the door wondering why her parents were knocking so early which was unlike them. They normally leave her to come out whenever she wants. She let out a sigh, hoping it’s not to please Paulina again. 

  She opened the door to see her mother. 

   “Is Lina still asleep?” Her mother asked. 

   “Mummy, what?” Sophia grumbled. 

   “Chairman Stanford is in the living room.” Her mother whispered, and Sophia’s eyes widened in shock. 
*
 Paulina slept so well all night, she haven’t slept that soundly at her supposed father’s house, but going to the living room to see her father  made her realize that his house seems to be actually were she belonged now. 

   “Good morning, sir.” She and Sophia greeted when they got to the living room,, and chairman Stanford embraced her  immediately, surprising everyone. 

  “Paulina, I’m glad you are fine.” He muttered, patting her back gently.

   “Wilson.” The couples chorused. 
    
    “Mr., and Mrs. Wilson.” Mrs. Wilson added, and Mr. Wilson gave the chairman his card.

   “Lina here… and my daughter are close friends, they are like siblings, sisters to be precise. She is also like a daughter to us, so this is her house. She is welcome here at any time, right honey ?@ Mrs. Wilson said, and her husband nodded along. 

    “Of course, she is always welcomed here.” Mr. Wilson added. 

   “I sincerely appreciate. I wish there could be a way to express my gratitude.” Chairman Stanford said. 
    
   “Of course, there is always a way.” The couples chorused, and laughed again. 

  “Mention it, and it’s done.” Chairman Stanford said. 
    
   “There..-“. They both said, and looked at themselves.

   “…-Mr. Chairman, we’re not asking for too much. If you could just put in a word for us to have access to the coming parents- students dinner. That’s all.” She said, and her husband looked at her.

  “The grande dinner that holds in holland high every year. I heard the president is one of the honorable guests attending this year.”

  “But why request to attend that?” He asked

But if you could use your influence to put in some words for us, let my husband and I be an exemption. We promise to behave!” She said, and clapped her hand’s together feigning a pleading look. 

   “Is that what you want?” Chairman Stanford asked, and her eyes brightened. 

   “Yes!” She said. 

   “No!” Her husband shouted, and they all looked at him. 

   “Sweetheart, what are you saying? That’s what we want.” She said, pitching him discreetly. 
  
   He adjusted away from her side a little and composed himself.
  “Mr. Chairman, if you could make our shopping mall one of your suppliers district…” He said, and paused. “I would forever be grateful to you!” He shouted, and startling them. 

   “Yes, sir. We’d appreciate that as well.” His wife added laughing sarcastically. 

   “I really appreciate your hospitality, so that won’t be a problem.” Chairman Stanford said. 

   “Thanks a log, sir.” The couples chorused. 

   “Don’t mention. We’ll be on our way now.” Chairman Stanford said, and stood up. 

   “Yes, Mr. Chairman. But I’d appreciate if you could give us your business card, you already have mine, but we might just…”. Mr. Willson shuddered. 

   “Yes, I should.” Chairman Stanford said, and gave him his card. “Just give me a call, so I can fix an appointment for us to discuss better.”

   “Oh, of course, sir. I would do that immediately.” Mr. Wilson said. 
    
    “Mr. Chairman, I hope you didn’t forget our first request?” Mrs. Wilson said. 
     
   “What first request?” Mr. Wilson blurted, eyeing her. 

   “I’ll see to that as well, I’ll contact chairman Russ.” Chairman Stanford said. 

   “Oh, you will contact the school chairman? Thank you so much  sir.” Mrs. Wilson shouted, and the couples bowed. 

Chairman Stanford left later with Paulina after thanking the couples again. 

  The family giggled after they left, and Mr. Wilson started kissing chairman Stanford’s card. 

   “This is the key to our never ending success.” Mr. Wilson muttered in gratitude. 

   “We should frame the business card, and hang it up. Who could believe a day like this will come when we will receive a direct business card from the chairman of First Group, not to talk of having his audience.” Mrs. Wilson said. 

   “Mummy, hanging that means sharing the contact.” Sophia said. 
    
   “Oh, yeah, you are right. Sweetheart, we should keep this safe.” Mrs. Wilson said. 

    “What’s that your request for? A dinner party?” Mr. Wilson said. 

    “What are you saying? Attending that dinner is a pure connection. It’s a free pass to a gathering of conglomerates.” She said, and Mr. Wilson eyes widened in realization. 

   “Yes, you are right.” He shouted. 

   “Daddy, you don’t have to shout.” Sophia whined. 

    “Let him shout. This is the reason we sent you to holland high, our sacrifice yield after all.” Mrs. Wilson said, and breathe out in relief. 

   “Sophie, continue to be good to Lina, ok?” Mr. Wilson said. 

   “Yes, daddy. She is my best friend.” 

    “That’s good.” Mr. Wilson muttered. 

    “I need to book an appointment with a famous designer before the dinner. How does LY sounds?” Mrs. Wilson asked. 

    “Cool!” Sophia muttered, an winked. “Mummy, I’ll also get to attend, right?”

    “Of course. It’s a parent-students dinner.” Her mother said, and they both giggled. 

   “Must you design your outfit from LY? You tried it last time but failed to book an appointment.” Mr. Wilson said. 

   “That’s why I would do it earlier now till I get the appointment. She is the designer that designs chairman Russ’s wife outfit during the unveiling of their new company in Los Angeles last year.” Mrs. Wilson said.

 CHAIRMAN STANFORD’S MANSION ^**
^
  They went to the living room, and Paulina stood next to her father ready to get an earful from Ms. Grace. 

  Chairman Stanford showed her the message on Ms. Grace’s phone. In the message, an anonymous person threatened to post an article with her picture, a picture of when the thug held her hand in that rural area. The picture was properly edited that it looks like she was smiling. He threaten her to pay a certain amount, otherwise they will expose her identity as the adopted daughter of the Stanford’s family who is leading a carefree life. 

    “This isn’t true.” Paulina muttered, in tears. She already knew that it was plotted by Ava, but she doubted if they will believe her. 

   “Who cares about the truth? The election is behind the corners, and you are trying to ruin my reputation.” Ms. Grace shouted, and slapped her. 

    “Grace, that’s enough. I thought we discussed this last night, let’s hear Paulina out at least.” Chairman Stanford said. 

   “What does she has for say? Either it’s true or not, it won’t change anything.” Ms. Grace lashed. 

   “Even if you believe me or not, things like this will keep on happening.” Paulina shouted, and they both looked at her - that was the first time she will ever be that bold. She was also awed by her sudden confidence, but she realized that it either she speak up now or allow them trample over her because no one will speak up for her. 

    “What did you say? Did you just lashed at me?” Ms. Grace muttered in disbelief. 

    “It’s all Ava’s doing, even if I want to live quietly, she is always looking for a way to get rid of me. Either you believe me or not, she locked me up in a strange room weeks ago, and set me up in that trap as well. It’s all her doings, your daughter is the one who wants to ruin your election.” With that she ran to her room. 

    “That little…”. Ms. Grace grumbled within clenched teeth’s staring at her retreating back. 
*
  Paulina went to her room and broke down in tears. With everything that happened today, she realized that she would continue to have a difficult life if she doesn’t start speaking up for herself, because obviously her so-called father is scared of his wife. 

  She sobbed on her pillow missing her mother, and also wondering how her mother got involved with such people. She was in another world in her  thoughts when she heard a knock on the door. 

  She muttered a response and composed herself thinking it was the maid, but she was surprised when her father walked in - that was his first time in her room. 

    “Paulina, can you explain what you meant by everything was Ava’s doing?” He asked, and she glared at him. 
    
    “What will telling you change?” She lashed, and his eyes widened in shock. 

     “Paulina…”

   “The few weeks I spent here made me realize that you have no say, you can’t even fight for me when your wife was trampling all over me. Why do I’ve to live like this at my supposed father’s house?”
     
    “Paulina…”

   “I don’t care about whatever history you had with my mother, but now I could see clearly. You c0wardly abandoned my mum to suffer, and you brought me here so your wife could control me. I hate you, I don’t want to have a father like you. I never wish to have a father like you. I want to leave this house.” She cried, and wrapped herself on her bed. 

   Chairman Stanford stood there unable to utter a word, no matter how harsh her words might sound, they were all true. He abandoned Charlotte to be with Grace, and he still failed to protect his daughter from Grace. He left the room later without uttering a word, and Paulina cried herself to sleep. She needed someone to vent on, and her so-called father was the fit person, yet, he didn’t try to deny any of it, neither did he tried to comfort her. 
*
 Chairman Stanford and Ms. Grace didn’t summon Paulina for discuss anything again till the weekend fly by. Paulina didn’t leave her room either, she always instruct the maids to bring her food to her room because she wasn’t ready to face any of them - a father who can’t stand up for her, her stepmother who is more concerned about her political career, and her half-sister who is determined to get rid of her, regardless the consequences.
