 HOLLAND HIGH SCHOOL ^^^
^
 Paulina emerged the hallway heading to the elevator and the few students she passed by pointed fingers at her mumbling words to themselves. She didn’t pay attention to them until she saw some pictures on the floor. She picked one of it, and was startled to see the same picture her stepmother showed her. She instantly understood what all the stares were about, and she started picking up the pictures until she got to the elevator entrance, and saw a big poster on the wall with the picture boldly displayed and many hates comments. 

  She let out a sigh and wanted to rip it off, but then she was surrounded by many students. 

   👤”You piece of trash, leave out school.”
   👤”She is a cheap sIut”
   👤”She is a wh0re.” 

  The students shouted, throwing things at her and she tried to dodge it all until they stopped. 

 S he looked up later, and saw Ava and her friends standing in front of her. 

    “Shame on you, Lina. A cheap sIut like you can never be rebranded. You still retuned back to where you belonged.” Lia mocked. 

   “Your cover has been blown, Lina. I couldn’t believe I almost became friends with the likes of you.” Alicia blurted. 

   “Why? Must you take it this far?” Paulina managed to say, she was already torn to the core and felt so helpless. 

   “I want you gone, Lina. I’m going to dig out all your dirty acts fill you are gone completely. Out of this school, out of my house, and completely out of my sight!” Ava blurted. 

   👤”What? She lives at Ava’s house?”
   👤”What a shameless peasant.”
   👤 “She is such a parasite.” 
   👤”She is leaching off the Stanford’s.”

   The students mumbled, and Paulina looked around hoping someone will stand up for her, but there was no one. Everyone around was throwing shades, and glaring at her. 

 She closed her eyes as tears streamed down her face. She was tired of everything, and she just wished to disappear as Ava wanted. The students continue to insult her, and started throwing things at her and she closed her eyes to take it all, until they stopped, and everywhere became awkwardly silent. 

   “How dare you all do this to her?” She heard a familiar voice shouted, and she opened her eyes to see the MASS. “Lina, are you ok?” Michael asked worriedly, and embraced her. 

  Everyone around gasped, and she was equally surprised until she remembered their agreement, and she immediately made up her mind to take advantage of their agreement as much as she could - she have Michael, and no one can dare step on her if she was by his side - not any students in school, neither students in the supreme class, and not even Ava and her friends. 

   He released from the embrace later, and tuned to everyone angrily. 

   “I told you all that she is special to me, I warned you all against bullying her, so how dare you…”. He shouted, starling everyone, including his friends. 

   “What do you know about her to tag her as someone special do you? Look at this, are you aware that she missed her flight to hang out with her fellow loser?” Ava blurted, and threw the pictures at him while everyone gasped. 

   “This? Is this all you got?” He mocked. 

   “Mike, that girl is a piece of trash, why are you defending her?” Lia shouted. 

   “Because I was with her that day.” He said, and everyone started mumbling words. “We missed the flight because we were together. If you don’t believe me, you can confirm anyhow you can. Lina and I missed the fight, because we were together that day.” He said, and everyone gasped, including his friends. 

    “Ava, you still have to explain how you got these  pictures and how your mother almost got blackmailed by it. I was lured out to town by Alicia, and almost got attacked by some thugs, but thankfully I was able to escape. You will have to explain how you got those pictures, and the story you attached to it because Mike and I were together after the incident, we both missed our flight because we were together. If this is your way to get rid of me, then you still have a long way to go because I’m going nowhere, and mind you, if you keep this on, I would careless about the contract I signed, neither your mother election. You know what I meant by that, remember - I’ve got nothing to lose.” Paulina blurted, to everyone surprise. 
     
   “How dare you…”. Ava shuddered. 

   “Let’s go, Mikey.” Paulina said, grabbing Micheal’s hand, and everyone gasped, including his friends.

  “Yes, yes, that was a nice acting you both pulled off, I’m sure Ava is sulking somewhere right now. But please… Miss scholar, no one is looking anymore, could you kindly retreat, and stop following us?” Austin said, and Paulina let out a sigh in realization. She was carried away by the act, that she followed them without thinking. 

   “Yeah… I should be on my way now.” Paulina muttered, and intended to leave but Michael dragged her back. 

    “She is coming with us.” Michael said. 
    
   “She is what?” Austin and Stanley shouted, and they all looked at him. 
   
    “You can’t be serious, man.” Austin blurted.

     “Seriously?” Austin shouted.

    “What does that mean?” Micheal asked, and they all looked at Stanley.

    “I second him.” Austin added.

   “And if I choose her?” Michael asked back. 

   “Then you leave with her!” Austin blurted. 

   “Seriously?” Michael laughed. 

   “Fine, she can come with us.” Stanley said, and Austin glared at him.

  Sage arrived late at school and summoned Alicia to the cafeteria rooftop. She joined him almost immediately, in excitement, wondering what he wants to discuss with her. 

  “Sage, why do you want to meet here?” She asked, adjusting her hair. 

    “Why did you do it?” He muttered, and she let out a sigh as she instantly understood him. 

    “What are you talking about?” She asked, feigning ignorance. 

   “We both know you are feigning ignorance, I’m not buying that.”

   “I don’t know what you are talking about, so it’s either you make me understand, or I return to class.” She blurted.

    “What exactly do I not know? Make it clear!” He shouted, starling her.

    “What nonsense are you spouting?”

That’s all your doings, so don’t try to play them victim complex with me.” He said, and she laughed sarcastically. 

   “Yes, I asked her to escort me to the restroom , and she thought I lured her out when my bag got stolen, we decide to return after 10 minutes but I didn’t see her, until the bus left.”

    “So, who locked her in the restroom?”

   “What nonsense are you blabbering on? My friends and I were part of the first people to board  the bus, so if she was really attacked by the thugs and went through all that, what time did she return to the restroom to get locked up there? We should’ve all be gone by then, expect her and Mike who missed the flight.”

    “What?” He muttered.

   “Getting along with them isn’t that bad either.” He said and she nodded.

    “But that’s not enough reason for you to end my call.”

    “Then you should’ve just done that.”

I could do the same for you as friend, not just for you, but Sophie as well. Because we are all friends, and friends should be there for each other.”

    “Friends? Of course we’re friends… just as we’re friends with Sophie. But friends should also listen to each other, and be on the same boat.” He said, and left. 

    “Sage.” She wanted to call, but controlled herself from calling him. He is over protective of her, and setting boundaries to their friendship was the only way for her to accomplish her mission with Micheal. “I’m sorry, Sage. I hope you will understand with time. I really have to survive, and this the only way I can.” She muttered, and looked at his card, remembering their first meeting. “He has always been good to me.” She thought, and  tears steamed down her cheeks.

   “Too bad… I recently employed a new chef. She cooks so well like a chef who once served here years ago, even before you were born.     “I really careless about those below me, that’s the point. No matter how significant they might think they were, I careless about their existence. And I believe we are the same.”

    “Oh… but since we never see on same page, you try to change that.” She said, and he looked at her. 

    “I don’t understand.”

    “You are growing too fond of a special scholar student, and I suddenly got interested in her.” She said, and he glared at her. 

     “Paulina did nothing wrong.” He blurted, trying to hide his fear. 

     “Paulina Andrew’s, 18-year-old special scholar. Her hubby is swimming, and she recently relocated from the countryside, but how come the name of  the village seems awfully familiar, although I can’t remember it. I must’ve had a cheesy history there somehow.” She said, and Micheal stood up immediately. 

    “Don’t you dare hurt, Paulina.” He lashed, and she smiled before taking a bite from her meal. 

     “Is there something I missed?” She asked, and chuckled.  

    “Paulina did nothing wrong, I’m the one hovering around her.”

    “Oh… so you are the one who broke your promise to me?” She shouted, and dropped her cutleries glaring at him. 

    “I didn’t forget my promise, I’m not looking at anything, and there is nothing special between Paulina and I. I had enough of you hurting those who are close to me.” He muttered. 

    “No, I never did such. I only made sure that you are surrounded with the right people, but I’m more interested now that you could speak at me to protect her. She must be one of the garbage you left behind.”

    “Don’t ever lay a finger on Paulina.” He muttered, and turned to leave. 

    “Is that a threat? If you don’t want to do it the easy way by staying away from that peasant, then I would do it my way.” She threatened, and he left.

   “Paul, why are you here so early?” She asked: he went to embrace her.

Her grandfather shot himself immediately, and her father was apprehended.

Thank you for raising me well dad, you raised me too well that I don’t how to abandon those I love for mh selfish interest. I know I don’t say this often, but you the person I cherish the most, you and grandpa. And grandpa is not even here anymore…” she cried.

   “No!” She blurted.

A patient opened moved her fingers gently, and opened her eyes. Her vision was blurry, but she could see a man in the room, although she couldn’t get a proper view of him.

   The doctor blabbed on, telling her about her condition, but she wasn’t paying any attention to him, as she kept wondering how long she has been there. 

    “Do you’ve any question?” The doctor asked.

    “Four weeks, you’ve been in the ICU for four weeks.”
    
     “What? Four weeks? Did anyone aaid me a visit?” She asked. 

     “No one did. Your cellphone damaged from the accident, so we were unable to reach anyone.”

     “So Paul didn’t know I was here?” She grumbled. 

    “But you don’t have to worry about anything, you are better now, and your baby is in good shape as well. You can use the hospital line to make a call if you want to.”

    “My baby? What baby?”

     “Oh… we ran some tests on you that confirmed that you were pregnant, you are six weeks pregnant now.”

     “What? How can I be pregnant?“ She grumbled, and the doctors left later.

   📞*Paul, Paul.* She shouted, and dropped the call.

 She heard news later that her father have been prosecuted, and sentenced to prison - he was defended by public defenders. Her jaw dropped upon hearing the news, which only means that Don couldn’t hire professional lawyers for him, and it’s all caused by her accident.

She refused to believe that he ended the call  because he heard her voice, so she put herself together with determination to get better, and pay him a visit, and tell him about the baby. She was determined to get rid of the baby, but she decide to consult him first before making such decisions. 
  
 She sold out her designer wristwatch to clear the hospital bills after she was discharged, and went to the detention center first, to see her father who was brought out to her. 

    “Daddy.” She called. 

    “Charlotte, are you ok? Are you hurt in anyways?”

    “Daddy, I heard that you got a life sentence. I’m so sorry, daddy… it’s all my fault.” She cried. 

    “Don’t say that. I’m glad to see you are doing fine. I heard about the accident. I was so worried.m, I’m so sorry I couldn’t do anything for you. I’m really sorry, my child.”

    “Daddy, it’s not your fault. I just got discharged, and I heard about your sentence. I’m going do as you say, I’m going to get the money and take some to Don, we will find a lawyer to reopen your case.” She said, and her father his head. 

    “Don’t! Don was the traitor.” He said, and Charlotte looked confused. 

    “What do you mean?”

    “You can’t trust anyone, Charlotte. Don was the traitor who sold us out to some political man, and sent all those evidence to the prosecutor. He came here and confessed to me himself. Forget about everything, Charlotte, and live your own life.”

    “How can I live you are locked up here and I can’t trust anyone.” She muttered, sniffing in. 

    “There is nothing much I can do, I’m sorry. You’ve to take care of yourself now.” 
*
  She left the detention center later, and board a taxi straight to Paul’s house. Even though there is no one she can trust, she could at least trust him, and hear his reason for not checking up on her because is sure he must’ve a good reason for it. 

 The cab she entered broke down in the middle of the road, so she left and went to a convenience store to buy a drink before continuing on her way, there she saw a news report on Paul and Grace’s wedding. The can drink in her hand fell down immediately, as she stare at the TV devastated. 

  She left there later, and boarded a taxi to his house, the securities didn’t let out through, but a car drove in at the moment, and Grace alighted from the car. 

    “Charlotte, I thought you are dead.” Grace said, and Charlotte’s eyes widened. 

    “You, you are not married to Paul, right? The news was false, right?” She shuddered. 

     “You only choose to believe what you want to believe. Remember I told you, Charlotte. I want Paul, and I’ll get him even if he is in any cheesy relax. We are married, our parents arranged it. I’m sorry about your family, I’ve always knew that something was wrong with that family of yours. But I couldn’t just figure it out.” Grace teased, and tears flowed freely from Charlotte’a face. Even though her father asked her not to trust anyone, she believed she could trust him. 

    “Grace, father is wa…-“. They heard Paul’s voice approaching them, but he stopped immediately he saw Charlotte. 

    “Paul…”. She shuddered. 

    “Security, escort her out. Paul, let’s go, father is waiting.” Grace aaid, and grabbed Paul’s hand, leading him away. 

     “Paul! You’re the worst!” Charlotte shouted as she security escort her out, but Paul didn’t even look back. 

   She left devastated and lonely, she has always lived like a princess all her life, starting a new life where she could trust no one was like a torture to her, so she decided to end it all. There was nowhere she could go, all their prosperities have already been seized

    “What’s there to live for?” She thought, and made the huge decision. “I’m sorry, mum, I’m sorry, dad.” She muttered, looking at her bracelet. 
*
 She but stood by a bridge watching the ocean, she had always loved swimming because she felt whole whenever she was in the water, she let in a deep breath, and she jumped into the water.

        <<<<BACK TO PRESENT>>>>
^
 Sage and Sophia went to the regular students cafeteria together after Paulina sent them a message that she wouldn’t be having lunch. 

 Sage sighed into his meal, but didn’t have any appetite to take a bite as the thought of what Paulina might be doing disturbed him. At times he wondered why he was that affected by her. He was attracted to her at Lia’s party because she was just his type, a had a soft spot for girls who looked innocent, and she had it all. After everything that happened at the party that night, he realized that she wasn’t just innocent, but also helpless so he felt the urge to protect her, and transferred immediately. The length of what he could do for her scared him a lot that he started doubting his feelings. He was never that way either the few girls he had dated in the past, and he doesn’t even have the courage to tell Paulina his true feelings from fear of losing her; she is going through a lot already, so bothering her with his feelings would only make her uncomfortable around him. 

   “Sage, why aren’t you eating? It’s getting cold.” Sophia said, after taking a bite from his meal. 

    “I’ve no appetite, we shouod leave when you are done.” He muttered. 

     “You like Lina, don’t you?” She muttered, and he looked at her. 

   “What do you mean? Lina is our friend, but yes, you are right. I like her, and it’s beyond friendship.”

    “I knew it.”

    “I never tried to hide it from the start.”

     “I didn’t expect you to admit to it upfront.”

     “I’ve no reason to deny it.” 
     
     “Why didn’t you tell her?”

     “I don’t want her to feel uncomfortable. She is going through a lot, and I’m sure she is passing through something that she doesn’t want to share. Telling her about my feelings will be bothersome for her.”

    “So will you cover it with mask of friendship forever?”

    “Not forever. Just until she feels more comfortable to share everything, until I think the time is right, just until… I’m sure she will accept my feelings.” 

     “Actually, I think Mike has feelings for Lina.”

    “What nonsense are you spouting? Don’t tell me you trust that j£rk after everything he had done?”  

    “Well… I don’t trust him, but I’ve no resentment for him either.”

    “So what?” He blurted, dropping his cutleries in anger. 

    “Although I vouch for Mike, like who wouldn’t? He is a young director, and also the heir to Russ Group, but you are my friend and I trust that Lina will be in good hands with you.” She said, and he nodded. 

   “I hope Lina feels the same way.” He muttered, and Sophia smiled. 

   “You should still tell her, Sage, tell her before it’s too late.” 

    “You think I should tell her?”

     “Yes. I just have a feeling that Mike is up to something, even though I’m rooting for him, I would be hurt if she chose him over you.” 

    “I would think about it. But do you think Lina can really go out with Mike?” 

     “We could never tell, she is full of surprises. Who knows a that she could pull out those dramas with Mike.” 

     “You’re right, I don’t just understand her at times.”
      
   “I hope she accepts your feelings, Sage. I hope things work out for the both of you, so she can open up fully to you.”

    “I hope so too. Thanks, Sophie. You are true friend.”

    “If things work out with Lina, you should set me up with one of your friends.” She said, he chuckled. 

    “I thought things are going well with you and Miguel?”
    
    “What Miguel? Forget about that loser, and set me up with better people.” She blurted, and he laughed. “Hey, what’s funny?” She shouted. 

   “Nothing, nothing.” He grumbled, coughing slightly. 

   “Better.” She muttered, and emptied her glass of water.

 Micheal told his friends about his fake relationship with Paulina, and as expected they were all against it. 

    “What? You must be out of your mind, Mike.” Austin shouted. 

     “Mike, this doesn’t make sense.” Stanley said.

     “Man, I’m totally against this.” Austin blurted. 

    “Me too!” Stanley added, and they all looked at Steven who looked away.

    “Who asked for our opinion?” Stanley added.

I have no reason to be here if that wasn’t the case.”  Paulina blurted, and they all lookEd  at her in shock. 

     “What? What made you think you can talk to us?” Austin shouted. 

     “If I’m to answer that, I would have do talk to you again, will that be fine?” She said, and Steven chuckled; they all looked at him and he instantly composed himself. 

    “What? Did she just talk back at us?” Austin shouted. 

      “You. To be precise.” Micheal teased, and they all glared at him. 
     
     “We’re late for class. Let’s go.” Paulina said, and Michael stood up. 

     “Let’s go, guys.” Michael said, and they all stood up as well.

     “It’s not over yet, Mike.” Stanley grumbled, and Michael nodded.

  After the day class was over, Paulina hurried out of the class first, and met Sage by the elevator at the ground floor. 

    “Hey, that was quick.” He said, and held her hand. 

  She hurried out of class to be able to tell him about her relationship with Micheal before he made any move. It might be a fake relationship, but she can’t be opened about it to Sage because she is certain that he will be totally against it, and she isn’t sure if she is doing the right thing as well. 

    “Sage, there is something I’ve to tell you.” She muttered. 

     “I also have something to tell you, but not here. I’ve to hurry home now because my grandfather is around, but let’s meet later.”

     “Later? When?”

     “Will you be free for dinner? We Gouda have dinner together and watch a movie.”

     “We are going out? Is Sophie coming along?”

     “Nah… you said you wanted to tell me something. We could do it then.”

    “Sage, bu…-“. She was saying when his phone rang. 

   📞*Hello, dad, I’m on my way.*. With that he dropped the call. 

     “I wish I could give you a ride, but I’m in a hurry. See you later.” He waved, and hurried away. 

     “I want to tell you about Mike.” She grumbled. 

     “What’s it about me?” Micheal asked from behind, startling her.

     “I’m very clingy, you should know that.” He whispered in her ears. 

  His friends stood behind looking at them in disgust.

     “I would answer your question one at a time, Charlotte, I’ll give you an answer in as much as you can handle it.”

      “Did anything happen to my daughter?” She asked, already alarmed. 

    “No, I checked and she was doing just fine.” 

     “I’ve to go home, Louis, my daughter must be worried about me. I’ve never spent a night out before.” 

     “A night? Who said you’ve been here for a night?” He teased, and she looked at him. 

     “How king have I been here.”

     “Almost a month.”

     “What?!”

     “You shouldn’t shout, you are still recovering.”

     “Louis, what really happened to me?” She cried. 

    “I believe you can answer that better! You would be fine after you recover, you are just experiencing that little memory loss of that day event because it must’ve been a really traumatic event, but you will remember it all after you recover. So just be calm.”

    “How do you expect me to be calm? I’ve no idea why I’m here and how my daughter is doing. How do you expect me to be calm, Louis?” She cried, and he embraced her. 

     “Your daughter is doing fine. I’m sure someone is looking after her. That’s all I can tell you now till you fully recover.” 

    “Who is looking after her?”

    “Charlotte, please! You’ve to complete your treatment first.”

   “I want to go home, Louis, I want to go home to complete my treatment.”

    “No, you can’t do that.”

   “Why can’t I?” 

    “Because it might be dangerous! Not for you, but for your daughter.”

    “What do you mean by that? What aren’t you telling me, Louis?” She asked, gabbing him by the collar. 

    “Because you will act worse than this. I can’t tell you anything now until you get better. I’m leaving for now, Joy (the nurse) will look after you, I’ll be back by tomorrow.” He said, and peck her forehead before leaving. 

    “What are you not telling me, Louis?” She shouted, but he ignored her.

Sage is a good friend, and she doesn’t want her selfish decision to get rid of Ava come between their friendship. 

 He sent her another messages later to inform her that she was coming to pick her up so she hurried out of bed to go freshen up.

 They had a pack of pizza and soda before going to watch their movie.

   IN AN UNKNOWN PLACE^^^
^
  Charlotte think of a way to leave the place, Louis wasn’t willing to tell her anything, and she can’t trust him completely even if he was her friend. Paul and Grace betrayed her, so she can’t vouch that he wouldn’t do the same. He has been away for years, and suddenly returned, and knew everything about her made her even more scaled. She removed the IV drip being passed to her, and wobbled on her feet out. 

    “Ma’am, I’m sorry, but you can’t move around yet.” Joy said, rushing to her. 

    “Louis told me to get some exercise, even if it’s just walking for a bit.” She lied. 

    “But you aren’t fit for an exercise yet. Your surgical stitches are still fresh, and could open if you stress yourself.”

     “I won’t, my body felt stiff all of a sudden, so I think I need to walk  a little.”

     “Ma’am, I’m sorry, but you can’t.”

     “Even if my body felt glued to the bed?” She blurted. 
     
      “I’m sorry… alright, you can but I’ll have to install back the drip, you shouldn’t have removed it. Afterwards, we could stroll around for a bit.”

     “Alright, thanks.” 
*
  The nurse installed back the drip, and trailed behind her as they walk around together, while Charlotte started thinking of a way to be separated from her. 

    “I’m feeling thirsty, can I get some water?” She asked. 

     “Of course, I’ll escort you back to get you water.” Joy said. 

    “I’m too weak and thirsty, I’ll just sit here and wait for you.” She said, and sat down on a seat by the hallway. 

     “Ok, I’ll be right back.” Joy said, and hurried away. 

    Charlotte removed the drip, and tried to run but felt too weak, but she strained herself to leave before Joy retuned. The house was big and kinda confusing. 

  She found her was out, and was lucky to find an empty taxi so she stopped it immediately, and entered while the taxi driver drove off. 

    “Where to, ma’am?” He asked when they were already few miles away. 

    “I… I…”. She shuddered, and started feeling pains from the surgical stitches. 

    “Ma’am, are you ok?” He asked. 

    “I…”. She shuddered, and the driver stopped the car immediately. 

     “Are you alright?” He asked worriedly, and she felt too weak to open her mouth. 

  The driver went to her seat to look at her, and started to panic. 

   “Goodness, what’s this? I should call an ambulance.” He said, and went to get his onine when a call pulled over behind him. 

    “Charlotte.” Louis shouted, and hurried out of his car. 
    
     “Sir, do you know her?” The taxi driver asked. 

     “Yes, she is my patient.” He said, and showed the taxi driver his business card. 

    “Oh, please take her.”

    “Charlotte.” He called, but she already passed out.

   <<<<<<YEARS AGO>>>>>>>
^
  Charlotte opened her eyes, and everywhere was blurry for a while, she was forced to close it back and opened it later, and it was a little clearer. 

    “Oh, my. You are awake, Paulina.” An aged woman said, rushing towards her. 
     
    “Who are you? And why am I here?” She asked in fear, sitting up from the bed. 
    
     “Don’t be scared of me.” The aged woman said, and tears flowed freely from her face. 

    Charlotte looked at the woman who was in tears, and felt the urge to comfort her. She held back for a moment, but she couldn’t bear to watch the woman cry, so she embraced her. 

After the woman calmed down, she told her how she saved her from the ocean, which reminded her of her long lost daughter, Paulina, who drown the ocean, and was never found. 

    “I’ve been waiting here all these years for my daughter to return.” The granny cried, and Charlotte embraced her. 

    “She is back granny, from now henceforth, I’ll be your daughter and we could both live here together while we wait for Paulina.” Charlotte said, and the granny looked at her. 

    “My daughter.” The granny cried, and embraced her. 

   Charlotte made up her mind at that moment to start a new life there with her daughter. Ending a precious life that someone was yearning for was a really careless decision, and she made up her mind to live the best life she could with the old woman, and raise her child the best way she can leaving everything behind - her father, and Paul.

You are the best person I’ve met so far that why I don’t want you to get hurt because of me, I don’t want to hurt your feelings either because I know that they are genuine.” She muttered, fighting back tears and he embraced her. 

    “It’s fine, Lina. I’m also aware, I knew that you weren’t completely honest with me, and I’m willing to wait till you feel comfortable to share everything without being a burden.” He said, patting her back gently. 

   “Thank you so much, Sage. I must have saved a nation in my past life to be blessed with a good friend like you.” She said, and he chuckled. 

   “You sure did.” He said, and they both laughed. 
*
  He went to the cafeteria later to get them snacks, and they sat on the rooftop chattering away till school dismissed. 

 They both went to parking lot together later, on their ro his car, a man intentionally bumped into her. 

   “Oh, I’m sorry, sir. Are you ok?” She asked, worriedly, and the man looked up at her —he looked so unkept with unshaved breeds. 

    “Lina, let’s go.” Sage said, and dragged her away. “You shouldn’t talk to strangers like that.” He scolded. 

    “I bumped into him, and he doesn’t look scary at all.”

   “You say? He looks like a homeless man, I wonder how he entered the school premises. They need to tighten the security.”

     “Come on, don’t be that harsh. He looks pitiful to me, he must be related to a student here.”

     “The school probably got another scholarship student, because I can’t even imagine.” He muttered, and she glared at him. 

     “That’s enough, Sage. I’m on scholarship too.” She blurted. 

     “I’m sorry, ok. But the man really need to wash up.”

     “There you go again after saying sorry.” She muttered, and he laughed before driving off. 
*
 He drove her to the nearest bus station as she requested before he drove off. 

  She wanted to stop a taxi when she saw Steven at the bus station. 

     “What’s be doing here?” She thought, and went to him. “Hey, Steven.” She said, wondering if they were close enough to exchange greetings in public. 

     “You still owe me.” He muttered, and she looked at him. 

     “Oh, yeah… your pendant, I forgot to bring it again.”
  
     “Your bus card.” He said, and stretched his hands to her. 

     “What bus card?”
     
     “Card, to board the bus.”

     “You eBay to take the bus? Aren’t you supposed to be an elite? Do elites take public bus?” She asked, seemingly shocked. 

     “I’ve search through my bag, but I think I left my wallet at school. So help me with your card, or get lost.” He blurted, and she scoffed. 

     “Being so rude when you need help is totally old-fashioned. And meanwhile, I don’t have a bus card either. I take taxis, with my card.”

    “Good! We can get a bus card with that.” He said, and went to the station office to get the card. 

    They got two cards, and they both boarded the next bus together. There was only a seat left, and Steven hurried to sit there. 

    “So immature.” She muttered, and scoffed, standing next to him. 

    The bus arrived at his station, and he stood up to get off the bus when his bag fell off his hand and she scoffed as he picked it up and hurried out of the bus, but something fell off from it. 

    “You dropped something.”  She shouted, and picked it up, surprised to see it’s a coupon from a noodles restaurant. “What? He keeps coupon as well?” She thought, and kept it in her bag. 

  She alighted at the next bus station, and board a taxi to the noddles restaurant. 
*
  She got to the restaurant that’s in rural area, she looked around wondering how Steven found the restaurant in a remote area.  

    “Their noodles must be good.” She thought. 

  She went in, and was surprised to see Steven who was obviously startled after seeing her. 

     “Hey, who knew we could meet here.? What a small world.” 

    “Steven, we got an order what are you waiting for?” A man shouted from behind, and Paulina eyes widened, wondering what was going on. 

   “Oh, we have a customer. Welcome, miss. I’ll attend to the customer, just go deliver the orders.” The man said, and patted Steven shoulder. 

  Steven left, and Paulina was directed to a table still in shock. 

    “Miss, our today’s special is spicy noodles, but you can check the menu to place your order.” The waiter said. 

   “Is he a delivery guy?” She asked, because she couldn’t take her mind off it. 

   “Delivery… oh, you mean, Steven? Yes, he has been working here for quite a while now. He doesn’t take order, he only deliver.”

   “Oh… I see. I’ll go for the special.” She muttered in disbelief.

    CHAIRMAN GREENWOOD’S MANSION ^^^
^
  Sage was trying to work on his assignments in his study, but he couldn’t concentrate, so he went to the dinning hall to do it there. 

 He was still finding it difficult to concentrate when his mother joined him. 

   “Are you troubled?” She asked, and he chuckled before looking at her, she has a way of finding out anything going on in his life, even if he didn’t share. 

    “Mum, you are coming home early these days.” He muttered, trying to avoid her question. 

    “Why? Does it bother you that your nosy mother is now coming home early?” She asked, rubbing his hair. 
     
     “Kind of.” He muttered, and they both chuckled. 

     “Are you finding it difficult to concentrate?” She asked, taking the seat next to him. 

      “How can you tell?”

     “Because it’s your habit. You only come out to work on your assignments whenever you are finding it difficult to concentrate. You want to be distracted from your thoughts.” 

      “You know me too well, mum.”

      “Of course, because you are my son.” She said, patting his back. 

      “Does it only work if we are blood related?” He muttered, and his mother looked at him. 

      “Are you trying to understand a girl?” She asked, and his eyes widened in shock. 

      “Mum, how did you know?”

     “I’m your mother, and also a judge. I could tell straight ahead that you are thinking about a girl. Is it, Lina?”

    “Mum, I can’t just understand her.”

     “Oh… dear. It feels like yesterday when you were just born, but look at you now troubled about a girl. Oh… my son has really grown.” She teased, playing with his cheeks and he smiled. 

     “Mum, you and dad also fell in love in high school.” 

     “Mum, who fell first, you or dad?” He asked, and his mother almost shook on her saliva. 

    “That’s so random.” She muttered, and he chuckled. 

    “I’m just curious, mum.” He said, batting his lashes cutely. 

    “Well… if you act this cutely what can I do? You look so adorable.” She said, and he smiled. “I believed your dad fell first but as you know, he is shy, so I confessed to him first. When I confessed, he brought out a couple ring and proposed to me at that moment.” She said, smiling broadly like she could remember the moment. 

   Sage chuckled and slipped from his glass of water wishing his case would be as easy as his parents. 

    “Why was dad shy?” He asked, and his mother  smiled. 

    “I believe he was scared of father. Everyone at school was scared of me because of my father. He is quite intimidating, you know.” She said, and he nodded. 

    “I would have imagined.” He muttered. 

    “Sage, your father and I might have been couples since highschool, but we had our own struggles as well. Our relationship had a lot of up and downs, we broke up severally, and there was a point when I thought we could never be together again, and here we are. But that might not be the same with you, son.” She said, and he looked at her. 

    “I like Lina.” He managed to say. 

    “I know. I believe even your father and your sister know that too.”

     “But she is very discreet about everything. I want to understand her, but I can’t. I feel it’s unfair for her to keep things from me, she should feel free to share her burdens so we can find a solution together.” He said, and his mother chuckled. 

    “Did you tell her?”

    “What?”
     
     “About your feelings.”

    “I did, but the timing wasn’t right, so I couldn’t get a response, but I bet that she would reject me, not because she doesn’t feel the same, I believe its not protect me from whatever she is hiding.”  

     “Don’t push too hard, Sage. Don’t try to find out anything if she doesn’t feel comfortable to share it. Just stay by her side as a friend. Who  knows, she might not be the girl.” She said, and he looked at her. 

    “I think it can only be Lina, mum.”

    “Oh, my. You got a lot of courage, I wish your father was like you when he was your age.” She teased, and he managed to chuckle. 

   “Mum, are you making fun of my feelings?” He whined. 

     “Of course not, son. But don’t make her feel uncomfortable. I had a friend in highschool, he was very protective of me, and he loves inferring to everything I do. He felt obligated to know everything about me which made me uncomfortable. I avoided him even as a friend, because he was just not my type of friend. You should understand when your friend is trying to do something’s alone, friends don’t always have to rely on each other.”

    “You think I might be making her uncomfortable?”

    “If you keep interfering, then you can end up losing your friendship.”

     “I’ll keep that I mind and he more careful, mum.” He muttered, and she patted his back. 

     “Oh, my son is all grown up.” 

     “Mum, you are back. I did better in my English test.” Clara shouted, approaching them. 

     “Is that so? Let me see.” Her mother said, but Sage was quick to collect the report. 

     “What? 7 out of 20!” Sage shouted. 

     “You scored 7?” Her mother repeated. 

      “Yes, I meant that I did better than last time.” She grumbled. 

      “Oh, that’s my baby. This prove that you would gradually improve.” Her mother said patting her hair, and she grinned broadly. 

  Sage shook his head, and let out a sigh before taking his leave.

  CHAIRMAN RUSS’S MANSION^^^
^
  Micheal was trying to work in his study, but he couldn’t seem to concentrate. He has been thinking of the best way to get rid of Ava, but nothing came to his head. If it were other students at school, it would’ve been easy to get rid of them, but Ava is a Stanford, not just from an influential conglomerate family, she was also from a powerful political family. His father has high respect for the Packard’s, and he has also been trying to be business partners with the Stanford’s so getting rid of Ava would mean crossing a lot of line. 

  He made that agreement with Paulina because she was pitiful, and he wanted to protect her from Ava, but no it seems like he really needs to get rid of Ava for real. 

 He was still trying to figure out a plan when his secretary called his attention to the main dinning hall. 

  He let out a sigh before going to freshen up, wondering what his parents wanted to discuss again. 
*
  He got to the dining hall, and took his seat while his parents engaged on a conversation. 

   “You mean chairman Stanford asked for a favor for an extra invite to the next big dinner?” Mrs. Russ said in surprise. 

   “Yes, he said he wanted to gift it to a regular student’s parent.” Mr. Russ replied. 

   “Surprising! Who could have thought chairman Stanford could make such request.” Mrs. Russ said. 

   “This is actually the first time he would call me, so it was like an honor. This could be our chance to win his favour.” Mr. Russ replied. 

   “Say that again, sweetheart. Micheal, I thought you were close to their daughter.” Mrs. Russ said. 

    “No!” He muttered. 

    “No?” Mrs. Russ repeated. 

    “We used to be close before she went to England.” He muttered. 

     “Wow. You even know the details. Chairman Stanford is a strict businessman, but I heard he has a soft spot for his family, most especially his daughter. He even joined in his wife campaign last week despite his busy schedule. What a happy family.” Mr. Russ said, and Micheal exhaled heavily. 

    “Mike, isn’t she one of the students that was reported to the disciplinary board?” Russ asked.  

   “That’s old news. Mike, you should be closer to her. Put all misunderstandings behind, and ensure you win her favour before the next parent-student dinner. We should play all our cards, and win the Stanford’s over.” 

   “I heard that he doesn’t discuss business outside the company’s wall.” Mrs. Russ said. 

    “Yeah… he has so many policies.” Mr. Russ muttered, and Micheal let out a sigh. 

   “Why do you keep sighing like a sick dog?” Mrs. Russ blurted. 

    “Seems like you really don’t get along with the Stanford’s daughter.” Mr. Russ teased. 

    “Micheal, I heard about your new girlfriend.” Mrs. Russ said, and he looked at her. 

   “Is anything wrong with the girl?” Mr. Russ asked. 

    “Nothing you might be interested in, expect from the part that never get along with our dear Stanford daughter.” Mrs. Russ said, and Micheal made a fist. 

    “Breakup with her.” Mr. Russ blurted, and Micheal looked at him. “You shouldn’t be with any  complicated person, I ding care about who you date, but if whoever will affect our anticipated relationship with the Stanford, then you should know better. You are a businessman, you shouldn’t take such risk.”

    “The news is false, we aren’t together.”

    “So there is really a girl.” Mrs. Russ muttered, with an evil smirk. 

    “Then stop being friends with her, avoid any connection between the both of you.“ 

    “Ok, dad.” Micheal muttered, playing with his food. 

    “Did we get a new chef?” Mr. Russ asked later, and his wife smirked. 

    “Why? Does she remind you of our old chef?” She asked back, and Mr. Russ stopped eating to look at her. “Enjoy your meal, sweetheart. Good food shouldn’t waste.” She muttered, and continued eating.

    CHAIRMAN STANFORD’S MANSION^^^
^ 
  Ava tossed and turned on her bed trying to gather her thoughts together. She has been thinking of the best way to get rid of Paulina but no idea came to her head. Her friends were also annoying, they have all been chatting together on thier group chat like nothing conspired between them earlier. She got really angry, and left the group chat, clearing off all their messages before she dropped her phone. 

 A maid knocked on her door later to disrupt her from her thoughts, rendering her father’s message to her - he requested to see her in his living room, and she stormed out of the room, wondering what he wanted to discuss with her. 

 She got to the living room, and he gestured her to take a seat. She used to be very close to him when she was younger, but they grew apart gradually, and now she only enjoy a word that doesn’t involve any member of her family. 

    “Ava, you truly plotted the drama that happened to Paulina during that programe, why did you do it?” He asked, and she glared at him. 

  They have been distant enough, and she couldn’t help but wonder what he wanted to discuss with her. Ever since she returned from England, she hasn’t had a one-on-one conversation with him, only for him to summon her now to scold her for his illegitimate daughter. 

    “Are you upset that I set up your illegitimate daughter?” She blurted, and he glared at her. 

    “Ava, you know the consequences of what you are doing. Do you want to jeopardize your mother’s election?”

   “Is mum’s election your major concern, or you are worried for your illegitimate daughter?”

   “When did you become like this, Ava? This isn’t the daughter I raised.”

    “The daughter you raised without an illegitimate sister is gone the day that illegitimate daughter of yours step foot in this house.” She shouted, and stood up. 

     “I’m not done talking!” He yealled. 

    “What do you want to do? What do you still have to say? Are going to give me an earful for me to accept her, or you want to threaten me? I’m not scared of you, dad. You lose my respect the day you brought your illegitimate daughter home.” She blurted, and he raised his hand to hit her, but he controlled himself. 

    “Ava…”

   “What? Are you going to hit me? Why don’t you hit me? I was doing just fine in England, but you made me return and brought that illegitimate daughter of yours home as well. Don’t you think about how I’ll feel? Don’t you care about me at all? Obviously, you don’t. You only worry about yourself, that’s why you brought that illegitimate daughter of yours here. I hate you!” She shouted, and ran off. 

  She bumped into her mother on the way, but she ignored her and continued on her way, running all the to her room. 
*
  Grace looked at her daughter, and went to the living room to join Paul. 

    “Paul, what did you discuss with Ava to make her that angry?” She asked, and he let out a sigh. 

   “I just confirmed that Ava was truly the one behind what happened to Paulina during that programe, and she might likely be the one that set up that blackmailer to you.”

   “So?” She blurted, and he looked at her. 

   “So? Your daughter is causing troubles, and that’s all you can say.” 

    “Are you worried about her? Are you worried about your illegitimate daughter with Charlotte?” She lashed. 

    “Grace… Ava did the wrong thing, and I’m trying to scold her for it.”

     “Don’t you dare! You were the one who went wrong in many ways, you are the reason why our daughter is hurting, you are the one trying to jeopardize my election. It’s all you, Paul! If you haven’t gotten involved with Charlotte in the first place, if you never had a child with her then none of this would’ve happened.”
    
     “What are you getting at? “

    “What’s your justification for having an illegitimate daughter? What? You were with Charlotte before we got married? You would have married Charlotte if I didn’t enter the picture? Is that your justification?”

   “Grace, don’t bring the past into this. As long as I’m Ava’s father, I’ve all rights to scold my daughter when she does something wrong,” 

   “Go ahead and scold her all you want, fight for your illegitimate daughter. But remember this, Paul. Her existence is still a secret from father.” With that, she took the exit. 

  “Yes, it was all my fault.” He lamented, remembering how he ended up with Grace.

  FLASHBACK to 19 years ago***
*
  Paul saw the news about charlotte’s accident, he panicked in fear and he hurried to the parking lot intending to leave the house when his father’s car drove in. 

   “Paul, heading somewhere?” His father asked. 

   “I’m in a hurry, dad. I’ll be back.”

    “Where are you off to? We are receiving important guests later, and they will be here shortly. You should adjourn your outing.”

  “It’s an emergency. My friend was involved in an accident.”

    “Your friend? Or your girlfriend?” His father said, and Paul looked at him. 

    “Yes, my girlfriend.”

   “Paul, I never cared about your relationship, but you have to end it with her. Her family are criminals. I can’t believe I allowed you hangout with such people in the first place.”

   “Dad, please. Charlotte is in danger, I’ve to go to her.”

    “What will that change? Your being there won’t bring her out of danger. Now go back in, I already instructed the securities not to let you out, just until we entertain our guests. Return back inside.” With that his father left, and Paul let out a sigh before returning to his room. 
*
  He was summoned to the garden few minutes later, and he met Grace and her father there. 

    “Paul.” Grace called cheerfully, waving at him, and he managed to force a smile, waving back. 

   “You are all grown now, Paul. You’re now a man. I totally forgot the young kid that was always roaming around the compound with Grace.” Mr. Packard (Grace father) said, and they all laughed.
*
  The adults started talking about a wedding, and it took him a while to realize that they were planning his wedding to Grace because he had been absentminded since the whole conversation started. 

    “What wedding?” He muttered, and they all looked at him. 

    “Paul, aren’t you here the whole time? Our parents want us to get married.” Grace said, and the parents smiled. 

  “What do you think, Paul? It would be best for you two to get married, you’ve known each other since you were born. You literally grew up together.” Paul’s father said.

   “But you didn’t discuss it with me, dad?” Paul muttered, in disbelief. 

    “Paul, Grace likes you, and I’m sure you feel the same. So why waste time?” His father said, and Paul looked at him. 

   “Do you have why objections?” Mr. Packard asked, and Paul’s father laughed. 

   “Why would there be an objection? I believe this month is best for the wedding. It’s a busy month, and they returned from school vulnerably on their own.” Paul’s father said. 
    
   “Excuse me,” Paul muttered, and excused himself. 

    “Paul.” Grace called, and ran after him. 
*
    “Paul, wait!” She shouted, when they got to the hallway. 

   “Are you kidding me? Didn’t I made myself clear when I turned down your feelings? We can only be friends, Grace. I can’t marry you!”

   “Paul, it was all my father’s idea.”

   “But you weren’t against it. Your father wouldn’t have brought it up to my father if you were against it.”

    “Why shouod I? We both know I like you.”

    “I can’t marry you, Grace. I can’t. We can only be friends.” He said, and turned to leave. 

   “Because of that daughter of a criminal?” She shouted, and he looked at her. 

     “Grace…”

    “It should’ve just been me from the start, Paul. You shouldn’t have brought that criminal’s daughter to our friendship circle. If were just me, you wouldn’t be confused.” She said, and kissed him, but he pushed her away. 

   “Even if we were never friends with Charlotte, it can’t still be you.” 

     “What’s so wrong with me?” 

  “I don’t have time for this, Grace. Go back to your father and tell him to cancel his plans, I’ll tell my father the same. That’s all I’ve to say.”

   “Never! Remember I told you Paul, you can either be mine, or you choose me. It’s either way, Paul.”

  “Stop being pathetic! Our friend is in the hospital, and all you care about is this?”
     
   “Who cares about her? To me, she was never a friend. And if you call this being pathetic, then you will be stuck with this pathetic woman for the rest of your life.” With that she left.
*
  The Packard’s left later, and Paul’s father summoned him to his living room. 
     
    “Stupld!” His father shouted, and slapped him. 

    “I’m sorry, dad. But even if you hit me more than this, I won’t still marry Grace. You should have consulted me before making any plans.” 

   “Even if your chance of being the chairman is on the line?” His father muttered, and Paul looked at him. 
   
   “What are you talking about, dad?” 

   “The fact! The Packard’s are the only ones that can guarantee a future for you.” His father said, and hand over some documents to him. 

  In the documents, there are evidence that prove some funds embezzlements, and illegal tax payment. 
    
   “What’s all these, dad?”

  “Exactlywhat it is. I got a call from the chief prosecutor today, and if I get charges for these, we will lost tons of money. If I want to get out without serving time, and I would have to trade all our shares for it.”

   “So what does this have to do with the Packard’s?” 

    “You know Joffrey (Mr. Packard)  is a very powerful politician, I could come this far with his help, so he will help me cover this up till I fix it gradually without getting the public attention.”

   “But what does that have to do with me marrying Grace?”

  “He presented the marriage proposal.” 

    “Dad…”

    “Paul, our family’s future is in your hands. If we loose our shares, the company will no longer be ours, and your  future can’t be guaranteed.” 

    “Dad…”

   “Joffrey can’t let his daughter marry you if he won’t find a way to cover this, so it’s an assurance.” 

    “Dad…”. Paul shuddered, and his father patted his shoulder before leaving. 
*
  Paul left the house and went to a bar to drink, thinking about his father’s proposal and the consequences. He have always lived his life as the heir to the Stanford’s Group, the thought of losing it was very bothersome. 

    “You shouod have invited me if you are going to drink out.” He heard Grace said, sitting next to him. 

   “I want to be alone.” He muttered. 

   “I’m alone too. Let’s forget about our little fight, Paul. We’re still friends after all.” Grace said, and he nodded. 

    “Yeah… we’re still friends.” He muttered.

 They both got drunk together, and also ended up sleeping together.

  HOLLAND HIGH^^^

  CHAIRMAN STANFORD’S MANSION^^^

  Paulina arrived home after a long day at school she tried to work on her assignment till it was late, but a call from Micheal kept distracting her. 

  She ignored his calls severally, but she realized he won’t pick up, so she received it angrily. 

    📞*What? What do you what?*
   📞*Yes, where is the owner of the phone.*
    📞*Why am I the one he was trying to call?*
     📞*Hmmmm… ok, I’ll be right there, can I get the name of the bar again?*
    📞*Ok, I’ll be there in a jiffy.* With that she dropped the call. 

   Awry to what she expected, a bar attendant was the one who picked up the call to tell her Michael was drunk and trying to contact her.

    “If I were your childhood friend, that Mikey that you always call me… would you trust me? Even if it was a tiny winky trust?” 

    “Even if it will make you feel better, Mike, I would be honest with you. I don’t trust you, and even worse if you were Mikey.” She muttered, and he looked at her. 

      “Really?” He asked, and she nodded; remembering her childhood friend again.

    “Yes it was. But I waited for nothing, and was late to school.”

   “Promise.” She said, and they did the pinky swear.

   Paulina waited for him patiently the filling day for close to an hour, but he never showed up. She left angrily and ends to school with intentions to confront him later but he wasn’t at school. 

  She waited the day after, but it was still the same and she started to worry, he didn’t come show up when she went fishing, and he never shooed up again. She doesn’t know his house, so there was no way she could check up on her, so she gathered courage to ask his classmates who always bullied her, and she discovered that he transferred school, and she never saw him again. They were friends for barely a month, but his sudden disappearance really hurt her.

 Paulina let out a sigh, and put the bottle of wine away. 

   “Come on, let’s go.” She said, and helped him up. 

   “Why? Why don’t you trust Mikey? I thought he was your friend.” He muttered, and she assisted him up, wrapping his arm around her shoulder.

 She dragged him to confine walking, but he lost his balance and they both fell together, with him lying flat on her. 

   “Mike, get up.” She shouted. 

  “I… I’m not a true elite.” He muttered, and passed  out.

   “Having problems, young man?” His father asked, patting his back.

    “Gosh… that’s the most expensive wine here, I can’t believe you opened it while sulking. I paid a fortune to get that.” His father said, and poured himself a glass. “But I believe even this billon dollar wine, can’t solve your problems.”

   “Dad, what’s wrong with me? Why can’t she hit me like she hit him? Why can’t she tease me as well? Why can’t she laugh that hard with me?” He cried. 

    “Bad timing. Your mum should’ve been the one here instead. We both know I’m bad with words.”

     “Am I that bad?”
     
     “No, no, not you, but me.”

     “So I take after you.”

     “What? Never! You and Clara should take after your mother. I managed to be the chairman because I had no rival, and I’ve been struggling a lot to ensure the company continues to grow. I suck at a lot of things, son. I can’t even list them.”

    “Dad… we are just the same. She is more comfortable with them than with me.” He cried. 

   “No, son. You shouldn’t be like me. Please, don’t be like me. The future of the Greenwoods’ family relies on you.”

    “I’m sorry, dad… but I’m already lacking. She even left with them like we never existed.” 

    “Son, there is still hope for you, you can still be a better person.”

    “Don’t have any expectations from me, dad… I’m not just the one… mum was right after all, I might not be the one.”

    “Son, what’s the motto your grandfather taught you.”

    “Never give up, each day is a new beginning.” 

    “That’s my boy. I’m so incapable that I alway whine over a lot of things, I don’t even use that motto anymore.” His father whined, snd emptied his glass of wine. 

    “Dad… what do I do?” 

    “I don’t know, son. Your incapable father is lost. That’s why I married your mother to spare my children from my incapable gene. Do you know how much I struggled to get your grandfather’s blessing’s when I wanted to marry your mother? Despite coming from a good family, despite having a good reputation, your grandfather was still against us because he saw this day coming. Thinking about it gives me the creeps… opss…” 

   “I really like her, dad. What do I do?” He cried, and his father embraced him. 

   “I wish I could help you as your father, but I’m equally lost. I’m having some problems right now, and I wish your mother could just put away what she is doing and give me a listening ear.”

   “What’s going on here?” His mother shouted, from behind then father and son composed themselves immediately. 

    “Mum, shouod I give up?” Sage muttered, and his father gestured his mother to speak to him. 

   “Son, it’s late. Your problems can only be solved when your head is clear. Let’s talk it out tomorrow.” She said, and he nodded before leaving. 
    
   “Just like that?” Mr. Greenwood gasped, and his wife shook her head in disbelief.

    “Where is Stan?” He asked. 

    “I’m not his keeper,” Austin grumbled in annoyance. 

    “I can’t find him,”

   “He was somewhere with Kaila. They were dancing earlier, but they left together,” he grumbled, matching his feet hard on the floor in anger. 

   “Look for him. I’ve to find Lina, and I need him,”

    “Make it quick,” Micheal said, and Austin nodded.

  He hacked into all the cctv cameras, before he found her. 

   “Look, she is with a man,” Austin said, and Michael collected the phone. 

   “Who’s this?” Michael blurted. 

   “It can’t be that middle class guy, he can’t have access to the party, so who could this be?” Austin said. 

   “Doesn’t this back look familiar, like Steven’s?” Stanley muttered. 

   “Search for Kim in other footage, you should be able to see his face,” Micheal said. 

   “Goodness man, that’s a lot of work,” Stanley whined, and Michael parted his shoulder. 

     “Micheal, how rude of you to leave important guests and wander around with your lackeys,” Mrs. Russ blurted, joining them. 

  Michael gave him friends a reassuring look before following his mother to see the president off.

   “Where did he take her? Try to find it,” Austin said, and Stanley glared at him. 

Micheal’s phone beeped from a message from his father, he let out a sigh

  CHAIRMAN RUSS’S MANSION^^^

    “Frankly speaking Rae, we were all aware of that snobbish behavior from the start, and we endured it through middle school, and up till now,” Lia point out. 

   “At least we all have mutual enemies then. Now, she is just hating on that parasite without a good reason,” Rae blurted. 

   “And you said it yourself, she is a parasite. You would act the same if you were in Ava’s shoes, and we would have start up for each other the same way,” Alicia said. 

   “The problem started because that little schooler got lucky to be favored by Mike, but now the table turned, and Ava is the queen again,” Lia said, and Alicia nodded along. 

   “Whatever! I don’t regard her as a friend anymore,” Rae said. 

   “Rae… let’s just make up with her once and for all. For all I care, we were once friends. The ALAR.” Lia remark. 

   “So tacky,@ Rae grumbled. 

   “Come on, Rae,” Alicia urged. 

   “I’m out. And you, Alicia, didn’t you sly us once because of that middle class guy that you like? Why are you suddenly acting like you cared about this friendship?” 

    “Rae…” Lia called. 

   “It’s true, Lia, she sly us once and you were determined to cut off ties with her. You personally removed her from our group chat so stop acting up,”

   “That makes us friends. We fight and get back together,” Alicia said. 

   “Go to her and see if she considers you guys as a friend, but count me out,” with that she directed her attention to her phone. 

   “What do we do, girl?” Alicia asked. 

   “Make up with Ava of course. It was all Rae’s fault that we had this misunderstanding in the first place,” Lia blurted, glaring at Rae who didn’t spare her a glance. 

   “You’re right, girl. Rae started everything in the first place,” Alicia added. 
*
  The teacher enter the class and they all divert their attention to the attention to the teacher. After the tiring lectures, the teacher left and Lia turned to Rae. 

    “Bae, we’re going to Ava now,” Lia said, looking at Rae who ignored them by plugging her headphones to her ear. 

    “Yes, we should,” Alicia added, and removed Rae’s headphones. 

   “I’m out, suit yourselves,” Rae blurted, and left the class. 

    “What do we do now?” Alicia grumbled. 

    “Rae is just being stubborn,” 

    “We should give her sometime,”

   “There is no time, bae. If Ava join hands with Mike, we will be her first target before that parasite. Let’s just go to Ava now, and wait for Rae to come around,” Lia said, and Alicia nodded. 

  They both went to Ava who chuckled at the sight of them. 

   “Hi, girl,” they both chimed cheered 

   “Congratulations on your engagement,” Lia added. 

    “You and are just meant to be. You both made a perfect couple before you broke up, and now you’re matched together by your parents like destiny,” Alicia remarked. 

   “I know, right? Destiny!” Lia giggled. 

    “Get lost!” Ava muttered, and they both exchanged glances before letting out a sigh. 

   “Bae, are you still mad at us? We already talk Rae out for you,” Lia said, making Ava, and Alicia looked at her. She winked at Alicia who smiled, and nodded along. 

   “Yes, it was all Rae’s fault, so we taught her a lesson for you. How can we be friends with a nuisance like her?” Alicia added, and Lia smiled in relief. 

   “You two should get lost, but ensure you are alert when I need you, but for now, the teacher own the ground,” Ava stated, and their face brightened up in excitement. 

   “Does that mean we are cool now?” Lia beamed. 

   “I’m so glad, Ava, now we are back to how we used to be,” Alicia giggled. 

    “Can we return to the suite now?” Lia asked grinning softly. 

    “Get lost!” Alicia muttered, and they laughed sarcastically before returning to their seats. 

  Ava snickered after they left, before diverting her attention to her phone. She already expected them to return after the news of her engagement circulate. She have no intention of being friends with them again after everything that conspire between them, but thinking back and following her grandfather’s advise, they were the best people to keep around, the lackey that would do anything she wanted. 

  “It’s all working out, and it’s best with Rae out,” she thought, and a wicked smile spread across her face.

 CHAIRMAN RUSS’S MANSION^^^
^
 Micheal tossed and turned on his bed fighting his nightmare to wake up 
*
*
 ◐ˍ◑ IN HIS NIGHTMARE◐ˍ◑
*
  Eight years old Mike ran as fast as his legs could carry him to escape the monster chasing him with a stick. He ran crying in agony, without looking at where he was heading, he was almost ran over by a car and he panicked and fainted in front of the car. Waking up with thoughts that he has escaped the monster, he looked around and saw surrounded by a ton of monster. 

   “Argghhh….”  He screamed, and jolted up from bed, panting heavily. 
**
  His alarm might fail to wake him up, but he his nightmare never failed. 

  He had a long day all through the weekend drinking himself away, after his last conversation with his father. He couldn’t think straight afterwards, so in order to stop himself from thinking about anything. He isolated himself for days, and had nothing but alcohol. 
*
 He left the bed weakly, and almost collapsed on the floor but a strong grip lifted him up, and he looked up to see Mr. Jones. 

   “Mr. Mike, why are you doing this to yourself?” Mr. Jones inquired worriedly, and Micheal smiled weakly. 

   “Am I not allowed to do this as well?” He growled. 

   “Sir, you’ve a meeting today after school, it can’t be adjourned,” 

   “That again?” He sneered. “But even with all that, I still need her!” He snapped, and Mr. Jones bowed. “I’ve to be at school anyways,” he muttered, and wobbled on his feet to the bathroom. 
*
  He prepared for school, and the chef served his breakfast. 

   “Take them back,” he muttered. 

    “Sir… you should at least have a bite,” one of the chefs said, and the others glanced at her discreetly before looking away. That was the first time a lowly chef would talk back at him. 

   “You must be new in my quarters,” he spluttered, and the chef cower.  

    “Mr. Mike, she is your personal dietary, and also a premium chef. She started working at the mansion few months ago, and madam detailed her to your service, after learning about your health,”

   “Mum did?” 

   “Yes, sir,” Mr. Jones replied, and Micheal smirked. 

    “You, look up,” he said, and the chef struggled before taking a peek at him, and instantly looked away. 

     “He asked you to look up!” Mr. Jones snapped, and Micheal gestured him to stop. 

   “It’s fine,” he spoke softly before leaving. 
*
 He got to school and many students gathered him asking bunch of questions, he was quite unlucky to arrive at school during recess. 

  He sighed heavily in despair, he has never been so adrift before, he just stood there hoping his friends come to his rescue. 

He closed his eyes, tearing for an escape when he felt someone held his hand. The students can throw questions, but none of them has the audacity to touch him. Only his friends and one other person, Paulina. 

  He opened his eyes, and his gaze met her beautiful eyes. 

   “Let’s go,” she muttered, and intertwined her fingers in his. 
**
**
  IN MRS. RUSS’OFFICE^^^
*
  Mrs. Russ was watching a video on her tablet —video of Paulina and Michael that recently happened.  She chuckled derisively, and put the phone away. 

 Her face brightened up, and looked at her secretary. 

    “Has the new chef resumed work at Micheal’s quarter?” She asked. 

    “Yes, ma’am. And it was reported that he had a conversation with her,”

    “Wow,”

    “Ma’am, if I may ask, why did you detailed her to work for him? He has more than enough staffs,”

    “You may not ask,” she growled, and he bowed his head. 

    “Pardon my blunder , ma’am,”

    “It seems like everything is falling in place,” she muttered, her face lifted, grinning widely. 
.
