  Paulina has been lost in thought during classes, she kept thinking about Micheal and everything Stanley told her. A messaged beeped in her phone from Sage bringing her out of her thoughts, and she put the phone away. He has been sending a lot of messages, and called her all through the weekend, but she didn’t have the confidence to pick his calls or respond to his messages. She felt devastated, she was lost on how to explain the situation to him.

   She exhaled heavily, and glanced at the MASS empty seats. They mostly come late to class at times, and just slack behind in their suite. She couldn’t get many thoughts out of her mind, so she left the class with intention of going to thier suite to confront Micheal, ignoring the teacher in class. She has to confirm the validity of Stanley’s claims from him. 

She got to the hallway heading to the elevator, and Jessica, accompanied by one of her friends hurried to join her in the elevator. 

   “Hi,” Jessica said cheerfully. 

   “Hi,” she managed to wave with opposite energy. 

    “I was actually on my way to your class,” 

   “Then you shouldn’t have entered the elevator,”

    “Yeah, I shouldn’t have, but you’re the one I was going to meet,”

    “Me?”

   “I heard that you broke up with Mike after his engagement was announced. Some people saw you leaving the party with another man, I don’t blame you. I mean… anyone would do same,” 

    “Why did you want to see me?”

    “Oh, yeah,” she sneered, and swing her fingers. “I want to invite you to join my club. I’m aware that clubs aren’t recruiting new members, but I’m the leader, I pull the strings. You can’t possibly be in  same club with your unfaithful ex, so accept my proposal,” Jessica said, and stretched her hand for a shake. 

    “I’ll think about it,” she muttered, and Jessica dropped her hand with a jeer. 

    “Again? This is the second time you’re rejecting me. You won’t have a third chance. I really belihe that we will make a good team. I mean… on average I score 99.7% in maths, and others in the team are also 90% on average. You fit in perfectly,”

   “I told you before, I really have no interest in math. I study to pass my exams, and that’s all,”

    “You’ve till school dismisses to think about it. This is  the card to get access to our club room, see you after school,” Jessica said, and stopped the elevator at the next floor before leaving. 
*
  Paulina arrived at the MASS suite, and push the passcode in. She entered inside, and their face fell in disappointment, expect Steven who welcomed her with a smile. 

    “What are you doing here?!” Austin yelled, aiming at her but Stanley held him back. 

    “Where is Mike?” She asked. 

   “What do you care?” Austin blurted. 

   “He isn’t here, and we can’t reach him. I’m saved he might’ve isolated himself again, like last time. He is always that way when things aren’t going his way,” Stanley said. 

    “Why are you explaining to her? She left our group, remember?” 

    “When was he ever that way?” Paulina found herself asking. 

    “When he broke up with Ava, he was heartbroken m, and isolated himself for several days, I hope that isn’t the case,” Stanley said. 

    “Why are you explaining to her?”Austin snapped. 

   “Quit it Austin, we’re all worried about Mike here,” Stanley said, and Austin glared at Paulina. 

   “But she left our group, and she was with a some guy during the party. Yeah… it was Steven, why were you two together?” Austin blurted. 

    “Do you think Mike will come to school today? If he would, students will surround him to ask him questions, and history might end up repeating itself,” Stanley said worried. 

    “What do you mean by history repeating itself?” Paulina asked, already scared. 

    “I doubt if that will happen, he was heartbroken when Ava broke up with him, but he has no reason to be heartbroken now…”. Austin was saying, but he shook his head. “Yeah, you might be right,”

    “What’s going on?” Paulina inquired already restless. 

    “Mike doesn’t walk alone, he is always nervous  whenever he is alone, most especially when he is being asked questions,” Stanley muttered. 
*
  Paulina left later, and was returning back to class. It’s already recess break, and students so students started exiting their class, wandering around in group. 

  She received a message from Sophia, and she was took the elevator to the last floor, and met Sage waiting for her by the elevator. 

   “Did I do something wrong?” He snapped at her, starling her. 

  She looked at him, and shook her head. He nodded and spread his arms wide for her, she smiled softly, and walked into his embrace. 

    “I’m sorry, Sage,” she said in a soft whisper. 

    “It’s fine,” he muttered, patting her back. 

    “Hey, guys,” Sophia shouted joining them. 

  They break free from the embrace, and Sophia é embraced Paulina. 

   “Babe, how’ve you been?” She muttered, patting Paulina’s back gently. 

  They disengaged later, and Sophia cupped Paulina’s gave in her palm. 
    
   “It must’ve been really hard on you, but I’m sure he is going to suffer somehow for leaving you. What am I even saying, he is already suffering. Maby students have surrounded him by the hallway, and he is struggling to find his way. He is literally stranded there,”

    “How gan he be stranded?” Sage asked in disbelief. 

   “I don’t know. They were all bombarding him with questions about his engagement, and he was stranded there like an amateur,” 

    “How is that possible? What of his squad?” Sage asked. 

      “He was alone, I…-,” Sophia was saying, but she kept quiet when Paulina ran off. “Where is she running off to?” She exclaimed, and Sage ran after her. “Wait for me,” she should, and followed. 
*
 Paulina ran down the hallway after Sophia shocking news about Micheal. She remembered what Stanley told her earlier about Micheal. She ran as her legs could carry her ignoring sage and Sophia who were calling her. She got to the crowd, and started forcing her way to where he was until she got to where he was, and held his hand. 

 He looked at her immediately hand touched his, with yearning eyes, hoping for an escape. 

      “Let’s go,” she muttered, and intertwined her fingers in his. 

  He nodded softly, and embraced her, to everyone surprise. 

   Austin, Stanley, and Steven who just arrived froze on their feet, and the students started taking pictures and videos with their phone. 

    “Thank you, thank you,  you can all return to your class now,” Austin shouted, clapping his hands, and everyone started to dismiss. 

   Everyone left, and Micheal didn’t disentangled himself from Paulina until Austin tapped him. 

    “Man, let’s go, they are all gone,” Stanley said patting Micheal’s back. 

  Michael released Paulina, and nodded his head like a child. Paulina was surprised to see the superior Micheal that way. That was the first time she saw that part in him. He looked just like every other teenager, and once again, he reminded her of her childhood friend. The way he pout his lips, and nodded obediently, reminded her of her bold, yet timid friend. 
**
**
 Sage stomped off to his car in frustration, the earlier scene replayed in his head, and he hit his car steering wheel angrily. 

    “Sage, are you leaving already?” Sophia asked, knocking ok the window, and he whined down for her. “Can we talk for a minute?” She asked, and he nodded. 
*
  She entered the car, and she held his hand staring directly at him. 

    “Don’t be mad at Lina, at times we can’t control the heart,”
     
   “Are you trying to justify her relationship with Mike?” He asked, and she shook her head. 

    “I know you have good intentions, I know your feelings for Lina are genuine, and I’m sure she is aware as well, that’s why she is always filled with guilt whenever she was with you. I might not know much, but I believe that what’s meant to be will always be. My parents were childhood friends before they got married, just like me, my mother only wants to associate with more influential people so they dated different people from various classes, but they eventually discovered their feelings for each other, and got married. My maternal grandparents were totally against thier relationship, because they planned to arrange my mother with a more influential man. My father was  just starting up then, he was from an humble family and my maternal family were well-off. They managed to be friends because they grew up in same neighborhood, and my maternal family were the wealthiest in the neighborhood. My parents broke up eventually, and my mother was forced to marry her betrothed, with that they both went their separate ways. My mother wasn’t happy in the marriage, so she divorced her first husband. Dad was also doing fine on his own, and they eventually met again and got married afterwards,”  she concluded all smiles, and Sage smiled too. 

    “Your parents has quite the story, I wish to meet them someday,”

   “They actually don’t look their story,” she said, and he chuckled. 

   “Is this your way of telling me to give up on Lina?”
    
   “No, this is my way of encouraging you not to give up. Even though my mother got married to another man, my father encouraged her through the process, he assured her that he will always be her friend, but he left eventually due to personal reasons. You can show affection in maby ways, Sage. You shouldn’t try to separate her from her from Mike whom she already grew fond of all in the name of protecting her,”

   “But I already stop. I’m not angry about that anymore,”

   “But you’re angry at yourself instead,”

   “Why do you sound like my mum?” He asked, and she chuckled. 

   “Did I sound like an old geezer?”

  “Old geezer? You can’t call my mum that.”

   “Actually, mums aren’t old geezers anymore, i believe all mums are youngsters like my mum,”
    
    “I fully agree with you. Thanks a lot, Sophie, your words really meant a lot to me,”

   “Then set a blind date for me if you’re grateful,”

   “I can’t believe we are on this again,” Sage grumbled. 

   “Come on, Sage,” she whined, tickling him. 

    “That’s enough, that’s enough! Fine, I will,” he surrendered, and they both laughed. 

    “You promised, ok,”

    “Yeah, I believe what will be will be,” he said, and she pouted her lips again.

   “That doesn’t fit here,” she shouted, and started tickling him again. 
**
**
  The MASS went to thier suite with Paulina in silence, they all remained that way until Austin broke the silence. 

   “Guys, in for some games?” Austin asked, but no one responded. 

   “I’ve to go to class,” Paulina said, and stood up to leave but Micheal dragged her back, making her sit next to him. 

   “Yes, I agree with you, Austin, we shouod go play some games,” Stanley said, dragging Austin to get up,”

    “Ok, let’s go, Mike,” Austin said. 

    “No, leave Mike out. Let’s go. Steve, let’s go,” Stanley said, wrapping his hand around Steven’s shoulder. 

    “Why? Let’s go with Mike, the game would cheer him up,” Austin object. 

   “Can’t you read the room?” Stanley grumbled, and grabbed Austin’s hand, dragged him away. 

   “Hey, why are you dragging me away?” Austin shouted, as Stanley dragged him to the game room. 
*
  Paulina released her hand gently from Micheal’s grip, and he stared at her emotionless. 

    “Mike, are you ok?”

   “Why? You were mad at me, you even left our club group, so why did you rescue me?” He muttered, his voice soft but audible. 

   “Because you’ve showed up to my rescue  countless times as well,”

   “And?”

   “Because I believe you,” she said, and his eyes beamed. 

   “You believe me?” He asked, and she nodded. 

    “Yes, I do. I believe everything now. Our common goal to get rid of Ava, and I also believed that you didn’t collide with Ava to lure me to the party, I believe everything now, Mike,”

    “So you trust me now?” He asked, and she smiled. 

   “Yes, I trust you,” she muttered, and he embraced her. 

   “Thank you, Lina,” he muttered, and she patted his back gently. 

  After they released from the embrace, Paulina summoned the others, and they proceeded to their club activities. 

   “What’s this? Isn’t this for the math club?” Austin asked, picking up the card that fell from Paulina’s bag. 

    “Yes, Jessica gave it to me,” 

   “Why is that girl so persistent?” Austin asked, and Stanley collected the card. 

   “Are you going to accept it?” Micheal asked, and Paulina shook her head. 

    “I’ve no interest whatsoever in math. I struggle to pass my exams, so getting in a competition…. Gosh, it gives me the creep,” she grumbled, and they all laughed. 

    “We’re the same, I also struggle during math,” Austin said. 

   “That’s rich coming from someone who never made an effort. Zero points in math is nothing to be proud of,” 

    “Say that again, Stan, Austin really needs to improve,” Micheal said, proudly. 

    “Shut it, you. You’re so proud of that five points you got,” Stanley blurted, and the others grumbled. 

    “How did you guys became friends when you are nothing alike?” Paulina asked, and they all chuckled. 

   “Stanley and Austin tried to bully me in elementary school,” Micheal said, and Paulina gaped. 

   “You don’t mean it,”

   “Come on, I thought we were over it,” Austin grumbled. 

   “Why did you tried to bully him?” Paulina asked, looking at Stanley. 

   “It wasn’t me, it was all Austin’s idea,”

    “What? Are you trying to put it all on me again?”

   “But it’s the truth,” Stanley blurted. 

   “Hey!” Austin snapped. 

   “Mike, you ended up being friends with those who wanted to bully you,” Paulina said, in amazement. 

   “It was a long time ago,” he muttered, and narrated the story to her. 
*

 FLASHBACK to when Micheal was in elementary school.***

*
  Micheal sat alone behind a window at school during recess, his only friend has been forcefully transferred from school so he has be alone with no intention of making new friends, to avoid history repeating itself. 

  He didn’t see Austin and Stanley walking up his way until Austin busted out. 

    “Hey, look up!” Austin snapped, and he looked up in fear —they were students from another class who always bullied his friend even before Micheal  enrolled in the school. He had intention of confronting them, but they’ve been on a low till his friend transferred. 

   “What?” He grumbled, and Austin smirked. 

  “Do you care for new friends? You look bored,” Austin said, intending to hit his head but Micheal held it halfway, startling them. 

    “How dare you touch me? Who do you think you  are?” Austin snapped. 

   “I’m Micheal… Micheal Russ,” Micheal said, and stood up. 

   “Who cares?” Austin blurted. 

  “Micheal Russ? The heir to Russ Group, you’re the only son of chairman Russ who just returned from Australia?” Stanley asked, and Austin looked at him. 

   “What are you saying, Stan? Do you know him?” Austin asked. 

   “We’re in trouble, he is the heir of Russ Group,” Stanley said. 

    “You don’t mean it,” Austin gasped, and Micheal chuckled. 

    “Do you still wanna be friends with me? Because I’m actually bored,” Micheal sneered, and Austin fall to his feet immediately, dragging Stanley along. 

    “I’ll do anything for you!” Austin shouted, and bowed. 

  Micheal was stunned by thier reactions, but he composed himself and act along. 

  He bent low to thier level, patting Austin’s head. 

   “Then let’s get along,” he muttered, and they both looked at him. 
     
   “Yeah, let’s get along!” Austin hollered, and Stanley exhaled in relief.

  FLASHBACK ENDS^^^
^
  Micheal concluded his story, and Paulina couldn’t control her laughters. 

    “I can’t believe they really tried to bully you,” she said, still laughing. 

   “That’s not really called bullying…” Austin shuffled. 

   “I totally agree!” Paulina teased. “So, how did you and Stanley became friends?” 

    “You see Austin here is too persistent. It was a new school year, and we happened to be in the same class. He has heard a lot about me being a genius, so he ensured that we were seat mates, he was too clingy. My old friends cut ties with me because of him, he appears everywhere I go. He said it would be a fortune for him to have a genie as a friend, you can’t believe this nutcase called me genie. He always foot me in class, and we work on our assignments together, I mean,I do his assignments for him. He is a lazy a$$!” Stanley rants, and Austin hit his head. “Hey!” He shouted, and hit him back, with that they both started running around together, trying to hit each other. 

    “Aren’t you curious about Steven?” Micheal asked, and Steven looked at them.  

   Paulina smiled and shock her head. “I’m not,” she muttered. 

   “Why?” 

   “Steven told me,”

   “What? Steven did? When did you two became so close?”

   “We are closer than you can imagine,” Paulina said, and chopped knuckle with Steven who smiled. 

    “Oh… I see,” Micheal exclaimed.

   IN THE BUS STATION^^^
^
 Charlotte alighted from the bus when she saw a familiar face, she has been in the city for some days but she was unable to locate Paulina. She went to First Group headquarters, but all efforts of seeing Paul prove abortive as well. The security didn’t her into his estate, and she needs an appointment to meet him at the office. She tried to visit Grace campaign, but thought against it. 

 The familiar face from the bus looks like her father, but unlike the father she knew, he looked ragged and unkept, but her vision was clear enough for her to recognize him. 

  She searched everywhere for him but couldn’t find him again, she was already giving up when she saw Paulina alighted from a car. 

   “Paulina,” she called but before Paulina could see her, a large arm pulled her into a vehicle, and drove off before she could call for help.

 Louis hired a private investigator to search for Charlotte in the city after she left, and they got back to him almost immediately with her location in a motel, and packed his bags ready to leave for the city. 

 He has been keeping her safe all these while, that’s why he has been traveling between the city and the countryside everyday which is always inconvenient but that was the only way he could protect her. Now that things has went astray, going to the city and facing the battle together seems like the right option. He paid Joy a huge sum as severance pay before leaving for the city. 
*
  He arrived at his house, and his maid, Ms. Bella welcomed him in. 

   “Welcome, sir. You came with your bags, does that mean you are back for good?” She asked,  delightfully. 

   “Yes, ma’am,” he teased, and she giggled. 

  She has been working with him since he returned to California, he grew fond of her because she ask too many questions, and he respects her equally as an elderly woman. 
.
   “The house has been really lonely since Mr. Ethan left. When will he be back, he was always indoor, but he suddenly  left without  a word, and never returned,” she said, and he exhaled heavily. 

    “I’m sure he will return soon, he probably needs some time to himself, to protect what he needs to,” he muttered. 

   “I hope he returns soon. He has always been so quite that I’m always worried about him. I wonder how he is doing now,” she muttered, and Louis let out a sigh.
*
  He left his house the following day and headed to the location where he was told Charlotte was, he waited outside the motel till she came out, and he let out a sigh of relief. 

    “I want you to keep an eye on her,” he instructed his driver. 

     “Yes, sir,” 

   “Ensure you keep me updated about her location every time,“ 

     “Yes, sir,”

    “Let’s see what you’re up to, Charlotte,” he muttered between his breath. 
*
  He board a cab to the hospital he works, and the driver tailed Charlotte. 
*
  He arrived at the hospital, and the chairman  summoned him. 

    “Professor,” the chairman called, and extend his hand for a shake. 

    “Good morning, sir, and feel free to call me Louis, you’ve been a father figure to me since I was a child,”

   “I know, and I’m sure her husband would be lucky,”

   “Let me guess, you already found a woman,” he said, and Louis smiled. 

   “You’re very fast for your age, sir,” Louis muttered, and they both laughed. 

   “I’m not that old, 60  is the new 40 now, haven’t you heard?” He said, and they both laughed.

  LOUIS’S HOUSE ^^^

  Charlotte woke up the following morning welcomed by an inviting smell, she instantly knew that Louis was preparing breakfast. 

  She smiled cheerfully, and went to freshen up before going to the dinning room, there he and Ms. Bella were setting the table for breakfast. 

   “Good morning,” she greeted, and they both smiled at her. 

   “Charlotte, I hope you slept well?” Louis asked

CHAIRMAN GREENWOOD’S MANSION^^^
^
Sage pieced together the event that happened earlier at school, and reflected on Micheal confession. 
*
*
 FLASHBACK to Michael’s earlier confession ***
*
  Sage struggled to find his way to the front, and when he finally succeeded, Michael’s words made him dumbfounded.

    “…- My feelings for you disappeared the moment we broke up, and I moved on after meeting Lina again,” Mike said, and freed his hands from Ava grip. 
*
*
 FLASHBACK ENDS***
*
  Sage exhaled heavily and remembered Michael’s words again. 

   “Why did he indicate meeting Paulina again in his words?” He thought, and laid flat on his bed. “Does that mean they had a history together? Could that be what she is hiding from me?” He exclaimed, running in fingers through his hair. “Don’t relent, Sage, regardless whatever history was between them, you’ve to fight for what you want, that’s how to be a man,” he muttered in determination. 
**
**
  CHAIRMAN STANFORD’S MANSION^^^
^
  Paulina tossed and turned on her bed trying to gather her thoughts together. After the scene at school, she left school with Austin while Micheal left in a hurry for a meeting. 
*
  She took her phone, and sent a message to Steven to hear his thoughts about the confessions, but it took longer for him to reply so she called him. 

   📞*Hello, I sent a message to you, come online!* She shouted, and dropped the call. 
.
    She checked her message, and waited for a minute before he replied. 

   💬She: *Whats your thoughts about Mike’s confession?*
  💬Him: *Nothing, but don’t get ahead of yourself *
  💬She: *😡. What’s that supposed to mean?*
  💬Him: *Good night 😉*
  
  She hissed in annoyance and dropped her phone when it started ringing. She checked the caller ID excitedly thinking it was Steven who called her back after returning to his senses, but her face fell in disappointment when she saw it was Sage. 

  She exhaled heavily before she received the call. 

   📞 *Hello,*
  📞*What? You’re here?*
  📞*Yes, I know we were supposed go go out, but…*
  📞*Is Sophie with you?*
  📞*Alright, I’ll join you in a jiffy*
*
  She dropped her onine and hurried to go freshen up with thoughts that hanging out with Sage will help her think properly. 

  She descending the stairs when she saw Ava who smirked immediately they gaze met. 

   “Enjoy while you can, but remember that it’s not over yet,” Ava said, walking past her. 

   “That’s rich coming from someone who was publicly rejected on her own stage!” Paulina blurted, and Ava turned around in furry. 

    “What did you say?” She snapped. 

   “What you heard, I don’t have time to repeat myself. I got a date,” she mocked, and hurried away. 

   Ava scoffed at her retreating back, and her face twist in anger. 

   “You’ve gone too far, now I’ll show you why I’m the queen!” she muttered with clenched fist. 
*
  Paulina went out and met Sage standing by his car waiting for her. 

    “Hi,” he said cheerfully, upon seeing her. 

    “Hi,” she muttered, unsure of the right word to say. 

   “Shall we?” He asked, and she nodded. 
*
  They watched a movie together, before grabbing something to eat, and went to an arcade to call it day. 

   They left the arcade and they stopped by the roadside to go watch the night star. 

  Paulina stared at the moon, and Sage looked at her. 

   “Are you thinking about something?” He asked, and she smiled. 

    “Staring at the moon always takes my worries away. You should try it, it really works,”

    “Something else works for me,” he said, and she looked at him. 

    “What?” She asked, her curiosity piqued. 

   “Staring at you like this, being with you, and spending time with you,”

    “Sage…” 

   “I told you I like you, but you said nothing about it. I was serious about my confession,”

   “Sage…”

   “I don’t care about your relationship with Mike, I don’t care if you had a history together or how far your relationship has gone, I don’t care about your motive for being being him. I don’t care about any of those, Lina,”

    “Sage…I…” she shuddered. 

    “You can only tell me you don’t like me now, reject me, and I won’t pressure you again,” he said, staring intensely at her. 

   Her heart started beating fast as his eyes locked with hers, she wanted to look away but she felt Kyle she is glue to that position. Just earlier, Mike confessed and she is still doubting the credibility of his words, and now Sage just pour out a heartfelt confession to her. She has never been in such situation before, unlike many other teenagers, she never considered dating, she was a nerd who only enjoy the company of her mother and few friends. Everything was new to her, she doesn’t understand the meaning of love, the words were new to her, she can’t even explain the feelings she had for him. 
  
   “Since you’re speechless, that only means you can’t reject me, but you’re confused about your feelings. I’m sincere about mine,” he said, and held her hand, placing it on his chest. “My heart beats this fast just for you, and if your heart beats the same way, then you need to take your time to think about your feelings,” he said, and she started hiccuping. 

    “Sage…”

   “You don’t have to sag anything if you aren’t sure of your feelings. I’ll give you time to think about it, I’ll confess to you five times, and if you can’t still give me a response then, I’ll give up. This is my first confession,” he said, and nodded. At that point, that was the only command her body could respond to. 
**
**
   HOLLAND HIGH^^^
^
  Paulina wobbled on her feet along the hallway when she felt a hand on her shoulder, and she saw Steven. 

   “Are you still thuninh about that heartfelt confession?” He teased, and she eyed him before looking away. “I guess you haven’t found an answer yet,” he teased, and she hit his hand away from her. “Fine, I’m leaving now,” he sneered, and was about to leave but a thought came to her mind and she dragged him back. “What?” He snapped. 

    “You’re the only one I could think of,” she muttered, batting her lashes cutely. 

   “Nah… that won’t wole for me,” he teased, and scoffed. 

    “Then what will?” She muttered. 

    “Asking nicely,”

   “Please….” She shuddered, and he smiled, wrapping his hand around her shoulder. 

    “I’m all yours,” he muttered, and she smiled. 
*
  They went to the cafeteria rooftop and she narrated her problems to him, lying that her friend was the one with the problems. 

  Steven chucked after she concluded the story, and exhaled heavily. 

   “So, you mean another guy finfessed to you after Mike’s heartfelt confession? And now you are confused,”

    “Exactly! The  confession caught me off gua…-“. She saw saying, but she instantly realized what she was saying. “No, no, it wasn’t me. It was a friend of mine from my neighborhood,”
     
    “The Stanford’s estate? Is it perhaps, Ava?” He teased. 
    
    “Of course not, my former neighborhood! That’s not the point here, Steve, I really need your advice… to be able to advise my friend, of course,”

   “Well… whoever it is shouldn’t be the point, the advice is what matters,”

   “Yes, exactly!”

    “I told you last might not to jump the gun. Mike’s confession might all be unreal like your relationship. You both shared a common goal, and that’s getting rid of Ava, and yesterday’s event was just perfect,” he said, she concurred. 

   “So… you mean it might all be fake, even the kiss…” she shuddered, and he laughed. 

    “That’s not even a kiss to me, anyone could do the same, I can even do it now,” he teased, and wanted to kiss her forehead but she pushed him away. 

    “Get away from me!” She snapped. 

    “Mike can play all his cards to get what he wants, and that’s exactly what he did! Did he called you after his meeting later?” He asked, and she shook her head. “If his confession was real, he should have called you later to seek an answer, but singe he didn’t, forget about it and focus on your education,” he bantered, and she glared at him. 

    “What do you even know?” She shouted. 

   “Nothing! I know nothing. The last time I dated was in kindergarten school, so you should take advice from a more experienced person to assist your friend,” 

    “What friend?”

   “The one who needs the advice, remember it’s not for you,” he teased, and she slightly nodded. 

    “Thank you, you can go,”

    “You shouldn’t get involved with boys like Mike, you might be in same class, but you belong to different status. He is not an ordinary student like his peers. That’s my last advice to your friend,” with that he left. 

   “He is right, I shouldn’t get ahead of myself,” she grumbled when she felt a presence. “What do you want? I thought you left already!” She snapped, and turned to see it’s Sage. 

    “I see you are now bringing your other friends to our hangout,” Sage said, and she gasped. 

   “Our?” The word echoed in her head, snd she gasped again. “When did it became ours? This is the school premises everyone has equal rights here!” She lashed, starling him. 

   “Hey, don’t be so uptight, I’m merely cracking jokes,” 

   “You shouldn’t crack jokes like that, anyone could think otherwise,”

   “Who would think so?” He inquired, and she instability realized what she has been saying so far. 

   “What’ve  gotten into me?” She thought. “It’s all Steve’s fault,” she almost cried but instantly composed herself. “No one! Bye, I’m late for class,” she said, and carried her bag before running off. 

  She bumped into Sophia on her way, but she ignored Sophia and kept on running. 

   “Hey, Lina!” Sophia shouted. “What’s wrong with her?” Sophia thought, and joined Sage who was smiling cheerfully. “Sage, what’s wrong with Lina?” She asked. 

   “Nothing, she was just being cute,” he muttered all smiles, and Sophia scoffed. 

   “I made a mistake by coming here, a big one,” she muttered, before leaving. 
**
**
  Paulina got to class, and the MASS entered almost immediately, she exchanged greetings with her, and Steven winked at her before they all went to seat. 

  She exhaled heavily, and steal a quick glance at Micheal who acted like she doesn’t exist. 

   “Even if it was all an act, he shouldn’t have ignored me like that,” she grumbled, and placed her head on her desk, mumbling words to herself. 
*
 During recess, she carried her bag ready to elope into a comfort zone—although she had none. 

  She was about to run out of the class, but she felt a hand held her back. She tried to compose herself before she turned around and gasped when she saw it was Micheal. 

   “What? Why are did you grabbed me?” She snapped, and relapsed herself from his grip. 

  Austin and Stanley looked at her puzzled by her reaction, while Steven chuckled softly. 

    “Hey, why are you acting up?” Austin blurted. 

   “Let’s have lunch together,” Micheal said, and she snubbed him before looking away — he confided her with his confession yesterday, and ignored her afterwards, acting like it was all a part of their agreement. The agreement was to fake relationship, not affection. 

    “What a two-faced man,” she grumbled, and they all looked at her. 

    “What did you say?” Austin anded. 

   “No! I’m going to eat at the cafeteria!” She blurted, and intend to leave but he dragged her back. 

   “Then let’s eat at the cafeteria,” he muttered, and she looked at him wondering if that was also an act as well. 

   “An act to show others that we are close,” she thought. 

   “He should consider acting,” she grumbled, loud enough for him to hear. 

   “Who ?” Austin asked, and she looked at him, her gaze met Steven’s funny smile, and she looked away in embarrassment. 

   “I’m hungry,” Steven muttered, and they all looked at him. 

  “What did he say?” Austin asked, awed. 

   “You must be famished to complain. Yes, we should go eat,” Stanley wrapping his arm around Steven’s shoulder. 
*
  They entered the supreme class cafeteria, and the students gasped upon seeing them. They never had lunch at the cafeteria before, they all go there whenever they aim to bully anyone. 

  The students started mumbling words to themselves wondering who the target might be, but the MASS left them perplexed when they went to seat. They might have never had a meal at the cafeteria, but a table was specially reserved for them. 

  Paulina looked around in wonderment, that was her first time at the supreme class cafeteria, and unlike the others, things were done differently. There were waiters taking orders, and the seats were even more comfortable 

  They sat to eat after receiving their orders, and the boys engaged in random conversation about a  party. 

    “Lia hasn’t hosted a party since forever,” Austin said. 

   “She is busy plotting with Ava, that must’ve been more entertaining to her,” Stanley replied. 

  “I don’t think Ava is at school today,” Austin said. 

   “She is obviously embarrassed after what happened yesterday. If I were her, I would return to England at the spot,” Stanley said, and Austin laughed. 

   “Do you guys always attend Lia’s party?” Paulina couldn’t help but ask. 

   “Yes, she is the queen of parties. Her parties are always a lit. Always filled with vibes from all over the city. We always receive special invitations to her party, but she didn’t invite us to her last party. I miss the good old days, we used to be cool, even after Ava left,” Austin said, wistfully.

   “That’s enough,” Micheal muttered. 

   “Why is Rae alone?” Austin said, pointing at Rae who was eating alone. 

   “Do you think she had a misunderstanding with her friends?” Stanley muttered. 

   “Come to think of it, she wasn’t with them yesterday,” Austin point out. 

   “What’s your stress?” Micheal blurted. 

   “I used to have a crush on her back then, but she was dating Roy then,” Austin said, and Stanley chuckled   

   “I told you to tell her back then,” Stanley said. 

   “Not after Roy bought that scooter before us. There was no way I could compete with him, he was always a step ahead of us. I think that guy has someone spying on us,” Austin muttered. 

   “I wonder why they broke up,” Stanley wondered. 

   “Neither of them would have lasted in that relationship. I heard Roy is now in a secret relationship with a Miss America, he is going to flaunt her in Alex’s beach party next weekend. Alex bought a new beach house, and he is throwing a party there. We should get something unique as well, how does a garden house sounds?” Austin said, and Paulina choked on her food. 
    
   “Are you ok? Have some water,” Micheal said, worriedly and she had a slip from the water. She can never understand their conversation, they were always flaunting about money like their  aren’t all teenagers. Steven was right, they belonged to different status entirely. 

   “I rather buy a chopper than a garden house. While on that, what do you guys think about getting a chopper?” Stanley asked, and they all cleared thier throat. 

   “You’re at it again. I’m sure Roy will get that before us again!” Austin blurted. 

   “Mike, what’s your take? I vote for a chopper,” Stanley said. 

   “Garden house I stand!” Austin blurted. 

   “You shouod all date first, and stop worrying about material possessions “ he muttered, and Paulina chuckled. 

   “I’ll ensure I get a new girl before this month runs out, and we must celebrate our 100 days together in the MASS garden house,” Austin said. 

   “And I’ll get one too, and celebrate our 100 days together traveling around in our chopper!” Stanley said. 

   “Can you even last 100 days?” Austin teased, and Micheal chuckled. 

   “Leave the girls to Austin, man. We might end up with the garden house,” Micheal mocked. 

   “What’s your vote?” Stanley asked. 

   “I’m busier than that. Let the best man win. We should wrap up here, recess is almost over,” Micheal said, and they all nodded. 

    “I’m really going to find a girl this time, and we will celebrate 100 days, a year, and even more together!” Stanley lashed. 

    “I vote my new car that’s on the way,” Austin said. 

   “I vote my new scooter,” Micheal said, and they looked at Steven. 

    “Will you vote your car as well?” Austin asked, Steven who nodded, and they all looked at Paulina. 

    “What?” She grumbled, and they shook their head. 

   “Let’s go, guys,” with that they left the cafeteria. 
*
School dismissed later, and Sage offered Paulina a ride. Thankfully, he didn’t talk about his confession and she asked him to drop her at the bus station. 

  She looked around for the ragged man who was mostly at the station before she boarded the bus. She was fond of always seeing him there, and always felt some sense of relief whenever see saw him. 

   “I wonder if he left already,” she thought. 
*
  She got home later and couldn’t stop thinking about the ragged man, she was always curious about him and his sudden appearance sadden her in a way. She dibwr her thoughts away from him when a message beeped into her phone from Micheal. 

   💬*Do you want to watch the stars with me later?* the message reads, and she blushed softly. 
   💬*Sure!* she tested back wondering what he wanted to discuss with her. 
*
  He came to pick her up later, and they enjoyed a peaceful long ride, and he pulled at a feild. 

  He spread a comfortable sheet on the ground, and they stared at the sky together. 

   “The moon is out today,” she blushed, and he looked at her. 

   “Let’s end our agreement, Lina,” he muttered, and she looked at him. 

  Their gaze met, and neither of them looked away. 

   “My confession the other day wasn’t bonded by our agreement, so let’s end it. I want to quit deceiving myself, be my girl for real this time,” he stated, and he’d heart started beating fast. She couldn’t think of a response, neither could she look away. It was the same way she felt when Sage confessed, and she became confused at that spot. 

   “Who does my heart truly beats for?” She thought, still staring at Micheal.

  Micheal has been thinking of the best way to confess to Paulina, because she always mistook his affection as part of their agreement. After Ava tried to blackmail him the other day, he take advantage of her stage to put her in her place and confess his true feelings to Paulina, but he couldn’t man up to his words afterwards. His friends believed it was all an act, so as Paulina so he couldn’t think of a way to clarify himself. 

 He tried to send her messages, but he couldn’t compose a message, so he contemplated before call on her but his phone went off in the process so he took it as a warning sign, bd went to charge his phone. 
*
  He was about to go to bed when Mr. Jones called to inform him that his presence was needed at his father’s living room. After their last confrontation, he has been trying to avoid either of his parents. Resigning from work was out of the blues, but he was willing to do anything to escape the engagement at that point. Upon returning to his room, he regretted splitting that out, so he has been avoiding his parents since then. 

  He was aware than refusing to meet his father would be a bad idea, so he went to his father’s living room but he wasn’t there, so he went to his study instead. 

  After he placed a soft knock on the door, a vague response from echoed from inside, and he went in. 

    “I’ll give you just a week to turn in your resignation letter!” Mr. Russ blurted, and Micheal flinched in fear. He was dauntless about quitting, but now he felt intimidated by the potential consequences. 

   “Ok, dad,” were the only words that escaped his mouth. 

   “Are you resolute about your decision?”

  “Dad… u was with Ava, and we broke up. We had our reasons for breaking up,”

   “I heard that you suffered a heartache after she left, if you love her that much then getting back together shouldn’t be a problem,”

   “I loved her, but I moved on. I don’t want to return to the trash I dumped,” he said, and Mr. Russ chuckled. 

   “What happens when you realized you dumped a treasure along with the trash,”

   “I was careful when I trashed it, and I didn’t lose anything,”

   “Mike… you’ve been a businessman since you were a child. ‘Catch them young’, that was the goal, and you’ve been impressive until now. There is something you’ve to understand about businessmen. You’ve to turn around, and take another look after dumping something, why? There could be something I might need from what I dumped, did I made the right decision? That’s it, Mike. The Packard’s and Stanford’s only has a daughter who is the heiress to a big fortune. Is that who you called trash? Weren’t you thinking before dating her?”

  “I’m sorry, dad, but it’s not just Ava,”

   “Then who?!” Mr. Russ snapped. 

   “I…” he shuddered. 

   “It’s ok. It doesn’t have to be her,” Mr. Russ said, and Alvin looked at him waiting to hear his alternatives. “Theo Stanford’s already agreed to the engagement, and we’ve to turn them down, although we proposed it first. You seem determined, so I won’t push further. That’s a list of potential candidates that can replace the Stanford’s daughter. It’s a perilous idea, but your mother came up with it, so I’m sure she would be able to counterattack the consequences,” he said, and gave Micheal a drafted list. “You can either be with any of those listed there, or go for the Stanford’s daughter. There is too many things on the line, but choosing any of those candidates is our only weapon. The Stanford would likely give up if any of those family are involved. But the Stanford’s daughter is still our best choice. You’ve until this weekend to turn in your resignation letter, or choose any of those,” Mr. Russ said, and Micheal exhaled heavily. 
*
 He returned to his room and checked on all names. He look at really know any of them expect the Lockwood’s daughter, Jessica. 

  “Does the Holt have a teenage daughter?” He thought 

  He sent all the names to Stanley to help him confirm if they have a daughter, and Stanley replied after about half an hour with the names of their daughters, and pictures. 

   💬* The Alexander has a daughter in grade 8, and she resides in France, the Holt has a daughter who is in her second year in college, the Blakes has a disabled daughter who is a teenager, and the current chairwoman of Downs’s group who is in her late 20s,*

   “What? Am I a joke to dad?” Micheal shouted after reading Stanley’s message. 

   💬*Why are you interested in them?* Stanley texted, but he couldn’t reply. 

   He knows from the start that his parents can never approve his relationship with Paulina, and that has been his fear from the start. He was hoping on going public with her after he gain more power, but the sudden engagement was a barrier to his plans. 

  He put the list away, and went to freshen up not intending to think about anything. 

  After he freshened up, he went to stand by the window to stare at the moon, and his lips curved in a smile. 

   “Staring at the moon is indeed the best therapy,” he muttered, slipping from his glass of wine. 

  He heard a knock on the door, and he thought it was Mr. Jones because he is the only person who has the audacity to knock at his door, but the knocking continued, and she went to the door angrily —Mr. Jones would have given him a call, he only knocks if he was unable to reach him. Getting to the door, he opened it widely and saw the new chef there to serve him dinner. 

    “Good evening, sir. I brought your dinner,”

    “Did I request for it?” He blurted. 

   “It’s past your dinner time, sir,” she muttered. 

  His other staffs around, bowed their head and he exhaled heavily before slamming the door to her face. 
*
  During classes the following day, he couldn’t even glance at Paulina, the thought that the universe would never approve thier relationship was really overwhelming. When it was time for recess, she hurried out of the class and his heart sank at the thought that she was hurrying to Sage, so he dragged her back. As the day fly by, she left without declining his offer to give her a ride and he watched her from afar leaving the school with Sage. 

   “Why don’t you tell her?” Stanley asked him from behind, starling him. 

   “When did you got here? Where are the others?”

   “Austin refused to leave until he ace the game, and Steven isn’t giving up either,”

   “So, why are you here?”

   “To talk to you. I also know it can’t work, and it would never work. Even if you won’t be with Ava, it can never be her but you can still give it a shot. We only live once, man. Make yourself happy while it last, and worry about the consequences later,” Stanley said, and Micheal looked at him. 

    “Should I really do it?” 

    “You’ve to stay true to yourself, man. You advise Austin and I to find a girl, you shouod dos same to and be with someone who will make you happy even if it’s for a short period. You should at least make yourself happy before you marry any of those candidates I search on,” Stanley advised, and Micheal looked at him. 

   “Can you back into people’s mind?” He inquired, his curiosity piqued.

   “I wish I could,” Stanley muttered, and winked before leaving. 

  Everyone left school but Micheal couldn’t bring himself to leave. He was lost in his thoughts, his parents can never approve Paulina no matter how hard he try, it would only work if she can be reborn from an influential conglomerate family which can is impossible. 
    
     “You should really do it, Mike. You should enjoy it while it last and worry about the consequences later,” he thought, and sent a message to Paulina. 
*
 Paulina stared at him intensively, and he curious wait for her respond but her ringing phone made her look away, and she immediately picked the  call without checking the caller. 

   📞*Hello,*
   📞*Oh, Sage,” she muttered, and Micheal looked at her.

   “Lina, it’s getting late, we shouod get going,” he shouted, and she looked at him. 

  📞*Yeah, I stepped out. I’ll give you a call when I get home,* with that she dropped the call. 

  She released a deep breath, and as she met Michael’s gaze, his smile brought a slight smile to her face.
     
   “Let’s go,” she muttered, and he nodded. 
*
  He pulled over at the Stanford’s mansion, and held her hand before she could alight from the car. 

   “I’m willing to wait, Lina, if you won’t make me wait forever. But henceforth, I want you to consider my genuine feelings, because they are real,”

   “Since when, Mike?” she asked, leaving him speechless. 

   “I don’t know,” he whispered, his voice barely above a whisper, but she managed to hear. 

   “Ok,” she muttered, and get down from the car. 
*
  She went to her room and laid flat on the bed in despair, she took her phone and contemplated on sending a message to Steven but thought against it. 

  She think for a while but decide to take her mind off it by going to talk to her father. He promised to take her to visit her grandfather, but they haven’t had another conversation since then. 

  She went to his study and placed a knock on the door but his angry response from inside scared her, and she almost thought of retreating. 

   “Dad… it’s me, Paulina,” she shuddered, the door almost immediately, and she met a smiley face contrary to what she expected. 

   “Lina, let’s talk in the living room,” he said in excitement, and a bright smile spread across her face as she nodded. 
*
  They went to the living room, and he gave her a cup of coffee. 

   “Thank you,” she muttered, and sipped from the cup coffee. 

  “How has school been? I’ve been really busy lately, that I didn’t have time to check up on you,” 
  
   “I would’ve imagined,” she muttered, and he smiled. 

   “Is there something you want to talk about?”

   “Dad… you promised to take me to visit my grandfather…”

   “Oh, yes. I did promise that, but there is a good news and a bad one,”

   “What’s the news dad?” She asked, already curious. 

   “Your grandfather has always been out of prison,”

   “Really? That’s good news,” she said in excitement. “And the bad?” She muttered, already anxious. 

   “I don’t know his whereabouts. I sent people out to search for him, but they haven’t report back to me,”he said, and Paulina’s face fell. “Don’t worry, Lina, we are going to find him, and once I do, I’ll make sure I send you to him,”

   “You will do that?” 

   “I know that you aren’t comfortable staying here, but there is nothing I can do about it, but thinking about it now, I realized that it did be best for you to be with your grandfather,”

   “Thanks a lot, dad. I’d appreciate that,”

  “Don’t mention it, my child,” he said, and she smiled. 
*
  Ava who went to her father’s living room intending to talk him into sending her back to England. After giving it all a thought, she decide that being away was the best way to get over the embarrassment she received at school. She felt  too ashamed to go to school, neither did her friends cared to check up on her. She decided that leaving could be best, and her father will be easier to convince. But upon getting there, and saw him and Paulina together vent her anger. She made a fist and stormed away. 

   “Turns out leaving won’t do me any good. That bltch will take my place in this house, and at school. I won’t let her take everything from me,” she muttered in determination, and burst out of the house to take the step she should have taken earlier —consulting her grandfather. 
*
  She ordered her driver to drive her to her grandfather’s house, no one else but him can help her smoothly get everything she desire. 
*
  She got to her grandfather’s mansion and was directed to one of his garden, there he was disciplining someone. 

    “You want to write articles, right?” Mr. Packard teased, stepping on the bruised man lying flat on the ground. 

   “Sir… I’m sorry…” the man cried. 

   “Don’t be sorry. I’m going to give you more things to write on. We could even make others write about you, what matters is someone is writing,” he teased, and stepped on the man’s head. 

    “Sir… have mercy, I’ve a family,” he cried. 

   “I know, do you want them to join you?” He sneered hitting the man’s stomach and the weak man groaned in pain. 

   One of his bodyguards whispered words to his ear, and he turned to Ava who was standing far away from them. 

  “It’s your lucky day, my granddaughter is here. Ava, wait for grandpa in the living room, Ive to wrap up my meeting here,” he shouted, and Ava nodded. 
*
  She went to his private living room, and waited for him. She is used to seeing events like that whenever she visits him uninvited with her mother, that’s why she feared him a lot. The first time she saw him break a table on a someone’s head was when she was 10 years old, she couldn’t contain her fear and the event replayed on her head for days, giving her nightmares. 

  She waited for half an hour, and he joined her in the living room after he freshened up. 

    “Ava, to what do I owe this surprise visit?”

   “Grandpa, I adore you so much because you always have a solution to all my problems,” she said, and he chuckled. 

   “The solution lies within you, my child, you just have to find it,”

   “I feel like I would lose everything expect you,” 

  “You should desire more things, not lose what you’ve,”

   “That’s how it should be grandpa, but I’ll still lose everything soon,”

    “To her?” Her grandfather asked, and she looked at her perplexed. 

    “Who?”

   “Your father’s illegitimate daughter,” he blurted, and she gasped. 

    “How did you know about her?” She asked, her curiously piqued. Her mother warned her to keep Paulina’s existence away from her grandfather, so there is no way her mother could tell him, but she couldn’t be so surprised, her grandfather always have a way in fishing out informations on his own, and finding solutions. 

   “How won’t I know someone who is living under my nose? She has an identical disgusting face as her mother,” he said. 

   “Do you also know her mother? Why did you allow it happen, grandpa? Why did you allow dad have a mistress and even went as far as bringing his daughter home?”

   “Because we could always get her out of the way,” he said, and Ava chuckled. 

   “Not just her, grandpa,”

  “And the Russ’s son?”

   “How do you know?”

   “I know a lot of things than you can imagine,” he said, and she beamed. 

    “How do I do that, grandpa? All my efforts proves abortive,”

    “We will just put them where they belong. The Russ’s son next to you, and the illegitimate daughter out of the way. History will have to repeat itself,”

    “What history?”

   “A beautiful history that happened before you were born. This conversation reminds me of a similar situation from years ago,” he said, and remembered what happened years ago. 
*
*
 FLASHBACK to years ago***
*
 Grace broke down in tears in her father’s living room, and her father snapped at her. 

    “Are you literally crying over a guy?” He shouted. 

   “It can only be Paul, father… I love him, but that bltch seduced him… he would never be mine,” she cried. 

    “You will return to school, and continue your life, make new friends, move on from him, and I will sort out the rest,”

    “How would that work?” She cried. 

   “Trust me, my child. Paul is yours, he has always been yours from the start. You will marry him, and first group will be ours,” he said, and her face brightened up immediately. 
*
*
  FLASHBACK ENDS***
*
  Mr. Packard smiled wickedly, and looked at Ava who gave him a confused look. 

   “We will get the obstacles out of our way, my child, and Russ group will be ours. I’ll make you the queen, you will take over first group, and Russ group and I’ll make you the president over them all,” he said, and Ava smirked. 

   “Thank you, grandpa,” she muttered, and he smiled. 
.

 LOUIS’S HOUSE^^^
^
  Charlotte woke up from a nightmare, and tried to catch her breath before going out to receive some fresh air, there she met her father in the garden staring at the moon. 

    “Dad, why are you out here?” She asked, and he turned to look at her with a smile. 

   “You’re also up,” he muttered. 

   “I want to get some air,” 

   “I was unable to sleep,” he said, exhaling heavily. 

  “What’s bothering you, dad?”

  “How can I get back at the Packard’s? How can I get rid of Mr. Packard? How can I dig into his dirty past and send him to b£ll? All those thoughts are bothering me,”

   “Dad, you aren’t planning to do anything illegal, right?”

   “Charlotte, beaten once, must never be beaten twice. Our family didn’t fall apart because I did something illegal, our family fell apart because I trusted the wrong person,” 

   “Dad, please don’t. I don’t want you ti get involved in anything illegal. All I want is to get my daughter back, and we should all leave together. Can you do that please?” She pleaded, holding his hands. 

    “How will you do that? How do you plan to get your daughter back? You can’t have her back easily, and you gang even remember who tried to get rid of you. So, how do you plan to it that, Charlotte? I don’t care about going back to prison, must we must do what has to be done,” 

   “Dad….”

  “We can do that, without anyone going to prison,” Louis said, joining them. They didn’t notice his presence until he made it known. 

   “Louis, don’t tell me you’re in on this as well?” 

   “Louis, you’ve done more than enough for us that we can’t repay, but allow me do this on my own, it’s my right to do it. The Packard’s destroyed my family, I shouod repay that, he sent me to jail, I owe them that as well. It’s an eyes for two eyes, I’m going to repay what I owe in ten folds. Charlotte attempted suicide, and was separated from her daughter, they deserve to suffer the same pain,” 

   “Louis, please talk him about of this,”

   “He is right, Charlotte. Someone has to stop them. Uncle Joffrey (Mr. Packard) has always been scary since I was a child, I’m sure he is upto something unclean. We will expose them sir, and make them face justice the right way. If you really want to repay my kindness, you will do this. Let’s do what has to be done….the right way,” 

    “Louis, you don’t I how this things work. You can never get rid of someone powerful the right way,”

   “Let’s give my idea a try sir. Just until the electron is over, let’s try to expose them before then and get Paulina back, and if we fail, you can do it all your way,” Louis said, and Mr. Ethan smiled. 

   “Let’s see how it goes,” Mr. Ethan muttered. 

   “Dad, you’ve to promise me. I’ll denounce you as my father if you do anything illegal again,”

   “I’m not worthy to be your father if I can’t protect you and your child,” with that he went inside. 

   “Dad…” 

   “Charlotte. Leave him alone. We’re going to solve this the right way, and get Paulina back,” Louis assured, and he embraced him in tears. 
*
   Charlotte woke up the following day, and went downstairs to meet her father and Louis together in the living room. 

   “Good morning,” she greeted, and they both responded in unison. 

   “What are you both discussing this morning?” She asked, sitting next to her father on the long couch. 

    “Nothing much,” Louis was quick to say. 

  Charlotte picked up the note on the table and look at glance confused. 

     “That is the list of plate numbers that always give Paulina a ride from school, your father wrote those down,”

   “My daughter? Is she safe with them?”

  “She has really good friends, Charlotte. I’ve been keeping a close watch on her, and she seems to really enjoy their company a lot,”

    “Paulina doesn’t keep much friends. She only had two friends till she graduated from elementary school, and just a friend in grade school. I hope she is safe with them,” Charlotte muttered. 

    “Her closer friend seems to be a from the Greenwood’s family. They only have a teenage son, so it could only be him. Sage Greenwood. The only son of Chairman Greenwood, and only grandson of the world best lawyer,” Louis said, and Charlotte looked at her. 

    “Paulina is close to such person?” Charlotte gasped. 

   “Holland high is for children from prestigious family. The families of the students has to donate huge amounts for thier children to be able to attend that school,” 

   “Why is Paulina in such school?” Charlotte asked 

   “Paul probably enrolled her there,”

   “Don’t worry, Paulina is doing well at school, and she has good friends too,” Mr. Ethan assured. 

   “Who are the others?” Charlotte asked. 

  “This one belongs to the Russ families, so he is probably Micheal Russ, the only son of Russ family. He is a young director. He is probably a good kid,” Louis said. 

   “Paulina seems to he closer to him lately. It was the first guy from the start,”

   “And the last one?”

   “He is from the Wilson’s family, he is the only heir of Will’s group. Don’t worry, Paulina is safe with them,” Louis said. 

   “How did Paulina managed to make so many friends?” She grumbled. 

   “She has other friends,” Mr. Ethan said. 

    “Other friends?” Charlotte and Louis asked. 

   “A female friend from school, they seem to be mutual friends with that Greenwood, and a boy from school who works part-time at a noodle restaurant. She goes there every weekend to hang out with her friend, and also hang around there at times during the week,”Mr. Ethan said. 

   “Wow. Paulina seems to be living a good life,” 

   “You see that Charlotte, so be rest assured now that Paulina is doing fine,” Louis exclaimed, and she nodded in tears. 

   “I want to see my daughter,” she cried. 

  “We can’t do that, it’s dangerous for you to show yourself to her,” Mr. Ethan said. 

  “We can do it, but discreetly, Louis said, and they both looked at him. 

   “You’re right. We can go to that noodles restaurant during the weekend, and see her. She goes there every weekend,” Mr. Ethan said. 

  “Thank you, dad, thanks for looking after my daughter,”

   “That’s the only thing I can do for her. I can’t  even show myself to her. She has such a humble manner, she is just like you, Charlotte. Despite being raised by a father like me, you managed to grow up well on your own, and I’m glad you raised your daughter well,” he said, and she smiled. 

   “I’m sure it will all be over soon,” Louis said, and they nodded. 

   “Thank you for everything, Louis,” Charlotte muttered. 

   “Don’t thank me yet, when everything is over, we should all have a meal together with Paulina, it would be your treat, and a way to thank me,” he said, and Charlotte smiled.

School has really been stressful, with Sage and Micheal’s sudden engagement, and their teaching system at Holland high was different from fhe one she was used to.

Theo  week ended, and she prepared to leave the house on Saturday to have breakfast at the noddles restaurant.

  Go find out yourself,” he whispered in her ear, and she glared at him.

 She shove the thoughts away and finished her meal before going to Mr. Ethan. She doesn’t know why she went to him, but she just felt it’s proper to exchange greetings since she always see him in the bus station.

  HOLLAND HIGH ^^^

  Students started arriving at school, and were all welcomed by the mid-semester examination memo and grumbling about it.

  Paulina sat at the cafeteria rooftop, thinking about Sage. He invited her out the other day, and she spent all her Sunday with him, after a fun filled day, he confessed to her again, and just like the last time, she was speechless with just her heart dancing in excitement. She enjoyed Sage’s company a lot, and felt safe around him, likewise Micheal so she was totally confused. 

    “Why does my heart beat for two men?” She thought. 

   “Because they are both special to you,” she heard Sophia said, joining her and she instantly realized that she said that out loud. 

   “Sophie…”

   “I might not be experienced in love, in fact I don’t want to fall in love. I dated my ex boyfriend because he was from an influential family, but we broke up after few months. I might not be experienced in love, but I’m sure you can only love one person,”

   “How do you know when you love someone?”

   “You will feel safe around them, time flies whenever you are together, and even the little things makes you happy,” 

    “What if I feel that way for two people?”

    “Then… you’ve to focus. Your case is a bit new and serious, but let’s try if this will work. If those two people are Sage and Mike, then imagine being in a world where only of them exist. Exclude the benefits you get from them, and only imagine the time you spend with them, which one do you enjoy the most. I believe that should work,” Sophia said, and Paulina closed her eyes.

  Remembering her first meeting with Sage, and how they ended up as friends, and back to Micheal, comparing them both, she opened her eyes and realized that the problem was from her.

    “I think I enjoy Mike’s company because it helps me escape reality, but I always want to be honest with Sage. He is such a good person, and he deserves to be treated batter,” she muttered, and Sophia patted her back. 

   “And if you are honest with the both of them, which one do you think you want to be mostly?” Sophia asked, and she closed her eyes again. 

   “It’s Sage,” she muttered, and Sophia gasped. 

   “Have you gotten your answers now?” Sophia asked, and she shook her head. 

   “But I don’t think I’m good enough for him,” she muttered because she knows that she can never be honest with him.

I know I always recommend Mike, I mean… who wouldn’t? But Sage is a good person, you can never find a good guy like him. Even Mike can’t compete with him,” Sophia advised, and Paulina bowed her head in thoughts.

  Paulina followed the MASS to thier suite later added school to work on thier club activity. After doing that for almost an hour, they retired to chattering about random stuffs. 

   “I want to format my laptop,” Austin said, pressing his laptop.

  The other left, and Micheal cleared his throat aloud looking at Paulina who didn’t move an inch.

   “No, you can’t be Mikey, it just doesn’t make sense,” she muttered, and he bowed his head in guilt.

  Micheal bowed his head speechlessly, unsure of how to answer her question. He knew from the moment he was her that she was his childhood friend, Paulina. She never changed from how she was from ten years ago. He was surprised at first that she didn’t recognize him but putting it all together, they have only been friends for few weeks, while he has known her for a long time, although they aren’t friends then. He decided to keep things the way they were, he already made a promise never to return to his past, so he pretended like she didn’t exist. Even though, he wanted things for remain the way they were, he couldn’t just sit still and watch Ava bully her. She has always been bullied since elementary school because she was a nerd, and likely an introvert. She never stand up for herself, and even now, Ava just walked over her. He became frustrated at the thought, and ensured to attend Lia’a last party to find a way to rescue her from whatever Ava was plotting, but fate had other plans, and he ended up entangled in their plans, building a connection between them. From that moment, he made up his mind to be her hero again, but a real hero this time. She has always been special to him singe they were children, and seeing her again remained him of his cute friend who is only chatty with those she is comfortable with. There was no way he could show himself as her childhood friend because he would have to explain how it all came about to her, so being the Micheal he is now, and protecting her in his own way in form of an agreement was the only way, until he unknowingly fell for her. He knew from the start that he was attracted to her, but he thought it was bonded by the relationship between them in the past, but now it was crystal clear, he love her and was willing to go through any length to protect her, but revealing his past as her childhood friend was never a part of his plans. 
.
   “Mike, are you just going to stay quiet?” She snapped, and he looked at her immediately. 

   “Lina… there is a long story behind it,”

   “How is it even possible? No, how long have you known that you were Mikey,”

    “From the first day I was you. When I came to school that morning, and saw Ava bullying you, I knew from then, that was why I got involved,” he said, and she scoffed in disbelief. 

   “You’ve knew for that long, and you didn’t tell me. You even got angry whenever I compare you to Mikey. How does these makes sense, Mike?”

   “Because I like the way things are now. I don’t have to be Mikey to be friends with you. We are cool even now,”

   “You can say all that because you aren’t the one deceived. You called is friends and you kept something so important from me. How is all that even possible, Mike? How can my childhood friend who always go fishing with me became an elite over night?”

   “It all happened within a blink, that’s why I couldn’t tell you. There is a story behind it,@

   “That’s enough. I don’t want to hear any words from you. You always use every circumstance to your advantage even when you Mikey, and now, you still haven’t changed. I don’t want to hear any of your stories. You lied to me from the start about being Mikey, so how sure am I that you will be honest with me now?” With that she stormed out, and he ran his fingers through his hair in frustration. 
*
  Paulina ran out, and bumped into Sage on way to the parking lot. 

   “Line, are you ok?” He asked, and she looked at him. 

  “Do I look ok?” She muttered, and he embraced her. 
*
  He lead her to his car, and gave her a bottle of water. 

   “Thank you, Sage,” she muttered, and sip from the bottle. 

    “What happened to you?” He asked, and she shook her head. 

   “Why are you still at school?” She asked back. 

   “I forgot to return a book to the library, so I went to do that. Let me give you a ride home,” he said, and she nodded. 
*
  She asked him to drop her at the bus station, and she board a bus head in to the noddles restaurant. She alighted from the bus and took a taxi to the noodle restaurant, but she saw the delivery bike. Wondering if he was out to deliver, she eyesearched, and saw him surrounded by thugs. 

   “What? Steven. Sir, can you reverse?” She asked the taxi driver who drove back, and she dropped at the scene. 

  She ran down from the taxi, heading to where Steven was. Even though there was nothing she could do, she can’t sit by and watch her friend being bullied. 

    “Steven,” she called running up to him, and everyone around looked at her —they looked more like gangsters than thugs, and she flinched in fear but managed to compose herself. 

    “No, no, Lina, stay away,” Steven muttered,in a low whisper so she didn’t hear him. 

  She find her way to him, and rushed to hold him glaring at the scary looking men like she got something on her. 

    “I see… you found yourself a lover girl,” the man who looked like thier leader sneered, and Steven held her hand to hid behind him 

   “Don’t you dare hurt her!” He blurted, and Paulina started to panic. She has never seen Steven that angry before, and being caught in a scene like that was also beyond her imagination. 

   “I… I’m going to call thee police,” she shuddered, and her hands trembled as she hold her phone but Steven collected the phone from her. 

 The man chuckled, and left in his car while the others followed. 

  “Lina, are you ok?” He asked, and she looked at him, collecting her phone from him. 

   “I should be asking you that,”

   “I can take care of myself. Why did you get involved?” He lashed.
