   “Following its name, study room, that could only be its purpose,” she said, and he smiled. 

   “Why aren’t you asking me anything?” He asked later, and she looked at him. 

   “What?” She muttered, feigning ignorance because she knew he was referring to the news about him. 

    “Stop feigning ignorance, the entire school are talking about it,”

    “I know you have no choice. There is a scandal about you, so I believe you are probably doing this for a reason,” she said, following the contents of the note. 

    “Yes, I’m sorry. My true identity is my weakness.  It could jeopardize my future, and everything I’ve worked so hard for will be in vain. We live in a society where being an illegitimate is a crime,”

    “If being with Ava will be good for you, then you should do it,” she said, and he looked at her. 

    “What about us?” He muttered, and she held his hand. 

    “I’m still here, Mike, we will always be friends,” she assured and he shook his head. 

    “Just until the rumors about me dle down, just until I don’t need her anymore, just until I can stand on my own, just until we graduate from high school. I really don’t know, Lina, but will you still be there?” He muttered, and she nodded. 

    “Yes, Mike, I’ll still be here. I believe you will gather the strength needed to stand firmly on your own. You are right, we live in a society where being an illegitimate makes you an outcast,” she muttered, and he embraced her. 

    “Thank you, Lina, I promise to get this sorted out soon, I promise,” he muttered, and she nodded. 
*
  They sat together in his study ik silence, she could instantly tell that he didn’t know how to face his friends. 

    “Why did you broke up with Ava? I heard that you were head over heels for her,” 

    “I was, but she broke up with me and I have no feelings for her anymore,”

   “Why did she broke up with you?” She asked, and he looked at her 

    “She called me a piece of trash, and broke up with me,”

    “Why? Why would she call you such?”

   “I was also confused, so I kept pestering her until she released the bombshell. She found out about my biography, and dumped me because we can’t be in the same standard. That’s what she had against me, and that’s why she broke up with me,” he said, and she gasped. 

    “That must’ve been really painful,”

   “I was heartbroken because she left me for being an illegitimate, not because I was affected by her absence. I instantly realized that no one will ever accept me for who I am, I’ve to live on with no who knowing about my past. That was why I was hurt, she left me for being me,” 

    “How did she found out?” She asked, perplexed. 

   “Through an anonymous informant,”

   “Who could that be? That means someone else is aware of your true identity,”

    “Just me, my parents, and of course my biological mother. And now, Ava, and you. So who could the informant be?”

   “Do you think it’s your mother?” She asked, and he shook his head. 

   “It was me,”

   “What?”

   “I was feeling overwhelmed by everything. I’ve to live like this for the rest of my life when I’m not  even a real elite. I wanted to be true to myself for once. There is no way I could tell my friends, so I think of the best way to tell her, and sent it anonymously. Surprisingly, she reacted to it almost immediately. She called me a piece of trash, and left. But I still refuse to believe that she left me because of that, so I became persistent bug that was it. She threaten to expose me if I ever go close to her, and with that we went our separate paths. She went to England, and I continued living my life,”

    “Oh… you must’ve really loved her for you to confidently send that to her,”

    “I guess I did. She was my first. Since I became Micheal Russ, I devoted myself to being strong so I can win against my mother, and I can only do that if I was above her, so I worked really hard. I thought it was fate when we first met, she smiled so brightly, and for the first time, I met a girl who wasn’t drooling over me. She was dauntless, and filled with vigor, so had a lot of admirers, and they were always intimidated by her presence. All those qualities got me attached to her, I guess I was already used to the aura my mother processes, so I got carried away by that and found myself falling for her strength. Ava is really scared of no one, I bet you know better,” he said, and she nodded. 

   “ I wonder why she changed her mind about the engagement,”

   “Because she wants me to be her puppet. She thought that’s all I worth, that’s why I would never drag myself to that position,” 

    “If you liked Ava because of her strength, then why do you like me? You clearly like strong women, and I’m not fit for that,”

    “When we were younger, others bully you for being weak, so I thought of a way to protect you from them, and we eventually became friends. I can’t say my feelings develop then, because we were just kids and innocent. We enjoyed each other’s company and that’s all it was. But when we met again, when Ava bullied you on your first day, I was happy to see my long friend again, and I was frightened that I might not be able to protect you this time. I could say it all started because i wanted you to be safe. You were cast out and stepped on because you were different from them, but you were not different from me. We’re both outcast in this school that represents a world on its own. That was it, I fell for you while trying to protect you because we are both the same,” he said, and she chuckled. 

    “I don’t know if I should drool over that. Or, shouod I applaud m myself for being weak?” She teased, and he chuckled. 

   “I don’t care about strength anymore, I was just carried away by stress and think I could release some stress with her. But I was wrong, I was even more stressed when we started dating,” he said, and she scoffed. 

    “We shouod go join the others,” she said, and he gestured in agreement. 
*
  They went to join the others who seem to be having a serious conversation. 

    “What’s wrong?” Micheal asked. 

   “Austin might have to quit basketball,” Stanley muttered. 

    “What? What happened man? Are you sick? Did you hurt your back or your legs? Do you need surgery? I’ll get the best doctor in the world to fix it, you can’t quit. Not now, not until you graduate to work at your company. You can’t quit now!” Micheal said, and they all looked at him. 

   “I never said I would!” Austin blurted. 

   “Then what’s Stanley saying?”

   “His father threatened to ground him from basketball,” Stanley said. 

    “What nonsense! Is it because of your attendance? Is he at it again? You guys shouldn’t have waited for me, you should have gone ahead to class,”

    “If the penalty of skipping classes will cost me basketball, I would be the first in class every morning. I don’t love you guys that much,” Austin  said. 

   “Did he complain about your lavish spending?” Micheal asked. 

   “Then he should have just seized my cards,” Austin muttered. 

    “Then what the f¥ck happened?!” Micheal snapped, starling Paulina. 

    “He has to get at least 20% on his mid-semester examination, else he will be grounded,” Stanley said. 

   “Have you thought of another sport you like? You are quite the athlete,” mifheal said, and they all chuckled expect Austin who glared at him. 

   “I’m not giving up on basketball, never! I’m going to win a trophy in the next tournament,” with that he burst out. 

    “Man, you are too harsh,” Stanley said unable to control his laughters. 

   “He is barely managing to maintain his place at the bottom, how do you expect him to fly to 20% when the mid-semester examination is just around the corners,” 

    “It’s really easy for a bottom student to do 20% better than a top student,” Paulina said. 

    “In that case, it’s easily for a pauper to become an elite than Austin to do 20% better,” Stanley said. 

 Paulina and Micheal steal glances at each other before looking away nervously. 

  “That being said… guys, I think I’ve to buckle my shoes. I’m starting to slip,” Stanley said, and they both looked at him. 

   “Slip, as how?” Micheal asked. 

   “Man, this is really serious. I did a mock mathematics exam last night, it was an hour exam, but I barely managed to answer the questions within the time limit. I even guess some of the answers, I didn’t have enough time to solve them. Man, I’m do0med!” He said, Micheal exhaled heavily. 

   “What sort of exam is that?” 

   “It was just for fun. It’s for master’s students, I applied for the mock exams online for fun, turns out everyone could apply. But I couldn’t even meet the target,” 

   “Master’s exam?” Micheal repeated. 

   “That’s not the point, man. It was a math exam,” he whined, and Micheal shook his head before patting Stanley’s shoulder. 

     “I had enough of you, guys,” Micheal grumbled, and let out a heavy sigh. 

    “I think I’ve a way out,” Stanley exclaimed, and they both looked at him. 

    “I actually hope it’s a good way, so you would do better in the next professor’s exam,” Micheal said. 

    “This is my way out,” he muttered, and brought out a card. 

    “Isn’t that the card to the maths club?” Paulina asked. 

   “This is my last shot,” 

   “But Jessica would never approve you,” Michael said. 

  “I got it all planned, so just wish me luck,”

   “Seems like you got it all planned out before now,” Micheal exclaimed. 

   “I need to sort out myself, I really need to do this,” he whined, and left. 

    “Do you think he is really going to the math club?” Paulina asked, and Micheal shook his head. 

    “No. He is going to wait for Austin to convince him. I had enough of those guys,” Micheal exclaimed.

 IN AN UNKNOWN PLACE^^^
^
 Mr. Ethan crossed his legs in a dark room, and he turned on the light when he heard footsteps approaching. 

   “Boss…”. A man called upon seeing him, and he immediately fell to his knees. 

   “Don… you… you got nothing off your sleeves, you don’t even have guts, so why could you betray me?”

  “Boss… I… I was threaten, I confessed to you that I was threatened. My brother is the only family I had, and he was abducted by Mr. Packard, my brother just wanted to have a good life, he just wanted to study and be a doctor. He is weak, and scared. I had to save him,”

    “You said so. And that made you weaker,” Mr. Ethan said, and patted his shoulder. “What shouod I do with you, Don? You betrayed me, I lost my father, and almost lost my daughter. It was all your fault, Don. I lost two, while you were trying to save one. That wasn’t your promise to me when I took you in back then,” he said, and Don started to shiver, remembering the past. 
*
*
  FLASHBACK to when Don was a teenager***
*
   Don ran out of the stationary trying to escape those running after him when a car pulled up in the front of him. 

   “Get in, kid,” Mr. Ethan who was seated at the backseat said, and Don quickly entered the car when he saw a police van, and the car drove off in hurry, escaping the police. 
*
  The car drove nonstop even after they have escaped the police van, and Don started shivering in fear. 

   “Sir… where are you taking me?” He shuddered. 

   “I assume you are dauntless  that’s why you could steal, but turns out I was wrong. I’ve been observing you for while, but I can’t believe I got the wrong impression about you,”

    “Sir… I’m not a thief,”

   “You just got released from juvenile prison for theft. And now… you robbed a stationary. What’s this? A dictionary?”

   “My brother needs this. I didn’t intend to steal it,”

   “I know. We all have reasons for everything we do. From now henceforth, you won’t need to steal from others again, you will join me and we will make good fortunes together,”

   “What do you mean by that, sir?”

   “I mean… you would be able to afford everything your brother will ever want, and more,” he said, and Don eyes brightened. 
*
*
  FLASHBACK ENDS***
*
  Mr. Ethan chuckled looking at Don who was pleading. 

   “This isn’t how I raised you, Don,” 

   “I’m really sorry, sir. I’ve been repenting ever since,”  

   “Admiring to your sins, and pleading for mercy is for loser… because it can’t change anything.  How have you been repenting?”

   “Sir,”

   “What do you have on those Packard’s? That’s how to repent. It’s not called repentance without a weapon “

    “Sir… the Packard’s are really a strong and discreet family, I got nothing on them,” 

    “There you go lying again. I thought you have repenting, but you still have a long way to go. Why did I even summoned you here?”

    “Sir, I don’t want to drag you into this any longer. Mr. Packard… he is my enemy now. So allow me get back at him for you, if anyone had to go to jail… it should be me,”

   “Why should you go to jail? If anyone has to go to jail, then it should be the villain, we can only accompany them. So tell me, what do you have?”

   “I’ve been monitoring the Packard’s since you went to jail, and something suspicious happened,”

   “Now I’m interested,” Mr. Ethan said, patting his back and Don forced a smile. 
*
  He went to seat, and showed Mr. Ethan a picture of a man. 

   “Who is this?” Mr. Ethan asked. 

  “I don’t know. I dint know how is he is related to the Packard’s, but he is somewhat suspicious. Mr. Packard sent him to jail right after you went to jail, he was locked up in the detention centre without trail.”

  “What was his crime?” 

  “He drunk drive, and caused an accident,”

   “Oh…”
   
  “But he wasn’t the one driving the car. A lady was the driver but they both fell unconscious after the accident and the lady wasn’t found in the crime scene afterwards, and the car was also disposed

   “How sure are you?”
  
  “I was there. I coincidentally to witness it,”

   “Why are you telling me this?” Mr. Ethan asked, yawning loudly. 

   “Because he could be the Packard weakness,”

   “How? I’m getting bored,”

   “He got released years ago, he was lucky to meet a mafia boss who offered him a job in jail and helped him out, but he has been stalking the Packard’s since then. Most especially Mr. Packard’s granddaughter. She noticed at a point, and had a confrontation with him before she left the country. She left, after her last confrontation with him,”

   “Wow! What do you assume?” Mr. Ethan exclaimed. 

   “I believe he has a connection with the Packard’s, a close connection. So I’ve been keeping a close watch on him since then. Recently, a reporter wrote a story about the accident from years ago, but the post got deleted, and the reporter went AWOL,” 

  “Don, this will be your final repentance. I prefer to work with a traitor because I know they might  betray me again. And you know me well, I like risking it all. Charlotte is so soft, she is just like her mother, and I’m glad she grew up that way, but because of that… I can’t take action the right way,”

   “Miss Charlotte is alive?”

  “I’m not done talking,”

  “I’m all ears, sir,”

   “Gosh… where was I? Yes. I wang you to bring this man to me in whatever you can, that will be your first assignment for your final repentance. If he really has something against the Packard’s, then I could use him while I fulfill my promise to Charlotte “ he said, and Don bowed.

  HOLLAND HIGH^^^
^
  Paulina met Sage by the elevator when school dismissed  later and she smiled upon seeing him. 

   They went to his car together, and he looked at her.  

   “Are you ok?” He asked, and she nodded. 

   “Sage, I know you have doubts about Mike with all those rumors and the engagement, but there is more to the story. I won’t ask you to trust him, and I won’t stop you from confessing either,”

    “You assume too much. Can’t I worry about you as a friend? You look so weary, and I was worried,”

      “Yeah… I was really stressed, with the mid-semester exam coming up, and the school teaching syllabus is different from the one I’m used to,”

     “You need to relax, that will help you prepare for the coming exam. The students might look unserious, but their grades shouldn’t be taken for granted,” he said, and she chuckled. 

   “Thank you, Sage,” 

   “To the bus station, or straight home?” 

   “I’ll find my way from the bus station,”

    “Why do you enjoy taking the bus?”

    “It’s faster. Taking the bus is faster than a taxi or a private ride,”

    “I doubt,”

   “You’ve never tried it before, so shhh…” she teased, and he chuckled. 
*
  They arrived at the bus station, and she thanked him before alighting from his car. 

  She went to seat to wait for the next bus when a familiar face walked up to her. He looks familiar and also unfamiliar. She tried to figure out where she have seen the face and instantly realized that he is the ragged man —he was now looking clean, his breeds were properly shaved, and he wore freshen and glean clothes. 

    “Hi, little miss,” he said, and she looked at him in shock wondering why he was talking to her—he acted like she doesn’t exist the last time, and now he is suddenly being friendly. But she put all the thoughts away, and compared herself. 

   “Good afternoon, sir. Are you also taking the bus?” She asked, and he shook his head. 

   “I’m not taking the bus, neither do I live around the neighborhood you saw me the last time. Avoid being friendly to everyone, not everyone appears the way they look. Have a nice day,” with that he stopped a taxi and left. 

    “What’s that about?” She grumbled, staring at the taxi until a bus arrived. 
*
  She found herself alighting at the station closet to the noodle’s restaurant, and tried to reassure herself that she wasn’t going there for Steven. 

    “Their noodles is very tasty, why should I miss it  because of him when I’ll get a free coupon soon,” she said, trying to reassure herself as she stopped a taxi. 

  She arrived at the noodles restaurant, and was surprised to meet it under construction.
   
    “Am I at the wrong place?” She pounded, and saw the owner of the restaurant. 
   “Oh, Mr., Mr.,” she called waving at him, and he smiled upon seeing her. 

  He approached her with a smile, and she smiled back. 
*
 They both went in together, and he narrated the unfortunate event that happened to her —their restaurant caught fire, and before they help could arrive, a lot of faculties were already damaged. 

   “Oh… I’m so sorry about that,”

   “It’s fine. I’m glad that you dropped by. A lot of regular customers have been coming around. But we will be back to business soon. I hope Steven has been good?”

    “Steven? Isn’t he here,”

   “He left the day before yesterday. It was a relief that he wasn’t around when the incident happened,”

    “He didn’t come since he left?”

   “No, he didn’t. He is just a part-time worker. He started working here recently. He is such a diligent lad, I don’t pay him much, he only receive a tip in addition to staying here.  Even though it wasn’t convenient,”

    “What? Steven lives here?”

   “Yes. Aren’t you aware?”

   “We recently became friends, so we haven’t exchanged that much information,”

    “Oh… so you also know nothing about him,”

   “I guess,”

   “I believe he has some situation at home back then. He should have returned home by now. Finding a part-timer as diligent as him will be probably impossible,”
*
  The man prepared noddles for her, and she ate before leaving, thinking about Steven. 

    “How could he disappear from the noodles restaurant as well?” She thought, and started wondering if those thugs went after him again after she left. 
*
*
  The week fly by so fast, during the days that passed, the students stop talking about Michael’s engagement and the rumors, and the groundless article who brought down. During those days, Steven has been absent and his friends were equally worried about him. They tried all means to reach out to him, but he seems to have disappeared completely. 

  Paulina found herself sending him a text on Saturday morning. She was unable to sleep well all night thinking about him and what we could have happened to him. 

   “It was my fault, I shouldn’t have left when he asked me to,” she thought, staring at her phone screen. 

 A message beeped in from Sage to enlighten her mood and she hurried out of bed to freshen up. He already invited her in advance to go fishing with his family that Saturday, and she was so excited about it. The thoughts of going fishing reminded her of the time she spent with her mother, and she started missing those moments again. 
*
  She went downstairs when Sage arrived, and he opened the car door for her. 

   “I’m sorry for delaying you,” she said, and he shook his head. 

   “I didn’t even wait a minute. You must’ve been excited,”

    “I am,” she exclaimed, and he chuckled. 

  “Getting this excited over fishing is making me wonder if there is actually something fun about it. My mum and sister are equally excited over it,”

    “Fishing is fun, especially when you catch something big,” she said, and they both laughed. 
*
  They arrived at the airport, and met his parents and sisters there. 

    “Lina,” Clara waved cheerfully, and Paulina waved back all smiles. 

   She exchanged greetings with his parents, and they left in their private jet. 

   “I didn’t know  we are traveling this far to fish,” Paulina said, and Sage chuckled. 

   “This is the hassle we go through to fish. Mum never settle for any place close by,” he whispered, and she chuckled. 
*
  They arrived around an island, and Clara dragged Paulina with her to take pictures while sage and his father sat thier tent, and Mrs. Greenwood was settling their fishing tools. 

   “Hey, Clara, you will run out if energy if you keep running about like that,” Mr. Greenwood shouted, but they ignored him. 

    “They seem to be having a blast,” Sage muttered, and his father laughed. 

    “Hey, girls, time to start fishing,” Mrs. Greenwood announced and they both joined her 

  The men sat behind watching them as they put in efforts to catch fishes. 

    “Is she your girlfriend now?” Mr. Greenwood asked Sage, and he shook his head. 

    “I wish she would accept my feelings,” 

   “She rejected you?” He asked with widened eyes. 

   “I still have three trials left,”

    “Why are you being so nice to her after she rejected you? You when made your mother adjust her fishing plans for you,”

    “Dad… am I supposed to hate her because she rejected me?”

    “I’m not advising you to, but can you watch her be with another man?” Mr. Greenwood asked. 

     “Dad… I still have three trials left. Wish me luck ahead,”

    “Is this your string to make her accept you?”

     “Bro, Lina caught a big fish!” Clara shouted, and Sage waved. 

     “She has been so stressed these days, and I know this would help her relive some stress. With the mid-semester exams coming up… this is just perfect,”

     “But you hate fishing,”

     “You blabber too much, dad,” Sage teased, and left to join the ladies.
*
  They catch a lot of fishes, and prepared dishes with them. 

    “Wow, it’s so delicious,” Mr. Greenwood said. 

   “You love fish dishes but can’t even fish ,” Mrs. Greenwood teased. 

     “You don’t have to fish to enjoy these, we could  just buy some at the market,” Sage defended. 

   “Then get your hands off that and go buy some at the market!” Clara blurted hitting his hands. 

    “Lina, you are really good at this,” Mrs. Greenwood said. 

    “I enjoy fishing,”

    “Then I should always invite you whenever I’m going fishing,”
 
     “With all pleasure, ma’am,”

     “Mum, we aren’t going to fish after the mid-semester exams anymore, right?” Sage inquired. 

    “Was that why you made us come now? Do you’ve plans after the exams?”

    “The break after the exam is very short, so here is nothing much to plan,”

    “Mum, I’m going on a trip with Sally and Didi. Their uncle will take us, we are visiting thier grandpa’s farm,” Clara said. 

    “That’s a nice adventure for your age,” Mrs. Greenwood stated. 

   “Won’t that be too dangerous? If they are visiting a farm, then it would be unsafe for kids,”
Mr. Greenwood protested 

    “Dad…” 

   “That’s enough. We will ensure it’s safe before sending her there. Sage, you should also do something after your exams,” Mrs. Greenwood said. 

    “Lina, do you have plans?” Mr. Greenwood asked. 

    “I haven’t thought about anything yet, sir,” she muttered. 

    “Good! Then you can just make plans with Sage. You both can go to Europe together. It’s a short break, so you should take advantage of it,”

    “Dad, why would we go to Europe together?” Sage snapped. 

    “You guys are friends, right? So, what’s bad with going on a short vacation with your friend?” 

    “Do friends do that these days?” Mrs. Greenwood teased. 

    “What? Shouod you go on a vacation with your enemy instead?” He blurted. 

   “Sage, I thought Lina is now your girlfriend,” Clara said, and Paulina looked at her. 

    “Shut your mouth, and eat up. And dad… no body said Lina and I will spend our break together,” Sage remarked, promoting Mr. Greenwood to raise his hands in surrender, and his wife shot him a sly wink.  
   
  The kids went to play around after eating, and Paulina took her time to teach Sage how to fish. After spending few hours at the island, they left. 

  Paulina instantly made up her mind about Sage while she was there. He is a cool guy, and she is always confused about her feelings because it seems like she has mutual feelings for him and Micheal, but she made up her mind to choose Micheal. He has it all, families, real friends, and true identity. Like an outcast, Micheal is emotionally alone, and she’s similarly adrift. 
**
**
  IN AN UNKNOWN PLACE^^^
^
  Mr. Ethan blew out a heavy smoke when the door slugs open and Don walked in. 

    “Do you’ve news or you are just wandering?”

   “Sir, I don’t know if this will worth your time, but I found out something,”

  “Don’t leave me curious,”

  “The man I mentioned the last time, his name is Jerry,”

   “Is that the news?”

   “He met with Mr. Packard’s daughter, and they had a little argument. I think they dated in the past, and he might likely have a thing on her that pose a threat to her election,”

   “How valid is your claim?”

   “I approached him. I tried to get him to our side as you requested, but he attacked me with a confusing question. He asked if I belonged to the opponent party and wanted to jeopardize her election,”

    “And what was your response?”

    “I was lost at first, but I told him we are on same side,” 

    “And?”

   “He simply chuckled, and left,”

    “It’s settled! We now have something against the Packard, but that’s not enough to send them to jail “

    “What’s that, boss?”

   “Can’t you put yours words together? If there was a relationship between that man and that Packard’s daughter in the past before the Packard’s sent him to jail, and whatever he had on her could jeopardize her election. What do you think that could be?”

   “Her daughter…”

   “We’re close! But revealing that won’t send them to jail, so we need another way,” he muttered. 

    “Sir, but we could still shake up the election even if they would cover it somehow, at least they would also be shaken,”

    “Nah… I should hit it as hard as he did to me. Taking him off guard,” Mr. Ethan muttered ticking his fingers. 
**
**
  Paulina hanged out with Sophia the following day, and they talked about her day with the Greenwood’s before they called it a day later in the evening and she dropped by at the noddles restaurant hoping they would be back in business soon, but they weren’t. 

  She boarded a taxi bavk, and she saw a familiar person engaged in a fight on her way. She asked the taxi to reverse, and she ran down when she confirmed that the person was likely Steven. 

   “Steven!” She shouted, and they all stopped to look at her. 

  Another person aimed at him to hit him unaware, but she was quick to hit the person with a stick she was nearby, and another person hit her to the ground   

   “Lina!” He shouted, as she groaned in pain. 

  He gathered his strength and knocked them off before going to her. 

   “Come on, let’s leave this place,” he said, helping her up. 

   “Who are they?” She asked. 

   “More are nearby,” he muttered, and she stood up in fear. 

  They ran off holding hands until they were far from the scene, trying to find a taxi in the dark. 

    “Why did you get involved? Didn’t I made myself clear the last time?” He yelled. 

    “Steven… you don’t look good,”

    “What do you care? I asked you to avoid me,”

    “Why? I thought we are friends,”

    “But I cut that ties,”

     “A girl? Did you get a new girlfriend? This is the first time you are telling me about a girl, I’m so excited,” she said beaming widely, and he chuckled.

  After sending the resignation, he turned off his phone, and few minutes later, he heard a knock on the side. He ignored the knocking, and he heard Mr. Jones voice from the other end of the door, and he let himself in.

    “Do you think that’s all it takes to punish a son that disobeys his father?”

     “I’m willing to do anything, I have nothing to lose,” 

     “Then return all the cards with you,” he said, and Micheal laughed. 

     “That’s a special case, dad. I already talked to my lawyer, not the company lawyer, but a more vigorous lawyer. She is an expert in human rights field, we are going to calculate all my earnings since I’ve been working for you, I’ve been working for eight years, and you will also discuss how do we go about my regular needs. As far as the law is concerned, I’m still your son, and I’m just a minor, a teenager,”

    “Seriously?” Mr. Russ scoffed. “Do you think you can beat me by the law and have your way with everything?”

    “I have to survive, dad, I have to survive somehow. I can’t hit rock bottom after working my b¥tt off for almost a decade,” he said, and Mr. Russ scoffed. 

     “You’re right, Mike. You worked so hard for almost a decade, and I’m so proud of that. So, why? Why throw it all away now?” He shouted. 

   “Because everything is not going my way, I sing want to live that way anymore,” he muttered. 

    “Mike, have you forgotten our agreement? You said you wanted to beat your mother, have you forgotten that? How can you achieve that if you Becky this way?” He asked, and Micheal looked at him, remembering thier agreement.

   “Is this about mum election? I don’t care about that!”

    “Shut up!” He snapped, and she flinched. 

    “Grandpa…”

   “Listen to me, Ava. Don’t do anything, you’re in no position to make any mistakes, don’t do anything, Ava. Else, it would all crumble on you,” he said, confusing her. 

    “What are you talking about, grandpa?”

    “Sir, we have a problem,” one of Mr. Packard’s men said running in. 
    
     “What is it?”

    “This was recently published few minutes ago, it has reached the opposing party, and they are making good use of it,” he said, and Mr. Packard collected his phone — an article about the car accident that Leo and Ms. Grace where involved in years ago. 

    “It won’t be easy to bring this down because of our opponents,” he muttered, and his phone rang — a call from Grace. 

    📲*Father, what do I do?* she cried from the other end of the call. 
     📞*Get it together! Prepare for a press conference, I’ll join you,” with that he dropped this call. 

   “Addressing this now will makes it useless,” he muttered, with clenched fist. 

    “Grandpa, what’s going to happen now?” 

    “You see why we mustn’t act rashly. Leave it all to me, Ava, grandpa will handle it one after the other before the worst happens,”

    “The worst?” She muttered, and he patted her back. 

   “I’ve a press conference to prepare for,” with that, he left. 
.
TO BE CONTINUED…

 IN LOUIS’S HOUSE^^^
^
  Ever since Charlotte recovered her memory from that accident, she has been thinking of what she can do, and wondering who could set up that. She refuse to believe that Grace could pull that up. Grace could disgust her, but she doubt if she could actually waht her d£ad, and she even took Paulina with her, which shouldn’t have happened if she wanted her gone. She couldn’t share her thoughts with Louis, because he has been busier with work these days, he seems to have a lot of patients, and he is always rushing to the hospital upon emergency calls so she didn’t want to bother him. 

 After giving it all a thought, she made up her to mind to go her father. If he was going to bring the Packard’s down without playing dirty, then she should go to him and ensure he isn’t playing dirty. She also realized that being with him will also make it easier to set things right, so she can be with her daughter again. 

 She packed few things in a bag, and waited for Louis who arrived home after midnight. 

   “Louis, welcome,” she said, and collected his bag. 

   “Why are you still up? I would be disappointed if you were waiting for you,” he muttered, and she pouted her lips cutely. 

   “What do I do? I guess I already disappointed you,” she whined, and he chuckled. 

   “If you act this way, I can’t even scold you,” he stated, and she muttered. “It’s past midnight, you should have gone to bed,” 

    “You came back this late,”

    “Yeah… I know,”

    “You’ve been returning late these days. Do you have too many patients? Or is your patient’s case that serious?”

    “I only attend to VIP patients, and my recent patient is a handful. He is a congressman. That aside, he doesn’t allow other doctors attend to him, and the director is willing to do everything to please him. He got his surgery last week, and he is better now. Hopefully, he would be discharged soon,”

    “You must’ve been really exhausted,”

    “A cup of coffee,” he muttered, and sat freely on the couch. 

    “I’ll be right back,” she said, and hurried to the kitchen. 

  She returned with the coffee, and met him already asleep on the couch. 

    “He must have been really tired,” she muttered, and wipe off sweats from his face. 

   She put the coffee away and went to get blanket to cover him up. 

    “I shouldn’t be bothering you with my problems, your job is hard enough. I’m sorry, Louis,” she muttered, and kissed his forehead. 

    “Madam, you are still up,” she heard Ms. Bella said. “Is professor back?” 

    “Shhh… he is asleep,”

    “Oh, he must’ve be really tired. I haven’t seen him this tired since he moved back here. Traveling tho and fro everyday must have been hard for him, and now his job is taking over. Saving people’s life must be a tough job,” with that she left. 

  Charlotte stared blankly at her retreating back, and looked at Louis before going to her room. 

    “Helping me must have really stressed him out. More reasons why I should leave,” she thought, and laid flat on her bed.

 IN AN UNKNOWN PLACE, SOMEWHERE AT A HOSPITAL ^^^
^
   Don opened his eyes when he felt Leo’s hand moved, and Leo sat up immediately. 

    “You, who sent you?” Leo muttered, grabbing Don’s collar. 

    “Even if I wanna harm you, you are too weak to fight back now, so save your strength,” Don remarked, and jabbed his hand off. 

     “You aren’t one of them. You are the one who saved me, who are you? Who sent you? No, I think I’ve seen you before. You are the man from that day. Are you from Grace’s opponent?”

   “You’ve a good memory. At least you know I rescued you,”

   “You must’ve been strong for the both of us to them over those men alone. You didn’t even get a scratch “

    “Nah… I nursed it. I can’t risk having more than that,”

    “Thank you for saving me, but you won’t get any hung from me,”

     “That’s not the street rule, man. I saved you, you owe me one,”

    “I don’t hwge anything on her. I’m useless to you,”

    “Wow. So Mr. Packard sent those end to escort a useless man to afterlife, that’s a waste of resources if you would ask me,” 

    “You… you aren’t from the opponent party, who are you?”

   “Me, I’m someone who can only live if you are alive. Simply saying we both owe each other our lives. That’s who I am,”

    “What nonsense…” Leo grumbled. 

  Don phone rang later, and he picked it up immediately. 

    📞*ok,” he muttered, and dropped the call. 

   “We have to leave this place,” he said, and Leo looked at him. 

    “What?” 

    “Mr. Packard is on a search for you, I can’t explain everything now… but if you care about your life we have to leave now,”

     “And why should I listen to you?”

     “Because I was the one who saved you. We both have to live, b@stard, so listen to me!” Don shouted, pulling him by the collar. 

   They left the ward and told the nurse that they want to take a walk, and flee from there. 

     “I have a destination,” Leo muttered. 

     “You’re returning to shark lord?”

      “Did you run a check on me?”

      “That’s a no go area. Mr. Packard sent one of his men to shark lord residence, they are also on a search for you,”

     “Thats not possible,”

    “Why do you think so? Because he helped you out of jail, you think he wouldn’t dumb you to curry favor from a powerful politician?” Don blurted, and Leo grabbed his clothes. 

     “Let it! Else… we might both dle. I need to focus  to drive,”

    
“Why should I believe you?”

  CHAIRMAN RUSS’S MANSION^^^
^
  Grace was preparing to leave the house when she heard the door clicked open, and Paul walked in. 
    
    “Paul, you slept out last night,”

    “Yes. I have business in New York, so I slept over. What’s that article?”

    “What article ? Hundred of articles are released everyday concerning my election. Even though I know I’m going to win, all those articles made it seem certain,”

    “Don’t act dumb! That article about you being involved in an accident with a strange man, and sending him to prison without trial,” 

   “I already addressed that issue with dad, didn’t you watch the press conference? Oh… you must’ve been busy and missed it. The police will investigate whoever released the false information, so don’t worry about a thing. Its obviously from my opponent,”

   “Grace, if that’s just an excuse you made to the public, you better be honest with me now. Did you really got involved in that accident or not?” 

   “Paul… can’t you at least believe me?” 

   “I can’t, and you are aware of that,”

   “Paul, for crying out loud? We were friends for over two decades before we get married, can’t you at least believe me for the sake of our friendship?” 

   “You’ve changed, Grace. You changed a long time ago. And even now, you are still lying to me,”

   “If yiu don’t believe me, then go find prove that I lied. I had enough explaining!”

   “You’re right, I should always find proof before confronting you because I’ll never hear the truth from you. But, if you still considered the friendship we once shared, answer me honestly. Do you know that guy? The one in the article,” 

   “NO! And that’s all you will hear from me,” with that, she intend to leave. 

   “Then explain this,”  he said, showing her his tab—in it, there was a picture of she and Leo together. 

  She gasped, and her hands started trembling in fear. 

   “How… hooowwe….” She shuddered, short of words. 

   “If you really didn’t know him as you claimed, if that article was false, then why did you meet him?”

    “Where did you get this?”

    “Is that the answer?”

    “Answer me!” She shouted grabbing his shoulder. “You’ve to tell me how you got this. Did you have someone tail me? Please answer me,”

    “Why would I have anyone tail you?” He snapped, freeing himself from her. 

    “Then how… who sent this to you? Are they trying to blackmail you?”

    “I got this pictures this morning from an anonymous person, with a message that I would be shocked if I find out what you are hiding from me. The sender seems to know a lot about some  dirty things you might jags been involved in. He said he is going to review them gradually, and I should be ready,”

    “No, no. It’s Leo. No, no, dad has to hear this,” she grumbled, and her hands trembling as she was trying to dial a number. 

  Paul exhaled heavily, and collected the phone from her. 

   “Give it back, I have to call dad,” she said in fear. 

   “What are you so scared of to be this way? Who is this Leo guy and what does he have on you? Is it about the election? Goodness, Grace, are you going to be like this because of the election?”

   “It’s not about the election!” She yelled, startling him. 

   “Then what? What’s making you this scared?” 

    “You don’t know anything, Paul, and you don’t need to know,” with that she collected her phone from him and left.

SOMEWHERE AROUND SHARK LORD RESIDENCE ^^^
^
 Don has been in his car since morning after dropping Leo off. He couldn’t leave the residence from fear of what could happen to Leo, neither does he have the courage of going in. Shark lord is a fearsome mafia boss,  and he doesn’t dare to wander around his vicinity. 

  He stayed out in his car hoping that Leo would run out for him to save him if anything goes wrong. He can’t afford to miss Leo, because Mr. Ethan might really take his live if he does. 

  He waited in the car till it was late at night, when he saw someone fly out from the fence, he focused well, and confirmed its Leo, and he was being chased by men.

    “I told you didn’t I? You will have to live for me to live,” 
    
     “You were right, I think he boss plotted against me,”

   “That’s also what you told that Packard years ago before you were sent to prison, and you didn’t even tried to defend yourself,”

    “Goodness. You are one hard one, but I love the energy 

    “I’m someone who hates the Packard’s, and I want to bring them down. Every single one of them! So first, let’s stir it up by proving the little one true blood,”

    “No, don’t. Not that!” Leo blurted

    “What do you mean?”

     “I’m going to help you get the Packard’s. I have some things on them, and I’ll help you gather more. But not that, not that,”

     “But that’s the sharpest tool to bring them down now, with that the election will be automatically decided, and he will be busy trying to save the election, while I strike him from behind,”

He recently helped a criminal escape prison to help him get rid of someone. Stuffs like that, he does that a lot. I can help you get them all, but that not that,”

   “Good! Revealing that won’t send that b@stard to prison anyways, so let’s go for what you have. If he goes to prison, then his daughter election is automatically down. It’s still a win-win. But, we’ve to make an agreement,” 

    “What’s it’s, sir?”

    “If you fail to get the Packard’s for me before the election is over, I’ll reveal their dirty secret, and you will have to cooperate and work my way “

     “Yes, boss,” he assured, and Mr. Ethan chuckled, before patting his shoulder. He looked at Don who bowed slightly, and nodded. 

    “Don’t worry Charlotte, very soon, just very soon, I’ll make them feel the same pain, in threefold!” He thought, smiling brightly. 
*
  Leo excused himself to use the restroom, and Don looked at Mr. Ethan. 

   “Split it,” Mr. Ethan blurted. 

   “Boss, do you trust him?”

    “I don’t even trust me,” he muttered, and Don bowed his head. “I stopped trusting my subordinates, I guess this is what they call beaten one,”

    “Boss, so are you really going to do it his way?” 

    “If don’t have to leave everything to hold. We can also come up with our own way,”

    “What’s your plan, boss?”

    “I already sent Kai to the Packard’s,”

    “What? Sir, but Kai is incompetent,”

    “That’s an overestimate,”

     “Why did you sent him there? If you need an undercover, you should have sent someone more competent,”

     “That’s why I sent him there. He is incompetent, but u choose him because he will do anything to prove himself, and if he fails, he will dle by the Packard’s hands,”

*
  Leo returned later, and she begin to carry out thier plans. 

    “The chief prosecutor always helped him cover up all his crimes, he has been dirty since he was a politician,” Leo said. 

   “Obviously,” Mr. Ethan remarked. 

  “But the chief Prosecutor retried last year, and there is another capable prosecutor has been on the Packard’s,”

    “Who?”

   “Prosecutor Dennis,”

    “How do you know about this?”

    “He is my uncle,” he said, and they all looked at him. “No, no, no. It’s not the way you imagined it. 

    “Your uncle?” 

    “Yes, he has been curious about the Packard’s since he sent me to prison years ago. So he transferred to the center district two years ago,” 

    “Wow!”

   “It’s not what you think, he is here to get revenge or anything. My uncle actually dislikes me, and he didn’t even try to help me out of jail,”

    “So why is he on the Packard’s?”

   “He is also a corrupt prosecutor. He wants to have the Packard so he can curry favor from them,” 

   “Wow. This is going to be more difficult than I think,” 

   “There should be a way to get our hands on him,” Don said. 

    “What’s his weakness? Any family member?” Mr. Ethan asked. 

   “He divorced few years ago, and his wife went abroad with their children. She already remarried, but my uncle is the one funding for the kids,”

    “So he has no relative here expect you?”

    “None, expect my mum and I,” he muttered. 

    “Then it’s settle! A man with no weakness, now reputation will be his only concern. Let’s pay him a visit,”

    “What?” Don and Leo chorused. 

    “Now?” Don asked with widened eyes. 

   “Yes, let’s go,” Mr. Ethan muttered, and took his jacket exiting the room.

  HOLLAD HIGH^^^

  Paulina exhaled heavily at the sight of the students gathered talking about the timetable before she find her way to class. 

  She looked at the MASS seats and sent Micheal a message. She didn’t see either of them at school yesterday, she even went to their suite but it was empty. Their misunderstanding collide with Micheal’s unfinished announcement already started a rumor that the friends are splitting up—Steven is gone, and now the others are not attending school again. She tried to talk to Micheal on the phone, and he assured that he would return to school beside the mid-semester exam, that he needed time to reset. 

    “Why are they all being hard on theirselves?” She thought, and exhaled heavily. 
**
  Austin went to the poolside to revise what he studied with Rae the other day, she had threatened to ask him questions, and stop studying with him if he fails. Studying with her so kinda hard, he wish he never started it, but he refused to stop like a dumb loser. 

    “I see you are here already,” she said, and he chuckled. 

   “You told me you were going to come during recess, you are quite early,”

    “Let’s say I decide to start recess now. Music glass is really not my cup of tea,”

   “Never! I hate libraries. It gives me creeps,”

   “But I think all smart people love the library?”

  “Go say that to Stan. Have you ever seen him study before?” She blurted, and he shook his head. 

   “Stan is a genius,”

   “I believe everyone follows what work for them. I agreed to study with you despite not being comfortable with group study, and you are still making choices,”

    “I have a goal you know. If I end up losing after all these, I might really loose my mind,”

   “Then think of other options, the library is not just it,”

   “Then shouod we do offsite?”

    “Where?”

    “I just hates the library,”

    “Let’s just revise first,” she muttered, and he nodded.

MR. ETHAN’S WAREHOUSE ^^^
^
  Mr. Ethan drove into the warehouse when he received news that Charlotte was there, he only detailed men to guard her, he never expected them to bring  her to the warehouse. 

  He entered the warehouse, and saw hwr chattering with the men there. She has always been generous to his workers even when she was younger, she considered them all her uncles and never regard them as an underlining.  At times, he wondered how he managed to raise her that way. 

   “Miss Charlotte,” Don called surprised to see her, and that drew her attention to them.

   “No, you weren’t. Dad... dad told me. No, I mean… but why are you here with dad?” She said, and Don’s jaw dropped as he realized what she was getting at.

  They both went in to talk properly, and he gave her a brief on his achievements so far.

   HOLLAND HIGH^^^

   “It’s fine, Sage. Let’s take a break for a while, I won’t nag you and complain about our friendship anymore, but I’ll always be here when you need a friend,”

   “Thank you, Sophie. I’ll make sure to arrange those blind dates for you once the exams are over,”

   “Why are you thinking about that now?” 

    “What? Isn’t that what you always wanted?”

   “Yeah… that’s what I always wanted, but how could you think about that now that you got rejected? The pressure of the upcoming exams is hard enough, and Lina had to add hers to it. That girl, I really wish I could drag her ears!” She shouted, and he chuckled. 

    “You are more offended than I’m,”

   “What? Do you think I’m offended this way? Do you really want to see me offended?” She yelled, and he shook his head unable to control his laughters. 

   “No, please don’t. I don’t want to see you angry,” he said, still laughing. 

   “Look at you laughing. I’m glad that I could cheer you up, cheer up Sage, this is an inevitable phase in a teenager’s life, so it’s normal. It doesn’t mean that you aren’t good enough, it’s just normal,” she said, and he smiled. 

   “Why did you always sound like my mum,”

   “Gosh, I don’t know if I should be angry or honored to hear that,”

    “You should be honored, because to me, my mum is the wisest woman,”

    “I know. She is a judge, so she should,” 

   “Thank you, Sophie “ he muttered, and embraced her. 
**
**
   School dismissed later, and Paulina was walking through the hallway when she saw Sophie. 

 She wanted to call out to her, when Sage joined, they both hold hands leaving together.

   “What? Did they just left me out?” She thought sadly. 

  She instantly realized that it was best for her not to be there after rejecting Sage that way.

    “I think it’s best this way. He needs Sophie, and I… I would figure out myself,” she muttered. 

  She continued on her way and saw Austin heading to the parking lot. 

   “Hey, Austin,” she called out, and he turned to her with a glare. “Wrong timing,” she thought. 
*
 They entered his car, and the ride to the bus station was in silence until she broke the silence. 
    
    “I didn’t know you were at school today. Neither of you were in class, and the suite was also empty,”

   “What do you take me for? I’m not a good friend who could over look things like that. Mike and Stan kept a secret from me, and you… who are you? Why would he share something he hid from me with you? You were simply friends with us because of his urge to get back at Ava, but what? He even shared it with you and left me out. Well… it all makes sense, you are slightly alike. You are both illegitimate, with different backgrounds,”

   “Wow. Now I understand why Mike kept it all from you, you are very unreasonable. Stip the car, I’ll find my way from here,” 

    “We aren’t at the bus station yet,”

   “Do I look blind to you? I might be an illegitimate, but that doesn’t mean I have no pride. Stop the car!” She blurted, and he stopped it immediately. “While you are angry at Mike and Stan, you should also  take your time to think if you appear like a friend that he could freely share his shortcomings with,” with that she alighted from his car. 

  Austin hit the steering in anger. He couldn’t believe he said all those. 

    “Goodness man, get it all together!” He screamed , before he drove off. 
*
  Paulina board a taxi to the bus station, and find herself alighting at the station closet to the noodles shop. She stopped a taxi hoping they are back in business or Steven was back working there, although she knows it’s impossible. She think about him at times, and the thought of his being a murder baffles her a lot. He claimed  to be on the run, and if those guys were really murders, the thoughts of what they might have done to him was unthinkable.

  She got to the noddles shop, thankfully they are back on business but they are closed for a short break. 

   “Oh, miss, I’m glad you come again,” the owner welcomed her, and she smiled before in. 

   “I’m glad to see that you are back in business,” 

   “We reopened three days ago, and to our surprise, our customers seem to be waiting. We have too much orders that I had to put that sign outside to attend to the orders first before attending to more customers,”

    “Why don’t you get a help?” 

    “Yeah,  I should. But getting a part time worker is not really easy these days, and I am still wondering if Steven could return,” 

   “He never returned?”

   “He didn’t. He didn’t after he left, I wonder if he is doing well. Since you attend same school, and you are friends, you should be able to answer that more,”

    “He didn’t return to school either. It’s like he disappeared from the face of the earth,”

     “Do you think he travelled abroad?”

     “I would be glad if that was the case,” she muttered. 

    “Wait for a while, I’ll get your orders ready,”

    “But you said you have a lot of orders,”

    “Come on, you are like a family to me now. You weren’t just a regular here, but you also assist Steven with the deliveries whenever you stop by. So I can’t keep you waiting,”

    “More reasons why you should. If you have orders, you should attend to them, and if you need a hand, then I should assist you,” she said, and his eyes widened. 

    “No, you don’t have to,”

    “I won’t persuade you,”

   “Then allow me be trouble you, just this once,” he said, and she laughed. 
*
  She assisted him at the restaurant, while he went to send out orders before she placed her own order when everything dled down. 

    “Since you refused to get paid for today, then allow I’ll pack some noodles for you,”

   “No, sir. You really don’t have to. You have done more than enough by giving me this for free. I’m full, and won’t be able to eat those,”

    “You can share them with your family at home,”

    “Family?” 

   “I’ll be right back,” with that he left before she could protest. 

    She begun to pick on her food wondering if she had a family, she once did, and that was her mother. Her father might be paying title attention to her now but he was always too busy with work. She was engrossed in her thoughts, and missing her mother when she saw someone peeped into the restaurant, but the moment she caught the sight, the person ran off. 
   
   “What? Who could that be?” She thought and went out. 

 She searched around for the person, and saw the familiar back climbing on a motorcycle. 

   “Steven?” she called and he turned to look at her before driving off. “Wait! Steven!” She called out, but he retreat. 

  She stopped a taxi passing by to go after him, and they drove for minutes before he stopped at a big shopping mall. 

   “Wow, he must be fine seeing that he is here to shop,” she thought. 

   “This must be the infamous big shopping mall in this area,” the taxi driver said, getting her attention. 

   “Sorry, sir, but what made it infamous?”
    
    “People always tried to commit suicide here. It’s a really tall building…-“

    “Suicide? Sir, I’ll pay with this,” she said, and paid with her card before hurrying in. 

   “Come on, Lina, he has no reason to commit suicide. I mean… why would he?”
   “But he is on the run, he is being chased by murders,”
   “No, he said he is a murder,” 

  Different thoughts ran through her mind as she entered the elevator, heading to the rooftop and upon arriving there, her heart slipped a beat to see him standing by the tip of the rooftop. 

   “STEVEN… NOOOO…..” she screamed, and he turned to look at her. 

  She doesn’t care if she was exaggerating like the first day she saw him at the cafeteria rooftop, but she really think she need to stop him either he was just enjoying the view or attempting suicide. 

    “Don’t interfere this time Lina, stay away!” He shouted, and her eyes widened.  

   “This time? If you said this time, does that mean you really attempted suicide that day at school?” She shuddered. 

    “I told you to stop interfering, Lina, why won’t you listen? Because of you, all of this is happening because of you, it’s all your fault!” 

   “How is it my fault? Is it my fault that we are friends? What are you talking about? Why are you on the run and why is it my fault?” 

    “You shouldn’t have interfered back then, you shouldn’t have stopped me. Then all of these would have ended a long time ago. It’s all your fault, Lina, I’m suffering this way because of your nosiness, and all of that had to happen to Mr. Joe because of me,” he said, and she wondered why he mentioned the noddles restaurant owner. 

    “Mr. Joe? What happened to him because of you?”

    “Just let me be, Lina, pretend like this day never happened,”

    “Wait. If you are going to end it anyways, why don’t you just share it. That thing that hurt you so much, what kept bothering you, and all,”

    “You’re right, I should share it since I’ll end it all,” he muttered. 

   “I’m all ears, Steven,” 

  She took her phone and sent a message to Michael, but he didn’t respond. She thought of who else she could send a message to, and went to their after school club group and sent the message there hoping they show up and stop him. 

   “Steven, I’m listening,” she shuddered, with hopes that she could buy some time with that till someone show up. 

   “You must be curious about those guys and why I’m on the run, as I said, they are murderers, and their boss is my father, he murdered my mother,” he muttered in tears, and her eyes widened. 

    “What?” She muttered. 

   “And it was all my fault, she dled because of me, it was all my fault, I kllled her…” he cried.

  FLASHBACK***
*
  Five-year-old Steven was in tears as he watched his mother packed, after packing, she went to embrace him. His father wasn’t home, so she drugged the few guards around to be able to leave. 

   “Steven, I wish I could take you with me but your father will come after us if I do. I promise to come back for you, I’m going to file for a divorce, and ensure I take you with me,”

  “Mummy…. Don’t go…” he cried, grabbing her dress. 

  “Steven, just hold on to the pendant, it’s a heirloom pendant and it’s very important to me so I have to come back for it. Hold on to it, son, and keep it safe for me,” she said, and kept the pendant in his pocket. 

   “Mummy… don’t go…” he cried, and she freed his little hands from her before leaving. 

  He ran after her but she shut the door behind him, and he broke down in tears. He sat on floor in tears and heard the doorbell opened back and his mother came in.  

   “How could I leave without you,” she cried, and he embraced her. 

   “Mummy….” 

   “Let’s go, baby. I’ll ensure to protect you from him, I’ll ensure you don’t have to live like him. Let’s go, before he returns,” she said, and carried him.

 Michael was busy in his study when he saw Paulina’s message but he ignored it. He had some work to finish before the next day, and talking to her would distract him when he already knows what she wanted to discuss—blabbering on his return to school. Seeing her send a message in their after school club group chat made him wonder what she sent there, and his eyes widened upon seeing the contempt. He put away what he was doing, and hurried out, heading to the location. 
**
  Austin was having dinner with his parents, and he didn’t stop glancing at the wall clock waiting for ten minutes so he could excuse himself. He couldn’t even press his phone in the dinning hall with them, neither can he digest his food properly. 

    “Your couch contacted me regarding your training. What have you been telling him? Didn’t you tell him you might quit?”

   “I’m not gonna quit, dad!”

    “Do you have confidence?” His mother teased. 

  He exhaled heavily, and peeped at his phone when he saw a message from the after school club. Already curious about what it was, he took his phone. 

    “Put that away, young man,” his father said but he ignored them to read the contempt. 

   “What?” He shouted, and stood up. 

   “Austin, is there a problem?” His mother inquired. 

    “There shouldn’t be,” he muttered, and ran off to his room to get him keys, before heading out to the location. 
**
  Stanley put his phone away as he was enjoying his video game, he ignored all his messages until he aced the game and took his phone. 

  He saw several messages, but the one from the after school club caught his attention. He wondered what they were discussing there when their friendship group chat has been quiet. 

 He checked the message, and his eyes widened after reading the contempt. 

  He took the keys to his motorcycle ready to leave, but before he did, he searched for the emergency contact of that shopping mall, ti inform them about the intending suicide, and also requested for crisis response foam, incase things goes wrong. 
**
**
 THE SHOPPING MALL^^^
^
  Micheal arrived first, and headed to the elevator, when Austin arrived, the elevator was busy so he took the stairs, Stanley arrived later and ensured the foam was properly secured for however he landed before intending to go to him, but to his surprise, he landed on the foam. 
**
  Micheal ensured that the news will dle down there, and compensate the few people that make videos to delete it before they left the shopping mall in his car. 

  The ride was in silence, and he called his secretary to reserve the closest restaurant for them. After driving for few minutes, they arrived at an empty restaurant. 

    “I was really holding it all in, until now. What’s that for, Steven? Do you think you are in some dramas? Committing suicide at this age and time, are you even thinking?” Austin snapped. 

   “Don’t be too harsh, Austin, you don’t even know what he is going through,” Paulina defended. 

   “F¥ck whatever he is going through. Is he the only one going through a tough time. I’m struggling to improve my grades, doing what I never could have imagined. Mike also has his own hurdles, and what? Suicide? How can anyone think of ending their lives. You don’t even think of others. Your parents, and we, your friends, how can you be so selfish?” Austin yelled. 

   “That’s enough, man. At least let hear him out first,” Stanley said, and Austin groaned angrily before taking his seat. 

   “Steve, you better have a good explanation for this,” Micheal said, patting Steven’s shoulder. 

   Steven bowed his head in tears, he can’t even think of the best way to start. 

   “You guys are making this hard on him. Yes, everyone has their own  situation, but because you could endure yours doesn’t mean everyone are strong, people aren’t the same, everyone is going through difficult things. Don’t be too harsh on him!” Paulina shouted 

    “Steven, does this have to do with why you have been unreachable?” Stanley asked, and Steven nodded. 

   “Lina, since he already told you everything, then tell us,” Austin said. 

   “Why would I? If you are really his friend, you would make him share his problems with you, because that’s part of what makes  us friends,” Paulina said,  and they all exhaled heavily. 

    “Are you having some situations at home? Did your family went bankrupt?” Micheal asked, and Steven shook his head.  

    “Goodness. Do we have to puzzle our way to get the answers?” Austin lashed. “I got better things to do, man,” he added. 

   “Steven, we are the one who asked you not to talk, and I really regret that now. I never knew you were that obedient and would stay quiet until now. Talk to us, man, tell us what the problem is and let’s work it out together. Do you know how devastating it would’ve been for us to hear the news of your suicide? It would make us feel like we wronged you in some ways, and we will blame ourselves for it. We are friends, but you were so distant,” Micheal said. 

   “I’m sorry for making you worried,” Steven muttered. 

   “If you are sorry, then split it! What’s bothering you?” Austin nagged. 

     “Austin, that’s enough!” Stanley snapped. “Steven, if you are gonna be like this and not talk, we can’t force you, we don’t know anything about you and that’s our fault, but since it’s became this way, I’m gonna report this to the police, so your parents can come fetch you,” he said, and they all looked at him. 

   “No, don’t!” Steven, and Paulina shouted. 

   “What’s really going on, Lina?” Michael asked. 

    “You can’t report to the police, they are gonna find him if you do,” Paulina remarked. 

   “Did he ran away from home?” Austin inquired. 

    “Are you on the run?” Stanley asked. 

   “You all will be in danger if you get involved with me,” Steven muttered. 

    “What’s going on, man? I’m getting goosebumps,” Austin whined. 

    “My father is on the look out for me, and he can get rid of any obstacles as long as he finds me,” he muttered. 

     “I don’t follow. Is your father some assassin, or a mafia?” Stanley asked. 

    “What nonsense are you saying, Stan?” Austin blurted. 

    “He is a mafia boss,” Steven muttered, and their eyes widened. 

   “What?!” The boy chorused. 

    “Why are you on the run? He is your father, either is a mafia or whatever, he can’t harm you. Wait, what am I even saying? Why are you trying to commit suicide? Come on, man, don’t keep us in the dark!” Austin nagged. 

     “Relax, I’m gonna tell you everything, as long as you don’t involve the police,” he muttered, and they exhaled heavily. 

    They all listened attentively as he narrate his story to them, leaving them stunned. 

     “What? You are going through all these and you kept it on in without saying anything? How could you? How could you manage to endure that alone? How could you be that strong?” Austin yelled, hitting him. 

    “That’s enough!” Stanley shouted, and dragged Steven by his collar. “This… this must’ve been really hard on you,” he muttered, and embraced him. 

   Micheal ran his fingers through his hair, and shook his head before heading out. Paulina looked at him, wondering why he was leaving but going after him. 

    “Mike, where are you going?” She asked when they got outside. 

    “It’s all my fault, Lina, Steve is that way because of me, he almost committed suicide because of me,” he rants, and she looked at him confused. 

    “What do you mean it’s your fault?”

    “If I haven’t forced to be our friend… no, if I haven’t ask him to keep quiet, he would have shared everything with us, we would have find a solution together. But no, I kept him by my side as a stand in to complete the MASS, oblivious to all his suffering, to the extent that he almost took his own life,” he said, and she embraced him. 

    “It’s not your fault, Mike. Yes, you made mistakes, and you should make up for it, that’s why it’s called mistake. Steve needs you all now than ever, he needs his friends to support him, not to blame yourselves,” she said, patting his back gently. 
*
  They returned back inside, and called the attention of the waiter to place their orders. 

    “First, I don’t know how best to help you, Steve, but I’m sure that we would all be able to work this out together,” Micheal stated. 

    “Man, you don’t have to keep your emotions to yourself anymore. Let’s be real friends this time. We have all known each other for years, we have been friends with you since we are in middle school even though you never considered us as friends. And mind you, you aren’t the only one with hurdles, so never outcast yourself. It might seems like I have a perfect family, but I suffered discrimination my whole life from my smart parents, and sister. At times I wish I could run away, I felt indifferent in that family, I felt like nothing whenever I am with them. I even ran away from home once when I was younger, and Mike…” he said, and they all looked at Michael. 

   “I’m an illegitimate,” Micheal muttered, and they all gasped. 

   “You…” Steven shuddered. 

    “You must’ve thought I was perfect, but I’m an illegitimate. If I get bursted, I’ll face a lot of criticism, and I can never think of becoming the chairman of Russ group. That’s how messed up I am,” he said, and Steven mouth was hanged opened agape. 

    “Mike…” Paulina muttered, and held his hand. 

   “We all have a situation, Steve, but at least we could say our genius is excluded,” Micheal said, and Stanley chuckled. 

   “Yeah! What’s that look? I would have to do a lot of good deeds to have your life in my next life. Not only are you a genius, but you have loving parents as well!” Austin snapped. 

    “We could say so,” Stanley muttered. 

   “There you go,” Austin sneered, hitting him playfully. 

    “Guys, we have to think of a way to help Steven. I’m sure his father can’t harm us, I mean… even gangsters know better than getting involved with us. We aren’t some common noodle’s restaurant owner,” Stanley stated. 

   “Yeah… Stan is right, our genius Al ways have a solution,” Austin said, and they all chuckled. 

   “With that being said, just until we find a solution, you can stay with us to ensure your safety,” Micheal said, and they all nodded along. 

   “My house is wide open, bro, and my bed is big too,” Austin said, and they smiled. 

   “Thank you so much for this,” Steven muttered. 

    “Gosh, you have such nice voice, you shouod talk often,” Austin said, and they all laughed. 

     “Austin, I hope your parents would accommodate him till we find a solution,” Michael said. 

    “Come on, man, his parents only bite him. I sleep over at his okave often,” Stanley said. 
   
     “They like you because you are a genius. But don’t worry, my parents don’t bite others,” he said, and they nodded. 

     They all talked about random stuffs as they eat, and Austin point out a day that they got into a fight at a party. 

    “I was also frightened that Steve could bring down all of those guys on his own. Since that day, I reserved huge respect for him, and I was equally scared,” Austin said. 

    “Same here,” Michael added. 

   “What party was that?”Stanley asked. 

  “Julian birthday party in Europe,” Austin replied. 

    “Who is Julian?” Stanley asked, and the boys all looked at him in shock. 

    “Man, eat up. She is no one, she is just a little kid that I fancy so much,” Austin said, forcing a piece of meat in his mouth. 

    “If she was just a kid that you fancy, then why did we attend her party?” He asked in confusion. 

     “Is he really the genius we know?” Michael asked, and Austin shook his head. 

    “Julian must have left a deep scar in him. Their relationship didn’t even last a month,” Austin grumbled, and Micheal nodded in agreement. 

    “Is he normally like that?” Paulina asked, and Steven shook his head. 

    “From now on Steve, you aren’t allowed to give any head signals. Since you were so obedient all these years, you should be now as well and always talk. Goodness man, you have such nice voice,” Austin rants, and they all laughed.
**

  CHAIRMAN STANFORD’S OFFICE^^^
^
 Paul couldn’t stop thinking about the picture that was anonymously sent to him, even after he listened to Grace’s conference where she and her denied all allegations. 
    “If she met with him again after he was released from prison, it could only mean that he was blackmailing her without something, considering how scared she was,” he thought. 

  The thoughts of that made him ask his secretary to run a background check on him, and his secretary report back to him with everything he got on t  man.

   “Leo Thompson,” Paul muttered the man name, before looking at his secretary. “What relationship do you think he shared with Grace in the past?”

    “Sir, there is really nothing that could link him and madam Grace together. He lead a carefree life, that is awry to a world that fit madam Grace,”

   “During the period that he claimed to be at prison, what does he do during those time?”

   “He doesn’t have a permanent job, he also works as a chauffeur and an escort, madam Grace might have likely meet him from there,”

    “What does he do now?”

    “It’s not confirmed, but it seems like he works for a mafia boss, shark lord,”

    “Mafia? What business could Grace have such person,” 

   “Sir, and about Mr. Louis,”

   “Oh, yeah. What have he been up to?”

    “Sir, there is possibly that madam Charlotte who s alive,” 

    “What? Charlotte is alive?” He shouted. 

     “A strange woman moved him with him about a month ago, and we were able take a picture of her,” he said, and showed him the pictures. 

   “This is Charlotte, Charlotte is alive!” He said in shock, unable to believe his eyes. 

    “Where is she presently?” He asked. 

    “At Mr. Louis’s house,”

    “What? Charlotte and Louis are living together? What’s their relationship?”
    
    “That’s not confirmed yet. Sir, and we also believe that Mr. Louis was likely the one behind Mr. Flood’s release,”

     “Definitely! Since Charlotte is with him, he must have released her father… but why aren’t they coming to get Paulina?”

     “They are probably waiting for the right time,”

    “No! If Charlotte has been alive all these while, and made us believe that she was de@d, there must be a reason why Louis is quiet about it. I need to meet Charlotte,”

    “Sir, you are going to meet her?”

   “Let’s go,” 
.*
  The ride to Louis’s house was short, and he saw Charlotte in the compound from his car wetting flowers in company of a woman. 

 He asked his secretary to keep a watch on Louis after he discovered that Mr. Flood was out of jail. He couldn’t find any trace on Mr. Flood because the law firm was the one in charge of everything. He assumed that Louis could be the one to do it, and turned out he was right. 
.
    “Sir, aren’t you going in?” His secretary asked. 

   “I should, since we came all the way here,” h muttered. 

  He was about to alight from the car when a car drove in, and Charlotte smiled warmly as she went to welcome Louis. 

   “Are they a couple now?” He thought 
*
  He wanted to get down from the car when someone knocked at the window. He asked his secretary to whine down, and he looked at the guard knocking at the window. 

   “If I were you, I won’t think of it,” the guard said, and Paul became confused. 

   “Who are you?” He inquired. 

   “Jay, leave hun to me “ Louis said approaching them. 

    “Louis,” Paul muttered.

   MR. PACKARD’S MANSION^^^
^
  Mr. Packard received report that they lost Leo from his right hand man, and he chuckled. 

   “You lost a man, just a man! Even that mafia lost him too?!” 

   “Sir, he seems to be working with someone. We were able to get traces on him at a hospital, someone seems to be helping him, and shark lord’s men claimed that someone came to his rescue in a car, they were able to get the plate number, but turns out it’s a fake plate number,” 

   “Fake plate number? Who could be behind him?”

   “Shark lord’s men are everywhere searching for him,”

    “Goodness! Just a man?! Who could it be…”

    “Sir, we have another problem “

    “What’s it?”

    “You are being summoned by the prosecutor regarding a tax invasion case,”

    “What nonsense? Which prosecutor dares?!” He shouted. 

    “I!” They heard a said from behind, and they froze, and turned prosecutor. 

    “How dare you barge in here?” Mr. Packard’s right hand man shouted. 

    “Who are you?is your chief aware of this nonsense?”

    “I apologize for barging in, Mr. Packard, Im prosecutor Denis Godfrey, and I felt it would be honorable for me to escort you to the prosecutor’s office, rather than summoning you there like some criminals,” he said, winking at him. 

   “How dare you? You have no reason to barge in here, my lawyer will attend to this!” He lashed, and gunshot was heard, startling them all. 

    “Isn’t that gunshot, I should confirm that,” the prosecutor said, and Mr. Packard held him back. 

   “I’m a very busy man, so we should go. You, call my lawyer!” Mr. Packard said, leading the way out and the prosecutor chuckled before he followed.

 FLASHBACK to the day she went to the beach with Stanley***
