    “It’s left to you, it depends on how you play. Pay for the coffee, that’s enough to treat me for being kind to your daughter all these while… now it’s left to you if I would keep that up,” with that, she left. 

  Charlotte made a fist staring at her retreating back in fear. 

    “What do I do? I need to speck to dad,” she grumbled, and dialed her father’s number immediately. 
**
**
  HOLLAND HIGH^^^
^
  Paulina couldn’t concentrate in school, she kept thinking about her grandfather during classes until it was time for recess. 

  She packed her bags immediately, and hurried out of the class heading to the rooftop with no intention of returning to class since she couldn’t concentrate anyways. 

  She sat there staring blankly at the sky and started missing Sophia and Sage. She understood that Save was distant because of the rejection, but Sophia has also been awkwardly distant as well. At first she understood that she was with Sage to comfort him, but she also needs her right now. The thought of all that made her think she was selfish. 

    “Why are you here alone? I thought you ran off to be with your friends,” Micheal said, joining her. 

    “Mike…”

   “You must be missing them, I mean your friends,” he said, and she nodded. “What can I do to enlighten your mood?”

    “Thanks for the concern, Mike,”

    “I just realized that a lot of things have been happening lately, and we don’t even have time for a normal date,”

    “Date?”

    “Yeah, date. We are a couple, but it doesn’t even feel like we are dating at all,”

     “Oh… that?”

    “How about after school? We can go on a date after school. Tell me what you want to do, and I’ll make the reservation,” he said, and she smiled. 

     “Really? Are we going to do anything I love to do?”

    “I am a man of my words “ he said, and winked at her. 

    “You sure are,” she teased, and he chuckled. 
**
  When school dismissed, she gave him the list she drafted. 

    “What? All of these? You want us to do all these?”

    “You are a man of your words,” she said, and he scoffed. 

    “Yeah… I’m a man of my words,” he grumbled, and she laughed hitting him playfully. 
*
  Ava and her friends stood afar glaring at Paulina and Micheal till they were out of sight. 
    
    “Ava, are you going to watch them being all lovey-dovey even when you are engaged to him? If I were you, I would throw a tantrum!” Lia said. 

    “Lia is right, girl. The sight alone is so annoying to watch,” Alicia pouted. 

   “Let them enjoy the moment while it last,” Ava said before walking out, and the girls scoffed before running after her. 
**
 Paulina and Micheal got to the parking lot, and Paulina’s driver walked up to her. 

    “Good afternoon, ma’am,” he greeted, and Micheal looked at Paulina in shock. 

   Paulina looked away, and smiled sarcastically. “What are you doing here?” She asked. 

    “Madam, the chairman asked me to pick you up. It’s quite urgent, he said he has something to discuss with you,”

    “Dad said so?” She asked in excitement. 

    “Dad… Lina, what’s going on?”

   “I’m sorry, Mike, I really have to go. I’ll call you!” With that, she entered the car. 

  The driver bowed slightly to Mike, and entered the car. 

   “What? Did I just got ditched?” He muttered, looking at the piece of paper with him. “And what’s all that about? ‘Ma’am, dad,’ what’s that about?” He thought, staring at the car timi it was out of sight. “I really got ditched,” he grumbled, and throw his fist in the air. 
**
 The driver drove for a while before the driver stopped the car and got down. 

   “What happen?” She asked, but he ignored her. 

  She wanted to get down from the car ti check on him when another person entered the car and drove off. 

   “Hey! Stip the car! Who are you? Where are you taking me?” She shouted, hitting his back. 

  He looks scary on his face mask and sunglasses, scaring her even more 

   “You ask too many questions, and my ears are about to bleed!” He snapped, and spayed something at her. 

  She choked on the liquid he sprayed, and passed out in the process. 
.
 TO BE CONTINUED…

 HOLLAND HIGH^^^

 Michael sat in his suite staring at the list and his phone, while his friends argue over unnecessary things. 

   “I’m serious, man. I think she likes me,” Stanley said, and Austin scoffed. 

   “Don’t get ahead of yourself, man. Jessica of all girls here at school. Likes you? She declined your request to join the math club a thousand times, and you thinks she likes you?”Austin teased. 

    “I’m serious, man. If she doesn’t, then why would she say that?”

   “Simply because, because…”

    “Because of what?”

    “Because… there is no way she can like you. She must have a reason for leading you on. Probably, to add you to her lists of rejections!” Austin said. 

    “What? What did you just say?” Stanley shouted, and intend to hit him —he ran off, and they both started running around together. 

   Mifheal exhaled heavily still staring at his phone, and Steven was o preoccupied, playing video games. 

  Austin and Stanley stopped running later and dragged Steven to seat next to Micheal. 

   “Man, how come you didn’t say anything?” Austin said. 

    “About what?” Micheal asked. 

   “What? Aren’t you here the whole time? About Stan and Jessica!” Austin snapped. 

    “What happened to Jessica?” He asked, and Austin gasped, while Stanley chuckled. 

    “This man is gone…” Stanley teased. 

     “Hey! Don’t tell me you are this way because Lina ditched you?” Austin blurted. 

     “She said she was going to call, she promised to,” he muttered. 

    “Are you taking that seriously? Girls say that a lot when they want to ditch a guy,” Austin said, and Michael’s eyes widened. 

    “Really?” He gasped. 

    “Come on, man. I think you still need lectures about ladies from me. Not just you, but Stan as well. And you Steve, you should also find a girl. I’ll arrange one for you, don’t worry. How does Rena sounds?” Austin said, and they scoffed. 

    “Hey! Why Rena of all people?” Stanley snapped. 

   “Rena is just the start. He should date her for few weeks for some experience, and he will upgrade gradually. My main target for him is Jenny, but he needs a lot of experience to date her,”  Austin said, and Stanley exhaled heavily. 

   Micheal didn’t stop staring at the paper with him, and the guys shook thier head. 

    “What do we do with him, man? He is really heart broken. Lina really messed him up,” Austin said, and Stanley chuckled. 

    “What’s that with him?” Stanley asked, and grabbed the paper from him. 

    “Hey! Give that back!” Micheal yelled. 

    “Bring it!” Austin said, and grabbed it from Stanley 

    “Is that your to-do list?” Stanley teased. 

   “Hey! Bring it back. I mustn’t loose that!” Michael lashed, going after Austin. 

   “If you really care about that list, buddy, just leave him be. Else, you might really lose it,” Stanley said, and Micheal exhaled heavily because he knows Stanley was right. 

   “Holding hands while walking down the street, boarding the bus together, a visit to the market square, going do the amusement park with people around, riding a bike together, playing games together at an arcade, eat barbecue together at the roadside, singing at a karaoke together, and call it a day at the field, while staring at the lay night sky,” Austin reed the list out loud, and Micheal exhaled heavily. 

    “Can I have it back?” Micheal snapped, and grabbed it from him. 

     “Is that what you plan to do together?” Within teased, and Stanley chuckled. 

    “What? What’s wrong with the list?” Micheal blurted. 

   “No wonder she ditched you. If I saw that creepy  list, I would have ditched you as well,” Austin teased, and Stanley laughed, unable to hid it in. 

    “I think it’s fun,” Micheal grumbled. 

    “You think this is fun? No wonder Ava broke up with you?” Austin blurted, and Stanley hit him playfully. 

    “That’s enough, dude. Mike has been single for too long, let’s blame it all on his singleness,” Stanley said, still laughing, and Micheal pouted his lips innocently. 

    “It sounds like why Lina would like,” Steven said, and they all looked at him. 

    “What? Even you!” Austin and Stanley shouted. 

    “It’s all my fault, I should have organized this dating lecture on time, at least you would’ve been able to avoid this lackey list,”  Austin grumbled. 

    “Get yourself a new girl first,” Stanley teased. 

    “Hey! What’s that about?” He shouted. 

    “You haven’t said anything about that new girl of yours, neither did you introduce her to us. Why? Have you guys breakup?” Stanley teased. 

   “What? Speaking of which…” Austin muttered, and took his phone to see several missed calls from Rae. “Hey! Gotta run!” He shouted, and ran out. 

    “Are you going on a date?!” Stanley shouted after him. “I think he is hiding her from us,” he m grumbled. 

   “But she was the one who came up with the list,” Micheal muttered in a whisper, looking at the list. 

    “Obviously! It’s just like Lina to like weird things like  that,” Steven said, and Micheal looked at him. 

    “But it’s not that weird. I think it would be fun,”  he said, and Steven patted his shoulder. 

    “You suit each other,” Steven said. 

   “What does that mean?” Michael snapped. 

    “Nothing. I should have followed Austin,” Steven said, and Micheal scoffed. 

     “What? What are you guys discussing?” Stanley asked. 

    “Are you starting to whine like Austin?” Steven teased, and Micheal chuckled. 

   “He should hear that,” Stanley said, and they all laughed. “Since Mike got ditched, and Austin went on a date, we should do something as well.  What do you guys think?” He asked. 

    “I’ll head out first,” Steven said, and carried his bag. 

   “What? Is that a no! Mike, what do you think?” 

    “She promised to call… I wonder what happened,” Micheal grumbled. 

    “What’s all this really? Fine! I also have other plans!” Stanley shouted, and left.

    “Send someone to confirm his current location,”

   “Mr. Packard, you are more cunning than I anticipated. I let my guards down,” he thought, with clenched fists.

 They left the warehouse, and went to the location the prosecutor was, having lunch with his colleagues. 

 They dialed his number, but he rejected thier calls and Mr. Ethan chuckled. 

    “You are right. He switched sides,” Mr. Ethan said. 

   “What do we do now, boss?” Don asked. 

   “What we know how to do best,” Mr. Ethan muttered, and smirked.

    “It’s on the house, sir,” 

   “Oh… miss, how about ours?” His colleagues complained, and Mr. Denis chuckled. 

    “Thank you,” he muttered, and slipped from the drink.

 He saw a note under the juice and the others tried to peep into it.

   *Since you decide to play foul, there is no mercy for you. In the next 30 minutes, the poison will start it effect on you, your body will start itching by now. Come to the back of the restaurant quietly to get the antidote.* it reads and Mr. Denis’s eyes widened in shock. 

   “What’s it?” The colleague asked, and wanted to collect the note from him but he threw it in the drink. 

  His hands trembled in fear, and his body began to itch him. 

   “Mr. Denis, are you ok?”

   “I think I need to use the restroom,” with that he hurried to the back to the restaurant. 

   He got to the back of the restaurant, and Don approached him. 

   “You thug, where is the antidote?” He shouted, grabbing Don’s collar. 

    “This way, boss is waiting,”

   “Do you expect me to follow you? Bring the antidote now or I arrest you?” 

    “Seems like you don’t need it,” Don teased, and intend to leave. 

  Mr. Denis scratched his hair, and dragged Don back. 
*
  Don lead him to a car, and drove off. 

   “Where are you taking me?”

   “To where the antidote is,”

   “How can I trust you?”

   “Because you have no choice. We are running out of time, it only takes 30 minutes for the poison to spread, and we already spent few minutes,”

   “Hurry up! Where exactly is it? Why do you have to keep it that far?” He yelled, and Don smirked. 
*
  He pulled over at an empty restaurant, and they went in to meet Mr. Ethan, who welcomed them with a smirk. 

   “You sneaky thug, give me the antidote,” he shouted, and Don held him still. 

    “You… you betrayed me! Why should I give this to you?” Mr. Ethan sneered, showing him a bottle. 

   “Is that it? Is that the antidote?” Mr. Denis muttered, aiming to grab it but Don didn’t release him. 

   “Mr. Prosecutor, I thought you took a vow? But why did you let a criminal go?” Mr. Ethan teased. 

   “I should have remembered that and locked you up first. You are nothing but a thug, thinking you can destroy someone in power. Those people might be politicians, but they know how to play it more than you, stinky thug!” He blurted, and Don punched him. 

   “Stop! Don’t let him get to you. He have barely 10 minutes left after all. Let him continue spouting nonsense till then,” Mr. Ethan jeered, and Mr. Denis fell to his knees 

   “Please, give me the antidote,” he cried, scratching his body vigorously.

   “Please… I don’t want to dle,”

  “Is that it? Is that why you let the powerful politician go? Because you don’t want to dle? Is that how important your life is? Then I’m honored to have the privilege to watch that life fade away with the antidote with me,”

   “I didn’t intend to betray you, Mr Packard is more dangerous that we thought,”

    “We? I also thought there was ‘we’ I also thought we were on same side, but it didn’t even take you a week for you to drift away to the opposite direction,”

   “There is no way I can get the warrant against Mr. Packard, and I can’t get his confession either. Even the search warrant wasn’t approved!”
    
   “Was that why you let him go and take his side instead?”

   “No, I had other reasons,”
    
    “Too bad… you have just few minutes left,”

   “Please… give me the antidote,”

    “I was enjoying your story,”

    “Mr. Packard threatened me with my daughter!” he lamented.

  Mr. Denis stayed all day with Mr. Packard in the interrogation room, but even after all the threats, Mr. Packard still refused to confess. 

   “Who is behind you? Are you trying to get revenge for your brother? What did your brother fed you with?” 

   “Please!” Mr. Denis shouted, and hit the table. “Why are you asking questions? In the one meant to ask you questions. Why aren’t you calling your lawyer? What are you up-to?”

   “Me? You will let me go when you get nothing from me. An innocent man doesn’t need to lift a finger to prove his innocence,” Mr. Packard said, and smirked.

  SOMEWHERE IN AN UNKNOWN PLACE^^^
^
  Paulina struggled on  the chair she was tied to, her hands and legs were tied up and her mouth was covered. She groaned in tears, watching the two men who didn't seem to be bothered.

Who could have put them up to this? She thought because they seem to be waiting for an order.

They heard roar coming from outside, and the leader ask the other to go out to check. Few minutes later, the other smirked at her and left as well.

She'd struggled on the chair but it was impossible for her to loose the rope. She groaned, struggling in pain praying to loose it and run out before they return when someone barged in.

She looked up in fear, and her eyes brightened to see Michael.

Lina! He shouted, and ran to her.

He removed the tape in her mouth, and embraced her.

Mike....how did you? 

Don't ask questions, I'm glad you are safe. We need to get out of here. He said and started to untie the rope.

Stanley and Steven joined later, and she opened her mouth agape.

Lina, are you ok? Stanley asked worriedly and she nodded.

Did you all fight those men on your own? She asked and they all looked at Steven.

They were not that many, they were four of them. Austin already called the police, Michael said.

Do you know who they are? Stanley asked and she shook her head.

They seem to be receiving orders from someone. I don't know whos sent them, she cried.

They're my father's men, Lina got hurt because of me, Steven muttered and they all looked at him in shock.

  Micheal tossed and turned on his bed staring at his phone expecting Paulina’s call. He called her severally but she screened his calls, neither did she read nor reply his messages. 

   “Something must be off, Lina can’t screen my call like this,” he thought, and called on the MASS group chat. 

📳Austin: *Bro, wassup. Steve, Mike is on the call.*
📳Stanley: *Don’t start! You aren’t calling to whine about Lina, are you?*
📳 Micheal (gasped): Can you hack into my mind? You are indeed a genius.*

📳Micheal: *Wait. I really think something is off. Paulina has never screened my calls like this before.*

📳Austin: *I’m leaving too, and you should do the same* (he left)
📳Micheal:*Wait… Austin…*
📳Steven: *Good night. bro* (he left)
  

  “Not you too,” Michael grumbled, and dropped the call. 

  He sent another message to Paulina, and called her again, but this time her number was out of reach. 

   “What? Now it’s turned off!” He shouted. “This might really be serious,” he thought, and remembered seeing the driver smirked before leaving. “What was that about? Why would he smirk?” He thought, turning on his bed. “I should message Stanley privately,” he concluded, and slide into Stanley’s DM. 

  He sent a message to Stanley, who replied almost immediately. 
.
   💬Him: *bro, I really think something is off. Help me please😞
  💬Stanley: *Grosssss… that emoji freaks me out! 😖.

   “I don’t dare…”

   “No, boss, no. No body dares,”

   “Better! Crawl away,” be muttered, and raised him fingers to count down.

 Paulina has been confined in her room since the incidence, she has always been scared to leave the room, and haven’t been going to school.  The MASS were worried all worried about her, and have been checking up on her constantly on the phone. Micheal kept in touch everyday, and they mostly take hours on the phone. To her upmost surprise, Sage sent her a message as well, and she couldn’t think of the right response. She cant  tell him about the incident, and couldn’t come up with an excuse either, so she ignored his message instead. Sophia also sent her countless messages which she ignored, she pretended to angry at them, even though she was actually mad at Sophia. 

  She decide to keep herself busy, and started reading articles online about the Floods’, the family were a great family until they faced demise when their secret was revealed. The thought of the ragged-man being her grandfathers, and her mother suffering that much she had to take care of her alone made her wonder if those were all rewards for thier crimes. 

  She put away her laptop, and laid flat on her bed, that has been her routine since the accident. The police stopped thier investigation regarding lord shark gang after they saw the pendant at various marketplace, and teenagers also own one. They assumed that the pendant was an ordinal pendant, so the driver who already fled became thier major suspect. 

She took her phone later when a message beeped in, but a knock on the door distracted her, and the followed the maid to meet her father, there he introduced her to Don, and she felt comfortable with him the moment she saw him, because he looked so familiar—he appeared with her grandfather in some of the articles, seeing him clarified that she couldn’t be mistaken. The tattoo on his neck was visible in the pictures, and he didn’t try to hide it either. 

    “It was him, the man in the picture with my grandfather,” she thought, and found herself addressing him as “uncle” and when he shared t he story about the lady who was like a sister to him, she instantly assumed that he must be referring to her mother.  

  Having him around made her feel reassured that she would be safe, and the instantly had the confidence to return to school.

  She got ready for school the following day, and got ready for school. 

   “Miss Paulina, are you finally returning to school?” Naomi, the maid who mostly attend to her say, and she chuckled. 

    “Of course, I should. I have been slacking behind on classes, and the mid semester exam starts next week,” she said, and Naomi gasped. 

    “Wow. That’s so soon. Wiki you be fine, tho?”

   “I should. Dad assured me that my grades don’t matter, that’s my bail,” she said, and they both chuckled. 

   She left the room, descending the stairs, when she saw Ava also ready for school. 

    “Wow, look who’s finally returning to school,” Ava teased, but she ignored her and continued on her way. 

  They both got to the parking lot, and Ava’s driver helped her with her bag.  

    “What will you do now, poor Lina, at least in my case, my driver is trustworthy,” Ava mocked. 

    “I’ve really been trying to avoid picking up fights with you. Isn’t it too early to start a cat fight?” Paulina lashed, and Ava glared at her. 

    “What did you say?”

   “At least in my case, my ears are working properly!” Paulina sneered, and Ava inter to hit her, but Don was quick to help her hand. 

    “Let go of me! Who are you?” Ava shouted. 

    “My bodyguard! So be warned,”

    “Bodyguard?” Ava laughed. “Who do you tnunk you are to get a bodyguard? Because you almost got kidnapped, does that made you feel like an elite?” 

    “Why? Does reality hurt. I might be an illegitimate, but it didn’t change the fact that I’m an elite, sister,” 

    “Who the heck is your sister?”

    “Get a life, I’m late for class!” Having said that, she went to the car. 

    “That little…” Ava shuddered, and Don smiled before leaving. “Hey! Who do you think you are?” Ava shouted. 

  Don scared her with the car before driving off, and Paulina laughed at the sllly attempt.

  Paulina freed herself from Sophia’s grip when they got to the rooftop, and placed her hands akimbo.

    “You’re right!” With that, she ran off.

   “Not that. But she ditched me again!”

   “Do you think she is trying to avoid us?” Stanley asked, and Micheal looked at him. 

    “What would she?”
    
   “Who wouldn’t? She almost got hurt because of us,”

    “How does it correlate? We were the ones who saved her!”

   “But she was abducted because of Steve!”

    “Oh… could that be it?”

    “I think she is avoiding us. Even Steve is,”

    “Steven went to his relatives, he said he needed a short break, he will be back before the mid semester exam,”

    “You think so? What relative? He is on the run, and you believe he went to his relatives? We already lost a friend again! Austin is the only person that benefited from this.     “Austin is in a secret relationship,”

   “What?” Don’t make me laugh. Secret relationship could fit anyone but Austin. How could he date anyone in secret? He loves to flaunt. And why would he even date secretly?”

    “I don’t know… but do you know is new girl? He seem to be dating someone, but he is keeping it a secret from us. What could have caused that?”

    “Do you think it’s one of your ex?”

   “I only dated 3 girls, and they aren’t even from this school. But Austin is surely seeing someone at school,” 

    “Your crush?”

    “Jessica?”

    “So Jessica is your crush?” Micheal gasped. 

    “Come on, Austin knows better than to date Jessica, I mean… she would bluntly reject him,”

    “But you think she will accept you?”

    “I think this is about Austin,”

    “Answer the question,”

    “No body can tell, until it’s done,”

    “So, are you going to confess?” Micheal asked, with widened eyes. 

    “She is giving me the green light, but she can be unpredictable. Just till after the math competition. I will see how it goes then,”

    “ see you can’t give that up for love,”

    “Love? That’s too cheesy. Why would you use love? I like her, and that’s it,”

    “Like, love? What’s the big deal?” 

    “Love… the word carries too many weight, I can only use with my mother,”

    “I’m envious of you,”

    “That’s out of the blue. What’s there to be envious about?”

     “You’ve a close relationship with your mother, but that wasn’t the case with either of mine. I mean… your parents care about you a lot,”

     “Why are you suddenly like Austin?”

    “Austin parents always criticize him, my parents… I don’t even want to talk about it. But you, you got it all. You’re a genius, and you also get along with your parents as well,”

    “Austin, is that you?” Stanley snapped, shaking him vigorously, and Micheal laughed. 

   “Austin is gone,” Micheal muttered, and he by both laughed.

    “The albino twins?”

    “Nah… they are both in a relationship now,”

    “Too bad…” Micheal muttered, and looked at him. “Why does it seem like you know something?”

   “Me? I know nothing!” He blurted, and looked away. 

    “More reasons to doubt you. That denial is too blunt,”

    “I have promised not to pry into others private lives, most importantly my friends. But I can’t just help it. Austin was trying to hard to keep it a secret,”

    “What? Who is the girl?” Micheal asked, already curious. 

    “I really didn’t mean to pry,”

    “Quit that man! Tell me who she is. The girl he is dating in secret. Does she worth it? Who’s she? A junior? There is no way he could date a junior student,”

    “It’s Rae,”

    “Who’s Rae ?” Micheal asked, and gasped later.  “What? Rae? Lia’s friend, Rae?”

    “I’m not certain, but it could only be her,”

    “What makes you think that?”

    “They met by the poolside whenever no one is around, and others lonely places. My eyes almost get sore by trying to find this in the cctv. Gosh…”

    “Why would he date Rae?”

    “I don’t know. He had a crush on her ones, but he gave up when she was with Roy, so I’m also confused,”

   “Well… if he chose to date her in secret, good for him. Let’s wait for him to split it. But why does it seems like we are dating?”

   “We?”

  “I mean… Austin is dating someone in secret, Lina ditched me for her friends, and Steven abandoned us. The two of us are always together these days, doesn’t it seem like we are dating?”

    “What? That’s absurd? Mike, have you finally lost it?”

   “I think so. I’m losing my mind. I got ditched for the second time, while others are dating in secret. But good thing I’m not alone,” 

    “He is crazy,” Stanley muttered, and got up. A message beeped into his phone later, and he smiled. “I guess you are alone,”

    “Who’s that?”

    “Jessica. She wants to meet up,”

    “For what?”

    “She wants me to help her with a math problem,”

    “What? Why would she ask for your help? She has professional private tutors,”

     “I know, right? This is a brighter green light. I’m off,” he winked before taking the exit. 

     “I’m all alone this time,” Micheal thought, and threw the to-do list away. 
**
**
 Paulina, Sage, and Sophia hanged out after school, after spending hours at  an arcade, they accompanied Sophia to shop before they went to a snacks bar to get some snacks. All through the evening, Don was watching them closely from afar. 

   “Am I the only one who seems like we’re being followed?” Sophia muttered, looking at Don discreetly.

   “Followed? By who?” Paulina asked, looking around with mouth full of shawarma. 

   “That man. I also noticed that he has been on our track from school,” Sage said. 

    “Are you referring to uncle Don?” Paulina asked, and Sophia choke on her drink. 

    “Is he your uncle?” Sophia gasped. 

    “No, my bodyguard,”

    “Bodyguard?” Sage and Sophia shouted.

    “Why do you have one?” Sage asked. 

    “It’s a long story,” she muttered. 

    “Does it have anything to do with your absence from school?” Sage asked, and she nodded. 

    “What happened?” Sophia asked, already worried. 

    “It’s a long story, and it’s meant to be a secret, but I’ll share,” she muttered, and they paid full attention to her. 
**

   AUSTIN’S SUITE***
*
  Austin sat beside Rae who was trying to explain a particular mathematics problem to him. His attention was focused on her as she try her best to explain it. 

  He noticed a strand of hair on her face, and helped her adjusted it.

    “Nothing. There was a strand of hair on your face,”

    “You aren’t even paying attention,”

    “Was I?”

    “You aren’t paying attention like before, is it because your parents already cut you some slack?”

    “What are you talking about?”

    “I’m sorry for prying, but I saw your message, your friends were rejoicing because your parents finally cut you some slack,”

    “Really?”

    “Why are you still receiving lectures from me when you don’t even need it anymore?”

    “Why are you still coming here then?”

    “Because you keep calling me,”

    “But you should have said no,”

    “What are you insinuating?”

    “I kept calling you because I want to be this close to you,” he muttered, and moved his face close to hers. 

    “What are you doing right now?” 

   “Push me away if you don’t want it,” he whispered, and she swallowed hard. 

  He placed a kiss on her lips, and she devoured his lips, responding to the kiss passionately.

**
 The petition against Mr. Packard was approved, and his lawyer went to get bail on probation.

  After leaving the prosecutor’s office, he met with Grace at one of his guest house. 

    “Dad, what’s this? The election is behind the corners, and now all these… my range already dropped, and I might lose at this rate!” She yelled, and he chuckled. 

   “You can abandon me, Grace, I give you the permission to,”

   “Dad…”

   “If you can’t even abandon me, then what can you do?” He yelled. 

   “Dad…” she cried. 

   “You’re nothing without me, that’s why you are affected by this. You can’t even get married without my involvement, you can’t keep your life together, and you can’t handle the election as well. And you can’t abandon me, either. What can you do on your one, Grace? Nothing. You’re nothing without me,”

    “So what should I do?” She cried. “You’re right, I’m nothing with you. I can’t do anything on my own, so what do I do now?” She lament. 

    “I was able to leave on bail, but the petition was approved, so I’ll have to go to court. In order to get out of this, I’ve to win the trails,”

    “How do we do that, dad? They’ve a witness, and evidence. Your connection could only bail you out, do we have to bribe the judge?”

    “The judge in charge is not an easy judge, that’s why we need a lawyer that can beat all judges,”

    “I’ll get you all the best lawyers, dad,”

   “No, you shouldn’t,”

    “Dad…”

   “Get me only the best lawyer,”

    “Who?”

   “The best lawyer in the world. Louis Narson,”

   “I thought he retired,”

    “He might have retired, but that didn’t revoke nis license. His daughter will be the judge in charge, and he never lose a case,”

    “Dad, he is currently the president’s advisor, and mentor, do you think he would want to defend you?”

    “Then be useful for once. You will get him to represent me,”

    “Dad, why me?”

    “Because that will be the first time you will be useful. The first hearing is before the final election, so you should guarantee a win, I’ve to be innocent yo guarantee you win, because you can’t abandon me,”

    “Dad…”
**

  MR. ETHAN’S SAFEHOUSE^^^

  Mr. Ethan paced around in his warehouse after getting report that Mr. Packard was released. 

    “Why would they release him? There is so many evidence that could get him locked up till the trial, why would they release him?” 

    “The Packard’s are really powerful people, he might be plotting a way to escape this,” Leo muttered.

    AT A HOTEL^^^
^
  Steven was about to sleep when he heard a knock on the door, he already told all the staffs that he doesn’t need anyone to clean his room, so no one knocks at his door, and whenever he orders something, they usually place a single knock at his door, and leave his order. 

  He didn’t order anything, and whoever was there wouldn’t stop knocking either, so he angrily went to open the door, and was startled to see his father. 

    “Dad…” he shuddered, and was about to close the door back when he saw his friends walking towards their direction. “That’s my friends, would you be fine?” He muttered, and his father chuckled. 

    “Are you now worried about me?” 

    “You won’t hurt them, would you?”

    “Let’s talk later, I will be around,” with that his father left. 

    “Steve, who is that man?” Austin asked. 

    “Why are you here? Aren’t you supposed to be with your relatives?” Stanley asked. 

     “Aren’t you going to welcome us in?” Micheal asked

   “I’m sorry, guys, I don’t want to get involved with you all anymore. You are my friends, and you might all get hurt because of me,”

    “I said we were going to come up with a plan, but you suddenly disappeared again with a lame excuse!” Stanley blurted. 

     “You shouldn’t have tried to find me, I keep up with you guys online because I didn’t want you to worry,  you shouldn’t have bothered. I’m fine on my own!” He said, and was about to close the door, but Micheal held it. 

    “What nonsense are you spewing right now? Lina almost got hurt because of you, but you saved her. What matters is that no one gets hurt, so we have to come up with a plan to sort this out completely,” Micheal said. 

    “I don’t want  anyone to get hurt because of me…” he exclaimed, and broke down in tears.

   CHAIRMAN HEFLET’S MANSION^^^

    Stanley was engrossed in his thoughts in his room when his mother tapped him. 

   “Mum, when did come in?”

   “A while ago, I knocked, I even called you but you seem engrossed in your thoughts. Do you mind to share?” 

    “Mum, it’s nothing really,”

    “Is it about a girl?”

    “And that too,”

    “So you have a lot of things bothering you,”

     “Mum, I don’t want to bother you with my silly problems,”

    “Is it the girl you told me about?”

    “Yeah, Jessica,”

    “Jessica Lockwood?”

    “Yeah, it’s her,”

    “Wow. You sure have good taste in women,”

    “I’m even confused about the whole thing,” he muttered, and he remembered what happened earlier. 
.*
*
  FLASHBACK***
*

Stanley explained the mathematics problem to Jessica, which she obviously understood. He chuckled at the thoughts that she only used that as an excuse to see him. 

    “Thanks a lot for this. Even if you aren’t in the math club, I can still use your help. I’m thrilled,”

    “Jess, is this a green light?”

    “I don’t follow,”

    “You never asked for my help with any math problems, so I was just wondering if there are other reasons,”

    “Reasons, like…” 

    “Like this?” He muttered, and pecked her cheek. 

      “Do you think I’m giving you a green light?”

    “If it’s not, then what should I call this? You obviously understood the equation, you could ask help from your private tutors if you don’t understand. Everyone at school knows that your current private tutor was a professor from Harvard, your father paid a fortune to hire him, you have many other capable tutors, you could use their help, so why me?”

    “If you think that means I’m giving you a green light, then you aren’t the genius they call you. Thanks anyway!” With that, she left.

    “What’s that about? Did I made a mistake?” He grumbled, staring at her retreating back.

 FLASHBACK ENDS***

  He concluded, and his mother chuckled.

    “She just made an advance, is that’s what you call it,”

    “What? You mean, she likes me too and… mum, that’s the green light!” He shouted. 

    “So, what’s next?”

    “I’m going to find a perfect time to ask her out,”

    “Not that,”

    “Then what?”

    “She said that’s also a problem, Jessica isn’t the main cause of your problems,”

    “Oh, that…”

    “Did you pry on someone again? Your friend, perhaps,”

     “Mum… my friends always call me a genius, but you’re the real genius here,”

    “I know you did, that could only be the cause of your worries. Which of your friend this time?”

    “It’s Steven,”

    “What did you find?”

   “He lied to us about going to his relatives, I have been having doubts ever since, and when I tracked his phone location, he was somewhere nearby, o traced the location yesterday, and I found out that he was at a hotel. A lot of things have been going on in his life lately, and we want to help him together as friends, but he kept running away. What do I do? I can’t tell my friends how I figured it out, and it kept bothering me. I don’t want them to get mad at me, and I also want us to help Steven together as friends. What do I do, mum?” 

   She exhaled heavily, and patted his back. “Do shah you think is right. You are a genius, son. You’ve been a genius since i was pregnant with you. You knew the right time to kick to comfort me, and also what to crave for at the moment. You never gave me a hard time when you were born; you leaned to do things faster than other kids… you’ve always been a genius son. I’m so proud of myself for being your mother,” she said, and he chuckled. 

    “That flattered me,”

    “It’s all the truth. So what do you decide?”

    “I’ll bring it up, but I won’t tell them I already find out. I’ll push them, to see their reaction,”

    “Here we go again, my genius,” she said, and he smiled.

    “Lord shark?” Steven muttered, and they all looked at him. 

    “Why would he attack your friends?” Mr. Hearst asked bavk. 

    “It’s not because of me,” Steven grumbled. 

     “Young man, does that answer your question?” Mr. Hearst said to Micheal. 

    “Paulina didn’t get hurt because of Steven, does that mean someone else tried to hurt Paulina? Who could it be?” Micheal said, and started to panic. 

    “You’ve to be guarded, Kelvin’s men are on the look out for you. He helped you escape from the basement, and now he is keeping a close watch on you. What do you think?” 

    “Lord shark wanna get rid of me?” Steven muttered. 

   “You have more enemies than you think, and your friend also has hers. Talk later, entertain your guest, see you again, young man,” having said that, he patted Michael’s shoulder and exited the room. 

   Austin exhaled heavily after he left, and held his chest. 

   “Your father was really intimidating, thank goodness he left, I finally got to breathe properly,” Austin exhaled. 

    “Mike, you were really amazing back there, how do you manage to have a conversation with him?” Stanley asked.

 After Leo left prison years ago, he saw the news about Grace and how she is living happily with her family. Thinking it through, they were never in a relationship, he was a club escort that she grew fond of to satisfy her. When Grace got involved in a car accident while drunk driving, they pinned the blame on him and sent him to prison without trail—but he couldn’t comprehend their intentions, until he came out to hear the new of her marriage, and it collide with the time he went to prison. He thought about it all, and wondered why she had to send him to prison in order to get married when she could just cut ties with him. 

    “She is living happily with her husband, she even has a daughter now. But must she make me miserable to achieve that?” He thought, and his eyes widened in realization. “If wants to desperately get rid of me for no good reason, then there must be a secret behind it,” he thought, and did a thorough search on Grace’s family. “Her daughter, why does her daughter’s age and birthday collide with my calculations?” He pounded, and remembered seeing a pregnancy test kit in her car the last time they met. “She didn’t have a boyfriend then, could…” he gasped at the thought, and decide to find out—that could only be possible if he could access the house.

   “I’m done here!” Ava said, and left.

    “Not that again!” Lia snapped.

I refuse to accept you to the math club because I can’t stand to be belittled by you, the other members will also feel inferior to their knowledge, so I decline all your requests.     “The won’t end anyways. Either of us has to dle for it to end. You won’t divorce him because of me, so when I dle, you will be free, or you will be free yourself once you are gone. It can only work either way,”

  💬*The Hewlett’s family are falling apart*

   “Yes, that’s a brave step,” he muttered, covering himself up with the blanket.

  They managed to calm him down, and he looked away in embarrassment.

 CHAIRMAN STANFORD’S MANSION^^^
^
  Grace has been thinking all night about how to escape her present predicament, revealing Ava’s identity in the press conference would simply result to stepping down from the election. Paul will send Ava abroad, and she can’t even imagine what will happen to her afterwards. 

   “He is going to send Ava abroad, and abandon me since he never wanted this marriage from the start. I can’t let that happen, I can’t lose it all “ she cried, looking at her range that already fell massively. “Even if I can’t become the mayor, I won’t give up on Paul “ she muttered. “First, he left the bedroom for me, then next what? He will  leave the marriage. That can’t happen, I won’t lose to Charlotte. Never!” She shouted, shoving everything on her bed away. 

  After thinking all night, an idea came to her mind, so she called her campaign manager immediately to plan things with her before heading to Ava’s room. 

    “Mum, what are you doing here?” Ava asked, and she signaled her to be quiet. 

    “Pack few things, and quietly follow me,”

   “What? Where are we going?”

   “The press conference tomorrow, your father is going to announce that you aren’t his biological daughter and send you abroad,”

    “What? But he promised to protect me!”

   “That’s his way of protecting you. He is going to send you abroad, annul our marriage and we will know everything to Paulina,”

    “I can never lose to her,”

    “That’s why you’ve to come with me,”

   “What do you plan to do?”
    
    “Paul will never leave you to be with me, he knows you aren’t his daughter even before you were born, and he protected you and loved you very much, so we have to escape that press conference, until I am able  to sort it out,”

    “How will you do that?” 

    “If I leave with you, Paul will want to you back by all means, so I’ll tell him I can’t let you live as a ba$tard. Even if you go abroad, others will still trample on you. I will make realize that, and you will quietly go abroad while we sort out the mess “

    “Mum, are you sure about this?” 

   “Trust me, Ava. This is our only way out,”

    “Can’t we talk it out with him now?”

    “He won’t listen. I tried, but he didn’t listen. Have you forgotten the saying? Absence makes the heart grow fonder. If we leave now, he will understand how serious this is,”

    “Ok, I want to trust you for now. So, where do we do?” 

    “Somewhere no one can find us. Kira already prepared everything, and she is waiting for us by the back gate,”

    “Mum…”

   “This is our last chance Ava, if you want to remain the heiress of first group forever, then you will do this. I can’t let you lose to Paulina, never!

  Ava packed few things in a backpack, and quietly followed her mother to the back gate, there they met Kira waiting for them. 

  Kira drove for almost two hours, and she pulled over in an old abandoned building. 

    “Why are we here?” Ava blurted. 

    “Where is this place?” Grace asked. 

   “May-.. ma’am, you ask me to find a place out of reach from the media, where no one knows you, this is the place, ma’am,”

    “You couldn’t think of a better place?” Grace blurted. 

    “Ma’am, it was a rush and this is the quickest place to find. This is my grandparent’s old house,”

    “What?” Grace and Ava shouted. 
*
  They entered the building, that looks like it could collapse any moment, with leaking roof, and broken water pipes. 

   “Mum, I would return home and accept whatever dad has to differ!” Ava bluntly said, and turned to leave but Grace held her back. 

   “I also can’t live like this, but the fight worth it. We won’t spend another night here, I promise,”

   “The thought of spending a night here is making me lose my mind!” Ava yelled. 

    “Same! I am losing it!” Grace fired back, starling Ava. “But we have to endure. I’ll send the adress to Paul, he will come and pick us up tomorrow,”

  Mr. Ethan was sat on his bed thinking of the Packard’s when one of his men barged into his room.

    “Boss, Ms. Charlotte was abducted!”

    “Good! I know that creepy man will fall for the bait. Let’s see how he will get out of this,” Mr. Ethan muttered.before taking his phone to call Louis. 

 📞*hello*

  THE ABANDON HOUSE^^^
^
  Ava was unable to sleep well on night, and when she managed to, she dreamt that Paul announced to the world that she was a ba$tard, and Paulina was his only daughter, sending she and her mother away, and they moved thier belongings to the abandoned house. Waking up after the nightmare to see herself at the abandoned house almost traumatized her that wasn’t out of her nightmare. 

    “Dad loves me, he will never abandon me “ she tried to reassure herself until day breaks. 

  The house was out of water due to the broken pipe, after exchanging words with her mother, she left angrily. 

  She walked around the neighborhood thinking about everything that has happened so far, Paulina sudden appearance in her life, and losing everything to her. 

   “Never, Ava. You can’t lose to her. You are a queen, and you will forever be,” she muttered. 

  The neighborhood was so quiet, the few passerby didn’t even spare her a glance. After walking around for few houses. Walking around really helped her ease her worries, and she returned when it started raining. 

 She ran back to the house, and the house was already flooded because of the leaking roof. 

  “I should have just stayed home if water will leak out anyways,” she grumbled in annoyance, barging to the room to see the shock her of life—her mother was lying lifelessly on the floor with the bottle of sleeping pills beside her. “Mum, mum. Mum, get up,” she said, hitting her. 

  She collected her phone from her, and saw the message Grace sent to her father. 
  💬*you failed, dad. You failed to make me the mayor. You failed to make Paul mine forever* the message reads.  

    Ava’s eyes widened in shock, and she glance at the document on the floor, she picked it up and was startled to see that it was a divorce paper, with her father signature there. 

   “What? Dad is going to divorce mum,” she thought, and put the paper away. “Mum, get up and stop scaring me,” she cried, hitting her mother vigorously. 

  She was hitting her mother in tears, and reached  her phone to call for help when the house suddenly went ablaze. 
.

 CHAIRMAN STANFORD’S MANSION^^^
^
 Paul woke up when his phone beeped from a message from Grace. He exhaled heavily, wondering why she sent him a message. He opened the message and was shocked by its  contempt. In it, she sent the address of her current location, and tell him to come get them if he really won’t abandon them. 

    “What’s this?” He muttered, and put the phone away. 

  He went to his room to freshen up heading out, when Paulina walked up to him. 

    “Good morning, dad,”

    “I hope you slept well?” 

    “Dad, can you give me a ride to school?” She requested, and he looked at her. 

    “A ride? I wish I could, but I’m in a hurry now,”

   “Then who will take me to school?”

    “What happened to Don?”

    “His brother got into an accident, and needed surgery, his presence is needed as the guardian,”

    “And he left without informing me?”

    “It was urgent, and I gave him permission,”

    “Ok. I’ll call my secretary to give you a ride,” he said, but she held his hand. 

     “I can only be safe with you, dad. I only trust you and Don,”

    “Paulina…”

   “You must have been busy that day, but you rushed to school for Ava,” 

    “Are you jealous of your sister?”

    “She is not my sister, and I’m not jealous of her. I’m just upset. Even when I’m your biological daughter, I have to live like this because you didn’t find me sooner, and I have to watch you pay all attention to Ava because you watch her grow up, and loved her very much. Who am I to compete with her?” 

   “You don’t have do compete with her,”

    “I know. I know I don’t have to, but why do I feel like it’s needed,”

    “I’ll give you a ride, let’s talk more on our way,” he said, and she nodded. 
*
  Paul drove off in silence for a while, and he broke the silence after driving few blocks away from home. 

     “I’m sorry, Lina,” he muttered, and she looked at him. 

   “About what?”

   “I was a c0ward. I didn’t know I was hurting you by paying so much attention to Ava,”

    “I know you can’t abandon her, and I’ll always have to live as the hidden illegitimate daughter, so I understand,”

    “You must be really upset,”

    “It’s hard enough to think that you abandoned my mother who was your childhood friend because of her family’s crisis to marry Ms. Grace who was carrying another’s man baby in order to save your company, but I tried to understand. You had no choice, you’ve to save your company, so you had no choice than to abandon mum even  though she was pregnant, you abandoned her and drive her to sulcide,”

   “Paulina, there is more to the story,”

   “I’m not holding grudges for mum. I won’t hate you because of that, but I’m enraged at the thought that I will have to live this way for the rest of my life while you protect Ava and her mother. You couldn’t even protect my  own mother,” she muttered, sobbing silently. 

   Paul clenched his fist before holding her hand. 

   “I know I have failed a lot, and you are right. You don’t have to live this way, you are biological daughter, so you shouldn’t live in hidden. It might be too late, Paulina, but I’ll make sure to set things right,” he assured, and she nodded in tears. 
*
 Paul dropped Paulina off at school, and returned home. After thinking for a while, he called his secretary who arrived in less than 30 minutes. 

   “I want you to contact my lawyer, ask him to prepare divorce papers for me as soon as possible,”

   “Sir, divorce? You are going to divorce your wife?”

  “It’s not too late to set things right,”

  “How about the press conference?”

   “Grace left home with Ava, so I’ll have to attend to that alone. Bring the divorce papers to me first, I should sign it before sending it to Grace,”

    “Yes, sir,”

    “You made this choice, Grace, and it’s high time I set things right,” he muttered. 
*
  His secetary brought the divorce paper, and he signed his own part before writing a letter to Grace. 

  📝*Since you left first with Ava without consulting me, then let’s settle this without hurting anyone. Let’s end this, Grace. I never wanted this from the start, so this is the best way to call it quit. I’ll give you half of my asset, leave the country with Ava, and start a new life. As far as I’m concerned, Ava will forever remain my daughter, and you will forever be my friend, my best friend.*

  He kept the letter along with the divorce paper in a file, and gave his secretary the address it should be delivered to. 

  Few hours later, he received a call from Grace, and he exhaled heavily before picking up. 
.
📲*Divorce? How can you divorce me Paul? You said you aren’t going to abandon Ava, so what are you doing now?*
📞*I am not abandoning anyone, I’m just setting things right. Just give it a rest already, if you keep acting this way, Ava will continue to get hurt,*

.
  His secretary reminded him about the press conference, and he left the house heading to the location. 
*
   “I won’t answer any questions asides from what is necessary. Firstly, I want to inform everyone that my wife, Grace Stanford, will be stepping down as a mayor candidate,” he stated, followed by an uproar from the reporters, and many more questions followed. “That’s it about the mayor issue, and as for my daughter Ava Stanford, I would appreciate it if you all don’t interfere to my family affair, and anyone spreading any sort of rumor will face the legal team of First Group,” with that, he intend to leave. 

    “Chairman Stanford, is it true that you also have an illegitimate daughter, and that’s why you accepted your wife ba$tard as well?” A reporter asked. 

   “I want you to sue him for spreading false rumor, and every other charges that might apply to this,” Paul said to the head to his legal team before leaving. 
*
 He returned home, and returned Grace’s call, he saw several missed calls from her. 
..
📲* I never said I was going to step down,*
📞*that’s the only way to get the reporters off our case. Your range already hit the bottom, there is no way you can win the election,*
📲*since you made that decision already, why don’t we go back to how it used to be before I became a mayor candidate? I’ll go abroad with Ava until everything dles down. We used to be a happy family, Paul,*
📞*we used to be good friends, Grace, best friends. You are right, we should return to that. So sign the divorce papers,*
📲*Never! I’ll never divorce you Paul!*
📞*do your worse. We would have ended this quietly,*
📲*Paul, I know you are always intimidated by dad, I already abandoned him. Let’s give our family another chance. I promise to be good to Paulina. Please, don’t leave me…*
📞*its already too late, Grace. Let’s end this now,*
📲*what about me? What will happen to me? I might appear strong, but you know I’m very timid, I can’t do anything without dad, and I already abandoned him. You are all I have left Paul…*
📞*no, you still have Ava, and you have me too as a friend,” with that, he dropped the call. 
.
  He put away his phone and exhaled heavily. 
   
  “This is the right thing, Paul,” he muttered, trying to reassure himself. 

  A call came through from Grace again, and he picked it up already upset. 
.
📞*Grace, will you give it a rest!*
📲*I already took six pills… the normal dosage is one per day, but I took six, I’m taking another now that makes it seven, now eight,*
📞*Grace, what are you talking about? Are you drunk?*
📲*I heard that if I take overdose of my insomnia medication… it could be attempted sulcide,*
📞*Grace, what are you getting at? Don’t try anything silly,”
📲*it’s already late Paul… I swallowed 20… I can’t live without you Paul. I abandoned dad… I can’t be the mayor, and now I have to leave you? I can’t Paul…”
📞*get your tact together! I’m coming there right away!*
📲*I should leave a message to dad,*
📞*Grace, stop it already!* he shouted, and dropped the call before calling his secretary.   
*
  His secretary drove him to the abandoned house, and he met the place crowed. 

   “Are we at the right place?” He asked his secretary 

   “Yes, sir. Madam received your message directly from me at this house,”

   “What happened here?” He managed to ask one of the people around. 

   “There was a fire. We never knew people were even in,” the person replied. 

   “What fire? What was the cause of the fire?”

    “The electricity must be faulty. The fire workers confirmed that it started from an electric spark, and spread because of the fuel stored inside,”

    “What?”
   
   “It has been three months since I last saw someone enter this place. Who stores fuel in his house?” 

    “What? Fire… Grace, Ava…” he shuddered, and almost broke down but his secretary was quick to hold him. 

    “Were there any victims?” The secretary asked. 

   “Yes. There was a mother and her child. I don’t even know when they moved in. They didn’t look like they were from here,” 

    “Where are they?” Paul asked. 

    “They were rushed to the hospital,”

   “Sir, I already received an update,” the secretary said. 

     “Get in the car,” Paul said, and rushed back to his car. 

👤Who is he?”
👤”Is he her husband?”

  IN AN UNKNOWN PLACE^^^
^
  Mr. Packard pulled over at one of his guesthouse out of town. When he heard of Charlotte resurrection, he assumed that she would be the best bait to get her father to be quiet, rather than using Paulina. He asked lord shark to get her for him, and they decide on using one of his private guesthouse to avoid obstacles like the last time. Getting Mr. Ethan was easy for him after abducting Charlotte, but Mr. Ethan refuse to let go of his other evidence unless he confirmed that Mr. Packard was truly the one who held his daughter hostage. 

   “Sir, are you sure about this?” Mr. Ken asked. 

   “There is nothing to lose. I can flaunt to that Flood that he can never win me, and I’ll also witness the resurrection myself. That girl literally dled twice,” he said, and chuckled. 

   “What if it’s a trap?”

   “What trap? In my house? Who could have set it? The Flood? When his daughter is in danger? He is obviously shaken now. I just have to get him to remain quiet. Enough talking,” with that, he went in. 
*
 He was entered the building, and one of lord shark’s men escort him to where Charlotte was —there were just two men watching her, and she was tied down to a chair, with her mouth sealed. 

   “Put that Flood on the phone, let him watch the way I deal with his daughter and beg for mercy,” he said evilly, and Charlotte groaned in fear. 

  One of the men dialed a number, and within few minutes, they were surrounded by police officers in company of a prosecutor Donald, and Mr. Packard eyes widened in shock. 

   “I believe this is enough evidence to lock you up for good, Mr. Politician,” Mr. Donald said. 

    “You… you all set me up,” he muttered, in defeat. 

   “Take the criminals away!”

    “That gangster… how could betray me…” he grumbled, as the policemen handcuffed him, and escort him out. 

   “Handle him with honor, he was a former minister, and also the father of the mayor candidate that stepped down,” Mr. Donald teased. 
*
  Mr. Packard was taken to the prosecutor’s office, and his attorney joined him almost immediately, in company of Mr. Ken. 

   “Sir , there is a bad news,” Mr. Ken said. 

    “What could be bad than this?” He grumbled. 

   “There is still a way out, sir. We can pin it all on the gangsters,” his attorney said. 

   “That’s good to hear. What’s the news?”

   “Ms. Grace is d£ad,” 

    “What?” He shouted, and stood up immediately. He remembered the last message she sent to him. “My daughter… Grace… I really failed, I really failed…” he muttered, and a drop of tears slid down his face.

Don found out that Lord shark was behind Paulina’s abduction after having a chat with her about it, and he reported it to Mr. Ethan immediately. Upon getting the information, Mr. Ethan paid a visit to Mr. Hearst (Steven’s father)—they once worked on a deal together in the past, Mr. Ethan and his father were part of the few people that knew the anonymous mafia boss. Mr. Hearst was filled with guilt after Mr. Ethan told him about his granddaughter, because he owe a lot to Mr. Ethan’s father in the past. In order to set things right, he summoned lord shark over, and they set the trap for Mr. Packard together.

  MR. ETHAN’S WAREHOUSE^^^
^
  Mr. Ethan couldn’t stop laughing after he got the news of Mr. Packard’s arrest. 

    “All greedy politicians think alike, I knew he would fall for the trap,” he said, still laughing. “How is Charlotte?” He asked Don who delivered the news to him. 

    “I already sent her to Mr. Louis’s hospital, she fainted after the whole incident,”

   “That girl is really weak. I wonder how she survived this long on her own,” 

  Don checked his phone when a message beeped in, and he gasped after reading it. 

   “What’s it?” Mr. Ethan asked already impatient. 

    “Ms. Grace is d£ad,”

    “She is d£ad? So fast?” Mr. Ethan said, and Don looked at him. “How could she dle? What kllled her?”

   “He suddenly showed up by the doorway, and asked me to breakup with his daughter. That was it, he left, and I ended it,”

   “Just like that?” Michael asked. 

   “Man, you gave in like that?” Austin asked, and he nodded. 

   “I really suck, I know. I might be good with solving math problems, but I sucks at real problems so I decide to avoid it. I’m leaving anyways, I shouldn’t cause troubles for Jessica before leaving, that’s the least I can do. She has always respected her father, and followed his instructions, she thought he understands her better, so I shouldn’t make things complicated for her. We can’t be together forever, we will break up eventually, and I’m leaving too. So…” 

   “I can’t believe this. You broke up with Jessica so we aren’t getting the scooter because you didn’t last 100 days, you are leaving so we can’t get the beach house either. This is crazy,” Austin said. 

   “What beach house? Do you have a girl?” Micheal teased. 

   “No, for now!” 

  “Stan, you did the right thing. And it’s just perfect. We have exams to write, and you are leaving soon anyways so we can spend time quality while we can so your breakup is perfect,” Micheal said. 

  “Add this too, “and Lina is always studying so I get ditched every time,” that girl is such a nerd,” Austin said, and ran off before Micheal could hit him. 
.**
  Paulina arrived at school, and was rushing in from the parking lot when she bumped into someone, but t he person kept walking and didn’t look back. 

   “I’m sorry!” she shouted, and noticed that the woman dropped something. 

  She picked up the handkerchief and spun around to look for the woman, but she was already out of sight. 

   “Miss Paulina, did you forget something?” Don asked. 

   “That woman… I bumped into someone, and she dropped this,”

   “You’re late for your exam, you will return it back someday,” he said, and she looked at him. 

    “Someday?” she muttered, and looked at the handkerchief. 

 She gasped when she saw the heart symbol knitted there, she knitted that in all the handkerchief her mum had after she learnt how to knit. The handkerchief was obviously not one of the ones she knitted on, but the symbol was just the same. 

   “Mum,” she muttered, and started looking around. 

   “Miss Paulina, you are late for class,” Don said, and she nodded. She looked around one last time before she left. 
*
  Charlotte sat in a car staring at her daughter from afar, she sniffed in silently, and Louis held her hand. 

   “My poor baby,” she cried. 

   “You will be able to return to her soon, Charlotte. I’m sure of that,” he assured, and she nodded in tears. 
*
*
  WEEKS LATER***
*
  The mid semester exam ended in a week, and Stanley left as planned, but he kept in touch with his friends. Ava refused to go to school, and Paul let her do things her way. 

  The students arrived at school and few of them started to panic because the results of their last exam was out. They rushed to the board that it was pasted to check thier grades,and Paulina struggled in thier midst to get the front, but it was  impossible. 

   “Are you that impatient?” Micheal whispered in her ear before behind. 

   “They are just too many people,”

   “I’ll dismiss them for you,” he muttered, and she chuckled. “Everyone, step aside!” He shouted, and they all froze to look at him. 

   “Mike, are you seriously going to check it?” Austij whined. 

  The students all step aside, while some left and the MASS went forward with Paulina. 

   “Austin, if you are searching for your name, is at the bottom,” Steven said, and they all laughed expect Austin who grabbed Steven’s collar. 

   “Let it go man, your parents will be really proud this time,” Micheal teased. 

   “Not you too!” He shouted. 

   “Steven, you improved, you made it to top 100,” Micheal said. 

   “I did better last year,” Steven muttered. 

   “Oh,” Micheal grumbled. 

   “Speaking of equality, where is your name Mike? How come they never post your results?” Austin lashed. 

   “Because I asked them not to. There is no such thing as equality at holland high,” Micheal teased. 

   “You should have made the MASS an exemption!” Austin blurted. 

   “A particular person’s father believed that  his son will improve if his results are casted like this. But too bad… he hit the bottom,” Michael stated.  

  “That’s enough, that’s enough,” Austin muttered. 

   “Oh, Stanley took third place as usual,” Steven said. 

   “He is the genius at math but he suck at other subjects but always maintain a perfect grade,” Austin exclaimed. 

   “Jessica is first as usual, and Laura…” Micheal said. 

   “Laura Jones? I once had a crush on that girl, she is so intelligent,” Austin said. 

   “She is a nerd. How can you crush on a complete nerd?” Micheal blurted. 

   “I believe in transition. She will be superb!” Austin said. 

   “I doubt,” Steven grumbled. 

   “You should crush on intelligent ladies, it’s good for you. It will help your future gene. I can’t believe you hit bottom at supreme class and overall school,” Micheal teased. 

    “Will you stop it already?” Austin lashed. 

   “You should call him to see his result,” Micheal said, and Austin called Stanley who didn’t pickup. 

   “He must be busy at the farm,” Austin teased, and they all laughed. 

   “Paulina, Paulina Andrew, this is you,” Steven said, and they all looked at her grade. 

   “Wow, you made it to top 50!” Austin exclaimed. 

   “You deserve it, your hard work paid off,” Micheal said, and Paulina walked away. 

    “Isn’t she excited?” Austin said, and Micheal ran after her. 

  He held her hand when he caught her

   “Let me go,” she muttered 

   “Was that a bad score?”

   “That’s my worst! I have never been that bad,” she muttered, in a sober voice. 

   “But you did well,”

   “No, it was bad. I was searching for my name at the top lists, I didn’t even think of the average. I have never been that bad,” 

    “You did well, Lina. Your life took a drastic turn , you suddenly transferred to this school, get bullied, tried to fit in, and an entire new teaching system. I don’t know about others, Lina, but I’m proud of you, you did really well because you put in your best,” he said, and embraced her. 
*
Some people were not pleased with their results, while some were escalated, few others didn’t bother to check and some were unaffected with thier grades, but at the end, they all accepted their results and moved on to the next phase. 
*
*
 FEW MONTHS LATER***
*
 The school section was already coming to an end and the students started preparing for their final exams that is fast approaching, while others look forward to the exam, few others were preparing for prom dinner. 
  
 During the months that passed, Paulina, Paul and Ava have been living in harmony together until Paul insist that Paulina throw a party for her 18th birthday. Paulina protest at first because she has never celebrated her birthday before, but after much persuading, she gave in. 

  She thought of how to go about the party, and informed Sophie first who introduced her to an event planner. 

  She planned a small party with just few people, but Ava refuse to attend. 
*
 Everyone was having fun in the party, and she followed a familiar figure that she saw to the balcony, and it turned out to Ava. 

   “Ava, I thought you didn’t want to be here,” Paulina said. 

   “Why? Because it’s your party?”

    “What are you getting at?”

   “It’s a beautiful party, so you must be happy that you belong now. You must feel like a real elite now,”

   “Ava, if you are here to cause troubles, please leave. My friends are here, so don’t start a scene,” 

   “Your friends? Those friends were all mine! Everything was all mine before you showed up! Everything is mine, you are nothing but a thief!” she shouted, and Paulina laughed. 

   “Will you be the one to explain to everyone how I stole everything from you? You have been acting well all these while, were you just pretending?”

   “You think I’ll accept you as my sister?” 

    “No. Because I’m the one who needs to accept you. You are the real b@stard leaching off my family!” Paulina shouted, and Ava slapped her, and they started pulling each other’s hair. 

   “Everything will be over if you are gone!” Ava shouted, and wanted to push her down the balcony. 

   “No, no, Ava, no,” Paulina muttered, struggling with her. 

   “I won’t have to share anything when you are gone, because everything was mine from the stat!” She shouted. 
*
  Paulina’s friends were having fun at the party when they suddenly hear a loud cry. 

   “Noooooooo….” 

   “What’s that?” They shouted, and traced the voice to the balcony. 
.

 THE PARTY^^^
^
 The MAS arrived at Paulina’s party, and were ushered to the party venue. The whole hall was reserved for the party, and it makes many of them wonder who does that for an adopted child. Paulina only invited few people to the party; Micheal, Austin, Steven, Sage, Sophia, Jessica, and few other members of the math club. Lia and Alicia persuaded her for an invitation when they heard of the party, but she didn’t give them. 

   “Mike, did you heard the rumor going around school? Everyone assumed that Lina could be the chairman’s biological daughter,” Austin said. 

   “And that made me his stepson,” Micheal muttered, and they both looked at him. “What? I thought we are being unrealistic,”

    “Get lost,” Austin muttered. 

  They entered the hall, and exchanged greetings with the few people there before Paulina joined them. 

   They all sang the birthday song to her, and she blew her candles before the party begin. 

   “Happy birthday, girly,” Sophia said, and embraced her. 

   “Happy birthday, Lina,” Sage said, and she embraced him. 
    
  “Hun, hun. Lina, happy birthday,” Micheal said from behind, and she embraced him. 

  “We would have filled this place with people, what kind of party is this scanty?” Austin complained. 

   “My kind,” Paulina said, and they all laughed. 

   “Gosh, we are very few. I have never been in such party before,” Austin said. 

   “Someone else is coming to enlighten the party,” Paulina said. 

   “Who?” Austin asked. 

   “TADA!” They heard someone shout from behind, and they all spun around to see Stanley, followed by Lia and Alicia. 

   “Stanley!” The MAS shouted, and ran to embrace him. 

    “Are they always that cheerful?” Sage muttered, and Paulina smiled. 

   “They must have really missed him,” Paulina said. 

    She looked at the way Sophia was clinging to Sage and started wondering when they became that close. During the months that passed, Sophia was able to get over her feelings for Sage and thier friendship continued but there were still boundaries between them. 

   “Have you have Sage gotten closer behind me? What are you two discussing that you couldn’t carry me along?” Paulina grumbled. 

    “This close?” Sophia asked, and wrapped her hand around Sage’s waist, “or this close?” She asked and pecked his lips. 

   Paulina gasped, and dragged Sophia immediately. 

   “When? How? What?” She said confused on the first question to ask. 

    “Relax, girl,”

    “I thought you were going on blind dates, Sage fixed it for you,”

    “Yes, he did. And I went,”

    “So how did this happened?”
     
    “I went on all the 10 blind dates that he arranged, but they are not just my type. I don’t know, I feel like being an elite isn’t everything to me now. He promised to arrange another one for me but I refused, after much persuasion, I later gave in and went yesterday, and boom. It was Sage,”

   “What?”
    
   “I felt like I was hallucinating. Sage was the blind date, and he gave me a couples ring,” she said, flaunting her ring. 

    “Oh, my. This is so pretty,”

    “I know, right? He told me to stop attending blind dates, and be his girl…. Oh my God, Lina. I feel like screaming!” 

    “Sophie, I am so happy!” Paulina said, and embraced her. 

    “But where is Sage?” Sophia muttered looking around. 

   “Over there, with Alicia,” 

    “Why is that bltch at your party?”
    
     “I don’t know. I didn’t even invite her. I think she came in with Stanley,” Paulina said, but Sophia already went to drag Sage from her. 
**
  The MASS sat together later after all the cheerful moments. 

   “You didng tell us you were coming.  I mean… all the way from Spain… are you that close to  Lina?” Austin asked. 
    
    “You didng come all the way here because of the party, I’m sure of that. So, why are you back?” Micheal asked. 

     “Are you back for good?” Steven asked. 

    “So many questions, and I don’t even know which one to answer first. You are fight, I’m not in California because of the party, and I might actually be back for good,”

    “Really?” They all shouted. 

   “Did your parents made up?” Austin asked. 

    “Not that,”

    “So, what happened?” Austin asked. 

    “It’s quite a talk. Don’t let’s bore ourselves,” 

   “Say it away, I’m curious,” Austij said. 

    “Yeah, split it, man,” Micheal said, and he started his long story. 
*

  FLASHBACK***
*
  Stanley was working at his great grandfather’s farm when he was summoned back home, and was surprised to see his mother in company of the head secretary at his father’s company. 

    “Good afternoon, Mr. Stanley,”

    “What do you want?” 

    “Don’t be rude,” his mother cautioned. 

  He went to take his seat next to his mother, and the secretary started his story of how sick his father was. 

   “What? Liver transplant?” His mother gasped, and Stanley scoffed. 

   “Don’t tell me you are here to ask me to donate my liver to him. I have to run a test first to do that, so if you are here to convince on that, please leave! My mother and I are going nowhere, we have nothing to do with that man,” with that, he stood ip to leave. 

   “The chairman doesn’t wish for that either,” the secretary said, and Stanley looked at him. 

   “The chairman wants you to be healthy while he goes abroad for his surgery, because someone has to look after the company if the surgery goes wrong. This will be the fourth time he gets the transplant, so he is terrified this time,”

   “Fourth time?” Stanley’s mother gasped. 

    “What are you getting at” Stanley blurted. 

   “The chairman’s wish is for you to take over everything. He wants to transfer all his shares to you, so please sign this,” the secretary said, and dropped a document on the table. 

  Stanley’s mother gasped, and looked at Stanley who wasn’t moved. 

   “I have no interest in it.  Even when I was with him, I had no interest in his company. I can’t be stuck up in an office all day, wearing suit. Take that with you and leave, I’ll take care of my future!”

    “Stanley…” his mother called. 

   “That is the chairman only wish. He refused to go for his surgery if you don’t accept this,”

   “I don’t care. I’m off “

   “Stan, wait!”

   “Mum, please don’t try to convince me,”

   “Stanley, he is still your father, and that is your birthright,”

   “He disowned me, mum. No, I denounced him first,” 

   “Think about me, son,”

   “Mum, we are doing just fine,”

   “No, I’m not doing fine. This isn’t the life I want for you. I want to you accept it, it’s not a request,”

  “Mum… why are you doing this to me?”

   “Will you decline?”

   “I just have to sign it, right?” 

  “Yes, sir. But we will have to return to California as soon as possible. There is a general shareholder meeting next week Monday, and the chairman want to introduce you to the board,” 

   “What?”

  “He will go, he will do it,”

  “Mum…”

   “Sign it, son,” 

   “You are not being fair!” He blurted, before singing it. 
*
